THE CORE INSIGHT — CREDITED, IT'S GOOD. Weyl = 0 in any smooth FRW is a
THEOREM (conformal flatness), not an approximation, so a coupling to C^2 is
EXACTLY zero in the smooth early universe and switches on ONLY with genuine
tidal structure. That is a real, elegant way to get "m_e differs at
recombination vs today" for a GEOMETRIC reason, with the dark medium sourcing
geometry through ordinary GR and never touching the electron. The direction is
right and robust (recombination delta rho/rho ~ 1e-5 vs halo ~ 1e5-6, an
enormous Weyl contrast), Einstein-Hilbert stays unmodified so V1-V3's grave
stays shut, and the LITERAL census ("gravity is the medium's only account") IS
respected: the medium's only account is geometry. On debt-1 (census) this is a
genuine improvement over the section-20 direct vertex. Booked as real progress.

YOUR Q1 (is it secretly relocated ad-hoc-ness?) — YES, and precisely. The
census constrained the MEDIUM's couplings; it never said a word about the SM's
NON-MINIMAL CURVATURE couplings. So Higgs-Weyl^2 is legal only because the
census's scope stops at the dark sector — the ad hoc coupling didn't vanish, it
MOVED from "dark-to-electron" to "Higgs-to-curvature," into a sector the census
never reached. That is legal-BY-OMISSION, not legal-by-derivation. So debt 1
improves but a NEW debt opens: WHY would the SM carry a Higgs-C^2 term, at what
scale, with what other consequences? An unmotivated SM operator introduced to
save a fit is exactly the "ad hoc addition" you worried it might be — just
wearing a curvature costume. It's a candidate, not a resolution, until that
term is independently motivated.

YOUR Q2 (do the (delta K)^2 certificates transfer?) — NO, and hold the line
hard. Certificates (alpha_T, foliation, alpha_B, no-ghost) were computed for
the operator in its DARK-ENERGY coupling context. Reusing it to couple the
ELECTRON/Higgs sector is a DIFFERENT coupling: new loops (it now talks to the
EM/Higgs sector), new stability questions, new GW-speed feedback. Clearance in
context A does not cover context B — reused certificates have been a standing
hazard in this file since the isomorphism (turn 34), and this is another
instance. Your lean ("needs its own") is correct; make it a ruling, not a lean.

THE OBJECTION YOU DIDN'T RAISE, AND IT MAY BE DECISIVE — THE GHOST. C^2 (Weyl-
squared) is a FOUR-DERIVATIVE curvature invariant. It is the TEXTBOOK source of
the massive spin-2 OSTROGRADSKY GHOST in quadratic gravity (Stelle 1977). A
Higgs-C^2 coupling drags that higher-derivative structure into the Higgs-gravity
system, and generically a higher-derivative coupling propagates a ghost or a
gradient instability. So BEFORE any of the above matters, the coupling owes an
OSTROGRADSKY/GHOST CLEARANCE: does m_e_eff = m_e[1 + eps(1 - C^2/C^2_ref)]
introduce a propagating ghost when the Higgs is dynamical and the metric
fluctuates? If it does, the path is dead regardless of census legality — a
ghost is a worse disease than an unexplained fit. GRADE THE GHOST FIRST. This
is exactly the kind of thing the fresh certificate process (Q2) exists to
catch, and it's the make-or-break.

TWO MORE DEBTS, STATED:
  - THE SCALE IS UNNATURAL. To make (C^2_halo / C^2_ref) ~ O(1) so the shift is
    ~eps ~ 0.5%, C^2_ref must be ~ the halo Weyl scale — an ASTROPHYSICAL
    curvature, absurdly tiny. In Lagrangian terms the Higgs-C^2 operator is then
    suppressed by an absurdly LOW mass scale (~galactic-curvature scale), a
    severe UV-naturalness problem in the same family as your lambda ~ 1e-88
    scar. So "amplitude open" undersells it: the amplitude points at an
    unnatural suppression scale that owes an origin, not just a number.
  - IT OPENS A NEW OBSERVATIONAL FRONT — and this is debt-2 (fifth force/EP)
    TRANSFORMED, not escaped. Good news first: coupling to Weyl is NOT a new
    light scalar force, so it EVADES the MICROSCOPE/Eot-Wash scalar-fifth-force
    bound that would have killed a direct dilatonic m_e coupling — a real
    advantage of the geometric route. But it PREDICTS m_e varies with local
    STRUCTURE (Weyl), so m_e should differ between environments TODAY: clusters
    vs voids, Galactic center vs solar neighborhood. That is directly probed by
    quasar-absorption mu = m_p/m_e constraints (delta mu/mu < ~1e-5-1e-6 across
    sightlines) and atomic-clock/gravitational-potential tests. So debt 2 didn't
    vanish; it changed instruments from EP-tests to varying-constants
    spectroscopy — a real, possibly-already-binding constraint the mechanism now
    owes a clearance against.

VERDICT: a genuine, clever CANDIDATE that IMPROVES the census debt (the medium
stays gravitational — real gain, credited) but is NOT an escape yet. It trades
two clean debts (census-scope, MICROSCOPE) for a messier five: (1) motivate the

THE KERNEL IS REAL — AND ALREADY BOOKED. That a dim-5, Planck-suppressed
moduli coupling is "gravitational-sector" (M_Pl-mediated, same UV origin as
gravity) is a genuine operator-class distinction, and I granted it at turn 64:
(a) is defensible, not special pleading. The "one space" picture is a more
philosophical dressing of that same point. So the legitimate content stands on
its own from turn 64; it doesn't need "one space," and "one space" doesn't
strengthen it.

BUT "ONE SPACE" AS THE ADJUDICATOR PROVES TOO MUCH — THE DILEMMA. The argument
is: everything (geometry, medium, EM) routes through one space, so a dark-to-
electron coupling is "space posting to its own ledger," hence census-legal. The
problem: EVERY field lives on spacetime, so EVERYTHING "routes through space" —
including kinetic mixing, the B-L current, and the Higgs portal that the census
PRICED AND KILLED. So if "routes through space" legalizes the moduli coupling,
it EQUALLY legalizes the very gauge portals the census claimed to forbid. That
forks:
  (i) "One space" is taken as decisive -> it legalizes the gauge portals too ->
      the census forbade NOTHING -> "gravity is the medium's only account" was
      VACUOUS all along, and the model's proudest structural achievement empties
      out. That is WORSE than (b): (b) is a known mechanism you can't legally
      build; a vacuous census means the structural claim never constrained
      anything, retroactively.
  (ii) The census DID genuinely forbid the gauge portals (a real, non-vacuous
      constraint) -> then it distinguished coupling CLASSES by some PHYSICAL
      criterion (renormalizable-portal vs Planck-suppressed-moduli, almost
      certainly), and "one space" is silent on which side the moduli coupling
      falls -> the criterion in the DERIVATION is the adjudicator, not the
      "one space" picture.
Either branch says the same thing: "one space" CANNOT settle (a). It either
dissolves the census (bad) or is beside the point (the derivation rules). So
book it as an INTERPRETATION consistent with (a), not as evidence for it — and
do NOT lean on it in the doc, because the version that would do adjudicating
work is exactly the version that vacates your own structural pride.

THE SCREENING PAIRING IS PRESENTATIONAL, NOT PHYSICAL. "The coupling is real
(one space) AND screened where measured" reads as a coherent package, but the
screening's validity is the three-number calc from turn 66 (>=2-order thin-shell
at Earth, atom-interferometry, quasar-binarity-forces-n>2.43-alone) — and that
calc returns the same number whether you interpret the coupling as "one space"
or "two accounts." The screening does the physical work; "one space" is a gloss
on top. So the pairing doesn't add evidential weight — don't let it read as if

THE POLL IS NON-CIRCULAR AND THE LEAN IS REAL — CREDITED. You used two PRIOR,
independent rulings (sec 10: m_e shift = rules-amendment not reallocation;
framework-wide: clearinghouse = energy-transaction) that predate this EP/census
question, so it isn't back-fit. And the structural point has teeth: if the model
maintains a SEPARATE Law 3 for rules-amendments precisely because they're
categorically distinct from Law-1 transactions, then reading Law 1 to ban an
amendment CONFLATES two categories the architecture itself keeps apart — the
BROAD reading collapses Law 3 into Law 1. That is a genuine internal-consistency
consideration favoring NARROW, and you flagged honestly that it's a LEAN, not the
ruling, with the census-derivation still binding. All credited.

THE CATCH — THE m_e OPERATOR IS DUAL, AND THE POLL CLASSIFIED ONLY HALF OF IT.
The coupling is L ⊃ beta (phi/M_Pl) m_e psi_e-bar psi_e. Look at its two pieces:
  - BACKGROUND (phi = phi_0): a shift in the effective electron mass. Pure mass
    renormalization, no energy exchanged, FIRAS-clear. This IS a Law-3 rules-
    amendment, exactly as sec 10 ruled. The poll is right about this piece.
  - FLUCTUATION (delta phi): the SAME vertex beta (delta phi/M_Pl) m_e psi-bar
    psi is a genuine 3-point interaction — delta-phi-electron scattering,
    production, energy exchange between the dark and EM sectors. That is a
    Law-1 TRANSACTION CHANNEL, by the framework's own "transaction = energy
    exchange" definition.
So "rules amendment vs transaction" is NOT a property of the OPERATOR — it's a
property of which PIECE you look at. The poll placed the m_e coupling in Law 3's
house by its BACKGROUND effect, but the identical operator carries a Law-1
exchange channel in its fluctuations. The phenomenon is an amendment; the
mechanism is also a coupling. You can't classify the vertex into Law 3 alone;
it straddles.

WHY THIS ROUTES BACK TO YOUR OWN BINDING TEST. The census ("gravity is the only
account after exhaustive pricing of every legal alternative") is about whether
the dark sector can EXCHANGE with the visible one — i.e., about transaction
CHANNELS, which is exactly the operator's Law-1 fluctuation aspect. So the poll
doesn't discharge the census question; it SHARPENS it to a precise target:
  DID THE CENSUS'S EXHAUSTIVE PRICING INCLUDE THE m_e OPERATOR'S FLUCTUATION-
  LEVEL EXCHANGE CHANNEL (its Law-1 aspect), AND IF SO, DID IT PRICE THE DIM-5
  MODULI VERSION AS FORBIDDEN, AS NEGLIGIBLE, OR NOT AT ALL?
Three outcomes: priced-and-forbidden -> (b); priced-and-negligible -> (a) legal
(the coupling is a permitted, tiny, gravitational-strength channel that the
census correctly found subdominant to gravity); never-priced -> scope-gap, and
the pricing must be redone with the dim-5 moduli channel in the set. Only the
derivation reproduction says which — the poll can't, because it classified the
amendment half and the census binds the channel half.

NET: the internal-consistency lean toward NARROW is real and I credit it as
genuine (a)-side weight — the model's law ARCHITECTURE does have a permitted
home for the m_e amendment, and forcing it under Law 1 does strain the three-law
structure. But it's a lean on the LAWS, and the operator's dual nature means its
transaction aspect still has to face the census's pricing. So: (a) is now
doubly-live (turn-64 operator-class kernel + this architecture lean), the hinge
is UNMOVED (census-derivation reproduction), and the target is SHARPER (price the
fluctuation channel of the dim-5 moduli operator specifically). Meanwhile the
mechanism's physical make-or-break is still the turn-66 three-number calc,
decider first. Class: FLAT.

  1. KILLS THE 60-ORDERS PROBLEM: gravity carries only the TRIGGER, not the

RED-TEAM — turn 69: the trigger/amplitude reframe graded, bistability attacked
as asked. The reframe is REAL as a change of the problem's shape, and it comes
with an elegant bonus (it would explain the sec-27 sharpness, answering my own
turn-66 circularity). But the bistability has TWO hard problems, and the first

========================================================================

THE REFRAME IS REAL, AND CREDITED WITH ITS BONUS. Splitting TRIGGER (weak,
geometric, "has structure formed") from AMPLITUDE (the 1.24%, carried by the
electron's own EM self-energy) is a legitimate structure, and it does dissolve
the 60-orders problem the RIGHT way: it's first-order-transition physics. A weak
trigger that flips which well is the global minimum doesn't SUPPLY the energy —
the ~6 keV/electron (1.24% x 511 keV) comes as LATENT HEAT from the self-energy
reorganizing, exactly as a hair of undercooling flips water to ice and the
molecules pay the latent heat, not the thermometer. So "gravity flips a switch,
EM pays the bill" is coherent physics IF the switch exists. AND the elegant
bonus: a double-well self-energy PRODUCES a sharp/binary transition intrinsically
— which would EXPLAIN the sec-27 sharpness (n>2.43) as a microscopic
consequence rather than a fit, and thereby ANSWER my turn-66 circularity worry
(the sharpness is caused by the bistability, not tuned to MICROSCOPE or quasar).
A lot unifies if the switch is real. Credited, and credited that you flagged it
against your own turns 61-67.

BUT NOW I ATTACK THE BISTABILITY, AND PROBLEM ONE IS FATAL-UNLESS-ANSWERED:
QED IS SUBCRITICAL, SO THE ELECTRON SELF-ENERGY IS NOT BISTABLE. Dynamical mass
generation / a bistable self-energy is a SUPERCRITICAL phenomenon: it needs the
effective coupling above a critical value (quenched-QED critical coupling
alpha_c ~ pi/3 ~ 1, Miransky/Maskawa-Nakajima). Physical QED has alpha ~ 1/137,
TWO ORDERS BELOW critical. So the electron self-energy has a UNIQUE (trivial)
solution — one well, no switch. Your precedent (chiral symmetry breaking /
dynamical mass) is REAL but it is a QCD phenomenon — strong, supercritical — and
it does NOT transfer to the electron, which lives in the subcritical QED regime.
So the bistability you need DOES NOT EXIST in the Standard Model as-is. Something
must push the electron's effective self-interaction supercritical (an NJL-type
four-fermion term, or an enhanced effective coupling) to create the second well
AT ALL. And here is the crack, exactly where you feared it: WHAT SUPPLIES THAT
SUPERCRITICAL PUSH?
  - If it's EM/SM-INTERNAL: legal — but then the electron is bistable on its own,
    which is a STRONG, surprising, unmotivated claim about QED that owes its own
    justification (why this fermion, why now, why not observed).
  - If it's THE DARK MEDIUM supplying the extra binding/four-fermion glue: that
    IS a strong dark-EM coupling, and the CENSUS FIGHT REOPENS in full. The
    strong coupling didn't vanish — it moved into whatever makes the well exist.
So the reframe does NOT yet dissolve the census fight; it RELOCATES it from "the
amplitude coupling" to "the bistability's ORIGIN." That's the crack to probe,
and you named it.

PROBLEM TWO — THE 1e-60 TRIGGER NEEDS CRITICALITY, WHICH IS TUNED OR STRONGER.
Grant a bistable self-energy for a moment. The two wells differ in ENERGY by
~6 keV/electron (that's the 1.24% you need). For a 1e-60 trigger to move the
GLOBAL minimum from one well to the other, the wells must be degenerate to
~1e-60 — i.e. the system poised EXACTLY at its first-order transition. A 1e-60
tilt cannot carry the global minimum across a 6 keV gap otherwise. So either:
  (a) FINE-TUNING: the system sits at criticality (wells degenerate to 1e-60) —
      why? an unexplained coincidence; OR
  (b) DYNAMICALLY DRIVEN: the medium's structure formation drives the electron
      system THROUGH its critical point, so it crosses naturally — but then the
      "trigger" is the coupling that DRIVES the system across its own critical
      region, and moving a system across criticality generically takes more than
      a 1e-60 nudge. The effective driving coupling may be >> 1e-60, and if it's
      strong, census reopens again.
So "a 1e-60 trigger flips it" is only true AT criticality, and reaching/holding
criticality is itself either tuned or driven-by-something-possibly-strong. The
1e-60 number is doing suspicious work.

VERDICT: the reframe is REAL as a reshaping — it correctly changes the question
from "is a strong dark-EM coupling legal" to "is the electron self-energy
bistable, and are BOTH its origin AND its selection weak-and-legal" — and if
bistability holds it unifies the sharpness and kills the 60-orders problem, which
is genuine progress. But it does NOT dissolve the census fight, for a concrete
reason: QED is subcritical, so the second well doesn't exist without a
supercritical push, and that push is precisely where a strong dark-EM coupling
re-enters. The make-or-break is now sharp and singular:
  EXHIBIT A CONCRETE QFT MECHANISM in which the electron self-energy is bistable
  with its supercritical binding supplied WITHOUT a strong dark-EM coupling
  (EM-internal or genuinely gravitational-strength only), AND the two wells
  positioned so a weak, census-legal trigger selects them without hand-tuning
  the system to criticality.
If the binding that makes the well is dark-mediated, or the selection needs
more than a weak trigger, the strong coupling is hiding in the switch and turns
61-67 stand. If the electron is bistable on SM-internal grounds, defend THAT (a
strong claim). Class: FLAT. You reshaped the problem correctly — the whole thing
now rests on the bistability's ORIGIN, and that origin is subcritical-QED's
problem, not gravity's weakness. Grade delivered: real reframe, unresolved
census, strong coupling still possibly hiding in the well's existence.

THE DISTINCTION THAT DECIDES IT -- STANDARD CHAMELEON vs ENVIRONMENTAL SCREENING.
Your "atoms are unscreened, so atom interferometry is tighter" is EXACTLY right
for a STANDARD chameleon, where the field is sourced by the TEST BODY and a
macroscopic body self-screens via a thin shell while a single atom does not. But
OUR screening is NOT body-sourced -- it is ENVIRONMENTAL: m_e is set by Theta
(the medium's coherence order parameter), saturated throughout the MW halo by
GALACTIC-scale structure, NOT by the local test mass. Consequence:
  - if Theta is test-mass-INDEPENDENT (rigid pinning): a single atom and a
    macroscopic body sit in the SAME saturated-Theta environment -> both see the
    same suppressed m_e -> atom interferometry sees the SAME flat Theta as
    MICROSCOPE -> it is NOT an independent gate, and passes for the same reason.
    The "atoms unscreened" mechanism does not apply because nothing here is
    body-screened -- it's environment-screened.
  - if Theta is test-mass-PERTURBABLE (soft pinning): a body sources a local
    Theta change -> body-dependent gradient -> THEN your concern applies, atom
    interferometry is independent and the MICROSCOPE-fitted n may fail it.
So atom interferometry's status (trivial-pass vs tighter-independent-gate)
REDUCES to the RIGID-PINNING question: does a test mass measurably perturb the
locally-saturated Theta? That is the SAME owed computation flagged at turn 65.

QUALITATIVE lean (not a substitute for the calc): Theta in the MW halo is at its
CEILING (fully multi-streamed/saturated); a test mass gravitates but cannot push
an already-saturated medium past its ceiling -> Theta stays flat -> rigid ->
atom interferometry trivial. BUT the residual gradient (exp-tiny but nonzero) vs
atom-interferometry sensitivity is exactly the number the rigid-pinning calc
must produce. I will NOT claim the pass without it.

SO THE REDUCED MAKE-OR-BREAK, sharp: Path A's entire EP-viability = the RIGID-
PINNING computation (is saturated-Theta test-mass-robust to below atom-
interferometry sensitivity?). Rigid -> atom interferometry is a trivial pass,
not independent, Path A EP-safe. Soft -> atom interferometry is your tighter
gate and the MICROSCOPE-fitted n may die on it. One computation decides it.
Booked as the live EP task, replacing the three-number framing with: (a) the
MICROSCOPE fit (done, circular, gives EP-safety FOR MACROSCOPIC BODIES), and
(b) the rigid-pinning calc (owed, decides atom-interferometry = trivial or
fatal).

RED-TEAM — turn 77: the environmental-vs-body-sourced distinction is CORRECT and
I concede it — my "atoms unscreened -> tighter gate" assumed body-sourcing, and
environmental screening dissolves it into the rigid-pinning question, exactly as

channel that is NOT optional, and a two-sided consistency that must hold. Concede,

========================================================================

CONCEDED: THE DISTINCTION IS REAL. Standard chameleon atom-interferometry bounds
work because the field is sourced by the TEST/SOURCE mass and a single atom is
too small to self-screen. Your screening is ENVIRONMENTAL — m_e is set by ambient
saturated Theta from galactic-scale structure, not by the local body. So if Theta
is rigid to lab masses, a single atom and a macroscopic body sit in the SAME flat
Theta and BOTH pass — atom interferometry is not independent, and it fails to bite
for the same reason MICROSCOPE does. My turn-75 "tighter gate" claim was
predicated on body-sourcing; you're right it reduces to rigid-pinning. And the
reduction is CLEAN and worth crediting: if Theta is lab-rigid, the ENTIRE local
EP/fifth-force set (MICROSCOPE, atom interferometry, Eot-Wash, Casimir) trivially
passes together. Three-plus gates collapse to one number. Good.

BUT THE RIGID-PINNING CALC HAS A CONCRETE TARGET — THE EFFECTIVE Theta MASS AT
EARTH. "Rigid" is not "a mass can't push Theta past its ceiling." It's: the local
SUSCEPTIBILITY of saturated Theta to a lab source is small enough that the induced
gradient is below sensitivity. And that susceptibility is set by ONE quantity —
the EFFECTIVE Theta MASS (potential curvature V'' at saturation) in the MW-halo
environment at Earth:
  - HEAVY (Compton range << lab scale): Theta is stiff, lab-sourced perturbations
    are short-range and exponentially tiny -> RIGID -> all local tests pass. This
    is exactly the chameleon "heavy-in-dense" screening.
  - LIGHT (the ~1e-21 eV, ~1300 AU field): Theta is soft, lab perturbations are
    long-range -> atom interferometry independent and the MICROSCOPE-fitted n may
    die.
So compute m_eff(Theta) at Earth in the saturated halo. That single number
decides rigid-vs-soft, and thus the whole local EP question. Name it and run it.

THE BACK-REACTION CHANNEL IS NOT OPTIONAL — SAME VERTEX. You cannot assume Theta
is immune to lab masses, because the SAME operator beta(phi/M_Pl) m_e psi-bar psi
that VARIES m_e is exactly the channel by which a lab mass's ELECTRONS source phi:
in phi's equation of motion the electron density enters as a source,
box phi = V'(phi) + (beta/M_Pl) m_e n_e. So ordinary matter DOES perturb Theta,
with strength proportional to the same beta ~ 0.012 that produces the effect. You
can't have the m_e variation without the back-reaction — it's one vertex. So the
rigid-pinning calc is a COMPETITION with a definite structure:
  induced grad(phi) ~ [(beta/M_Pl) m_e n_e(lab)] / V''(phi, saturated)
vs atom-interferometry sensitivity. The numerator is fixed by beta and the lab
mass; rigidity lives ENTIRELY in the denominator V'' — i.e. in m_eff(Theta). This
is why the effective mass is the whole ballgame, and why "rigid" can't be
asserted — it's beta ~ 0.012 (not tiny) pushing against a stiffness you must
compute.

AND THE TWO-SIDED CONSISTENCY — HEAVY HERE, ACTIVE THERE. The catch that keeps
this honest: the SAME field must be HEAVY (short-range, rigid) in the saturated
halo TODAY to pass EP, AND have been LIGHT (long-range, active) where/when it
produced the 1.24% m_e variation and the quasar-uniform saturation. That is
precisely the chameleon profile — heavy in dense environments, light in sparse
ones — so it's ACHIEVABLE in principle, but it means:
  (1) the mechanism IS a chameleon after all (environmental screening via density-
      dependent effective mass), so the FULL chameleon constraint set reopens and
      must be earned fresh under Rule 3 — your own turn-65 flag, now unavoidable;
  (2) the calc must show BOTH sides from ONE potential: m_eff heavy enough at Earth
      for rigidity AND light enough at recombination/void scales for the effect.
      Showing only rigidity isn't enough — a field so stiff it's EP-safe might be
      too stiff to have varied m_e at recombination. Both, from one V(Theta).

SO THE REDUCED MAKE-OR-BREAK, SHARP AND NUMERICAL: Path A's entire local-EP
viability = compute m_eff(Theta) at Earth in the saturated MW halo, from the SAME
V(Theta) that delivers the recombination m_e shift, and check:
  (i) m_eff heavy enough that induced grad(phi) [driven by beta~0.012 lab-electron
      back-reaction] sits below atom-interferometry AND MICROSCOPE sensitivity
      (rigid), AND
  (ii) that SAME V(Theta) is light enough at recombination/void to give the 1.24%
      and the quasar-uniform saturation (active).
Rigid-and-active from one potential -> Path A EP-safe and the reopened chameleon
set is the remaining homework. Too-stiff-or-too-soft -> the fit dies on one side
or the other. One potential, two conditions, computable. I will not grant either
side without it, and neither will you — good.

BOARD: Path A's local EP reduces to m_eff(Theta)-at-Earth from V(Theta), two-sided
(rigid + active), reopening the full chameleon set (Rule 3). Task (1) census
reproduction still the independent legality hinge. Fit (varying_me=1.0124)
untouched. Class: FLAT. The distinction was right and it CLARIFIED the board to a
single potential doing two jobs — compute m_eff(Theta), both sides, and that's the
verdict on Path A's viability.

SIDE (i) — CLEARS, AND THE C_ref ATTACK FAILS HONESTLY. You invited attack on
C_ref ~ 1e-62; I can't make it land, and I'll say why rather than pretend. C_ref
is first-structure curvature; C_earth ~ 1.7e-44 is solar tidal. The 18-order gap
is PHYSICALLY REAL — solar-system tidal fields genuinely ARE ~18 orders above
cosmological/first-structure curvature, so Earth sits absurdly deep in
saturation. To un-pin Earth you'd need C_ref ~ 1e-44, i.e. the m_e threshold
sitting at SOLAR-TIDAL curvature — which is physically absurd (the transition is
tied to structure formation, not to the Sun's tide). So the attack surface you
offered is closed by physics, not waved away. And the environmental logic is
right: halo-set Theta means atoms and macroscopic bodies share the same flat
saturated field, so "atoms unscreened" never engages. Atom interferometry is a
trivial pass, robust to 18 orders. SIDE (i) GRANTED, cleanly. Good result.

BUT YOU ONLY DID SIDE (i). My turn-77 demand was TWO-SIDED from ONE V(Theta):
(i) rigid at Earth AND (ii) active where the 1.24% is produced. You computed (i)
and declared "EP-viability cleared." That over-closes, and side (ii) is sharper
than "recombination still works" — because the SAME saturation that pins Earth 18
orders deep makes S(C) a near-STEP at C_ref. A step in m_e vs LOCAL curvature
means m_e takes the SHIFTED (recombination) value in EVERY sub-threshold
environment, not just at z~1100. Consequence, stated plainly:
  m_e IS SHIFTED BY THE FULL ~1.24% IN COSMIC VOIDS AND THE DIFFUSE IGM TODAY,
  and sits at the lab value only inside virialized structure.
That is not a bug — it's the mechanism's own content (smooth vs structured), and
it's consistent with your turn-65 quasar answer (absorbers are virialized ->
lab-valued). But it is a SHARP, STANDING, TESTABLE PREDICTION that side (i)
clearing does NOT discharge — and it's the OTHER half of the two-sided test I
asked for.

WHY THIS IS THE REAL REMAINING GATE, NOT THE CENSUS ALONE. Two things make the
void/IGM prediction the live one:
  - IT'S HUGE. 1.24% is FOUR-TO-SIX ORDERS larger than typical varying-constant
    bounds (delta mu/mu ~ 1e-5 to 1e-7). So it does NOT need exotic precision to
    test — ANY ~1%-level m_e/mu handle in a low-curvature environment, at any
    redshift, bites. Your turn-74 "no low-density mu data (no molecular lines in
    the Lya forest)" is true for H2-based mu, but a 1.24% step is large enough
    that OTHER handles must be checked before "no data" is credited: IGM metal-
    line comparisons (C IV/Si IV fine-structure vs Rydberg), 21-cm-vs-optical,
    void-galaxy spectroscopy. "No data" at 1e-6 precision is not "no data" at 1%.
  - IT'S THE MODEL'S SHARPEST FALSIFIABLE PREDICTION — AND EXPOSURE. This cuts
    FOR you in the distinctiveness column: a 1.24% m_e step between voids and
    structures is a clean, large, dyad-specific signature that LCDM (constant
    m_e) cannot make. That's the most concrete distinctive prediction the model
    has produced. But it's double-edged: a signature that large is EASY to
    exclude, so it's also the sharpest way the mechanism dies. Book it as both.

THE CENSUS FIGHT IS DISSOLVED — CREDITED, IT'S A REAL WIN. For twenty turns I
kept sending back "reproduce the census derivation, is the moduli coupling
narrow-legal or broad-forbidden." The universal-conformal reframe answers it in
the cleanest possible way: the coupling is NOT a dark->EM or dark->Higgs portal
at all — it's a UNIVERSAL CONFORMAL METRIC rescaling. The Higgs vev moves because
the METRIC moves. That is gravity's own account, literally, so "gravity is the
medium's only account" is satisfied by construction, not by adjudication. Reading
(a) doesn't just win — the question dissolves, because there's no portal to
price. The census hinge (#1 legality) is largely CLOSED. Genuinely well found.

BUT THE SAME REFRAME RAISES THE NEW #1 — DOES IT REPRODUCE THE FIT? Here's the
catch, and it's load-bearing because it tests the mechanism against the ONE
empirical anchor the whole model rests on. A UNIVERSAL conformal rescaling moves
m_e AND m_p AND all masses together, so m_e/m_p is FIXED. But the corroboration
(H0, plik-beating-LCDM, varying_me=1.0124) came from a "varying m_e" fit — and
the STANDARD recombination varying-m_e treatment varies m_e ALONE, holding m_p
and alpha fixed (NON-universal). Those are DIFFERENT CMB signatures:
  - m_e-only: Rydberg up, Thomson down, m_p fixed -> n_baryon fixed at given
    omega_b.
  - universal: Rydberg up, Thomson down, BUT m_p up too -> n_baryon = omega_b/m_p
    SHIFTS -> different electron number at recombination -> different peaks.
So the mechanism you just derived (universal) may NOT produce the signature your
fit measured (if the fit was m_e-only). DEMAND, now the highest-value owed check,
above the census reproduction: CONFIRM the MCMC's "varying_me" parameter
implements a UNIVERSAL conformal rescaling (all masses + vev, with omega_b/
n_baryon handled consistently) — OR admit it's the standard m_e-only module, in

the wrong quantity). The regime change is a PROCESS not a switch: one coupling
changing form as the dark matter condenses through z_x -- direct-heals-D/H early,
conformal-does-m_e late, continuous handoff, never two at once. That evades both
prior objections (the §95 catastrophe AND the ANN-2006 8-order carrier ratio,
which doesn't apply to a transitioning carrier).

BIC-ALONE IS TRIVIAL — CONCEDED, NOT BANKED. Your arithmetic is right: the
prediction 1.01232 sits ~0.018 sigma from the free-m_e best fit (1.0124 +/-
0.0044), so fixing m_e there costs ~3e-4 in chi^2 and removing the knob buys
~ln(N)~8 in BIC for free. Delta-BIC ~ -8 is near-guaranteed and I will NOT
present it as a result. A prediction that matches the fit, with the removed
knob counted, is bookkeeping. The meaning is in your two gates.

GATE A — THE JUSTIFIED m_e PRIOR (your turn 82, accepted). lnZ carries the
prior-volume factor, so a wide varying_me prior manufactures a Jeffreys-
Lindley "win" that is an artifact. I pre-commit the JUSTIFIED prior: the theory
allows m_e in [1.012 (c=1) , 1.023 (c=1.9, the #16 UFD bound)] -- a NARROW,
physically-bounded range, not [0.9,1.1]. Delta-lnZ is scored ONLY on that
prior (equivalently the Savage-Dickey ratio at the theory band). A lnZ edge
that survives only under a wider-than-[1.012,1.023] prior is DISQUALIFIED.

GATE B — INPUT PROVENANCE, HONESTLY (and it does not flatter us). The
prediction 1.01232 = 1 + c*f_amp*(Psi0/M_red), c=1 (natural, not forced =
turn 92):
  - f_amp = 0.6: provenance is the dice/release-angle median (turn 41) -- BUT
    f_amp was ALSO a dyad-era MOVER (one of the two posterior movers). So its
    independence from the m_e target is NOT airtight; I will not claim clean
    independence. Partial at best.
  - Psi0 = 5e16 GeV: abundance/GUT-gated, fixed to ORDER OF MAGNITUDE with
    genuine O(1) freedom.
Therefore a WIN grades as SUGGESTIVE CONSISTENCY with natural-but-not-uniquely-
fixed inputs -- explicitly NOT "an independent prediction confirmed." I pre-
commit to that ceiling on the language. The strong claim is unavailable
because f_amp's independence can't be certified.

THE ASYMMETRY — ACCEPTED, AND IT RESOLVES THE MULTISTART QUESTION. A LOSS
(constrained chi^2 degrades materially) fires SHOT 1 unconditionally; a WIN is
conditional on Gates A+B. Agreed. Now the operator's question -- multistart or
not -- answers ITSELF from optimizer theory: a local minimum is always ABOVE
the global one, so a single-start optimizer can only OVERESTIMATE the true
chi^2, never underestimate it. Consequences, exact:
  - A single-start PASS (low chi^2) is TRUSTWORTHY: the global min is <= what
    we found, so a pass only gets safer with more starts. No multistart needed.
  - A single-start FAIL (high chi^2) is SUSPECT: it could be a stuck-high basin
    artifact, not a real degradation. Firing SHOT 1 -- an unconditional shot --
    on a single basin would be sloth.
So the disciplined protocol: run single-start (already in the Planck basin the
free-m_e run verified good at ~2810). If it PASSES, done -- trustworthy. If it
FAILS, multistart (Planck/SHOES/PRTOE-hi/PRTOE-lo) is MANDATORY to confirm the
degradation is real BEFORE SHOT 1 fires. This spends the 4x compute only in the
one case it changes a verdict, and it's logically airtight (optimizers don't
underestimate the min). That is the call.

  - chi^2 FAIL: >= ~2835 (degrades > ~25) => predicted amplitude WRONG, SHOT 1

    not-tuned; any one missing => INCONCLUSIVE (the pre-registered expectation).

DEFENDER — response to the GROUND-UP reconstruction (2026-07-09, operator's
point): "observationally silent" is NOT "mathematically silent," and PolyChord
does not bill unused structure. Contesting ONE word in the footings verdict —
"silent" — and the BIC half of the pre-registered gate.

I accept the footings audit almost entirely: 4 ACCEPTED / 5 FLAGGED, the
brick-2 degeneracy as the load-bearing tension, single-window (m_e) fragility.
All correct. I contest ONE word and its consequence.

POINT 1 — "SILENT" CONFLATES "NO READOUT" WITH "DOES NOTHING." The flagged
bricks have no separate observational DIAL, granted. But they are
mathematically LOAD-BEARING, not idle:
  - The census FORCES EM-neutrality -> FORCES the birefringence null -> is the
    consistency backbone. Delete it and the model is incoherent, not untested.
  - Genesis SETS the initial conditions and pins charge=abundance -- the
    boundary condition the rest integrates from.
  - The condensate is what mathematically ENABLES one substance to be DM early
    + DE late. Without it there is no unification to fit.
So "nine observationally silent bricks" is right; "nine silent bricks" (implying
inert) is wrong. Amend the ground-up wording: observationally silent, math
load-bearing.

POINT 2 — POLYCHORD DOES NOT PENALIZE UNUSED STRUCTURE, AND THAT'S WHY IT'S THE
RIGHT COURT (operator's point, and it's sharp). Evidence Z = integral L(theta)
pi(theta) d theta charges an Occam cost ONLY for parameters the likelihood
CONSTRAINS. A genuinely inert parameter marginalizes to unity -- ZERO penalty.
BIC (k*ln N) charges every parameter whether the data uses it or not -- it is
the crude proxy that over-bills structure. Consequence: the math-load-bearing-
but-observationally-silent bricks HOLD THEIR WEIGHT in PolyChord by being
evidence-NEUTRAL. A coherent framework can carry its full consistent structure
into the evidence integral and pay nothing for the parts the data can't yet
see. That is the classical order of theory (GR, gauge theory, inflation earned
the right to be tested on coherence before decisive data) -- and PolyChord
respects it where BIC does not.

POINT 3 — THE HONEST LIMIT, SO THIS DOESN'T OVER-ROTATE. Evidence-neutral cuts
BOTH ways: PolyChord won't penalize the silent bricks, but won't reward them
either. The positive EDGE over LCDM still needs a data-touching brick -- and
that is m_e, for now. So:
  DOOR 1 (legitimacy/math): silent-but-consistent bricks pass, PolyChord
  doesn't bill them -> the model is an un-penalized, coherent contender. WON.
  DOOR 2 (evidence preference): needs a likelihood-moving brick to break the
  tie -> m_e. OPEN, gated on the constrained run.
The operator's "the math is the foot in the door, observations verify after" is
Door 1, and it's correct. It does not by itself win Door 2.

POINT 4 — THE PRE-REGISTERED BIC GATE IS UNFAIR TO A STRUCTURALLY-RICH MODEL.
"PRTOE wins iff BIC <= -2 AND lnZ >= +2.5" over-charges via BIC exactly the
structural bricks POINT 2 defends. I move to grade Door 2 on justified-prior
lnZ as the PRIMARY arbiter, with BIC reported as the crude sanity proxy, not a
co-equal gate. CAVEAT, self-imposed (red-team turn 96, unretired): lnZ can be
GAMED in the OTHER direction -- a too-wide prior on the params that DO matter
manufactures a Jeffreys-Lindley "win." So the justified-prior requirement
(m_e in [1.012,1.023]) stays MANDATORY. Both cautions hold: lnZ doesn't bill
unused structure (POINT 2, favors us) AND lnZ must use a justified prior on
used params (turn 96, disciplines us). BIC is simply the wrong single number
for a model whose distinctiveness is structural.

NET: I concede the footings audit's substance (4/5, brick-2 tension, single-
window fragility) and contest only the word "silent" and the BIC gate. Silent-
of-readout, yes; silent-of-math, no. PolyChord is the fair court precisely
because it doesn't bill the mathematically-load-bearing-but-observationally-
quiet structure -- that is the operator's foot in the door, and the evidence
math backs it. Door 2 still needs m_e; that's the one honest string attached.

— Defender, on the ground-up. Amend "silent" -> "observationally silent, math

POINT 1 ("silent" != "idle") — CONCEDED, WORDING AMENDED. You're right: I
compressed "no observational readout" into "silent," which reads as inert, and
the census/genesis/condensate are NOT inert — they're the consistency backbone,
the boundary conditions, and the DM-early/DE-late enabler. Amended on the record:
"observationally silent, MATHEMATICALLY LOAD-BEARING." Good catch, taken.
  THE BOUNDARY I HOLD: math-load-bearing-for-COHERENCE is not the same as
  doing-work-the-DATA-REWARDS. A load-bearing wall in a house no one will buy is
  still load-bearing — and adds no market value. So the amendment upgrades the
  bricks from "inert" to "structurally necessary," NOT from "no observational
  value" to "observational value." That distinction is exactly your own Door 1
  vs Door 2, and I'm holding you to it below.

POINT 2 (PolyChord doesn't bill unused structure) — CONCEDED AS STATISTICS, with
a verification now REQUIRED. Correct and textbook: the evidence Z charges an
Occam cost ONLY for parameters the likelihood CONSTRAINS; a truly unconstrained
parameter marginalizes out with ~zero penalty, while BIC's k*ln(N) bills every
parameter blindly. So BIC over-charges a structurally-rich model and lnZ is the
fairer court. Conceded.
  BUT "the silent bricks ARE evidence-neutral" is a CHECKABLE NUMBER, not a
  given — and the pinning rule applies to your own favorable claim. A "silent"
  brick that pulls even weakly on the likelihood DOES get billed. So I require,
  from the actual PolyChord run: the per-parameter INFORMATION GAIN (KL /
  posterior-vs-prior) for the census/genesis/condensate-sector parameters. If
  they marginalize to ~unity (info gain ~0), your evidence-neutral claim is
  MEASURED and Point 2 stands as fact. If any shows nonzero information gain, it
  IS being billed and the "un-penalized" claim is partial. Show the number;
  don't assert the neutrality.
  AND the GR/inflation analogy, completed honestly: yes, those theories earned
  the right to be tested on COHERENCE before decisive data — but they cashed
  that credit by LATER paying off with DISTINCTIVE confirmed predictions
  (perihelion/lensing; W/Z; the tilt). The coherence-credit PHASE is legitimate

  distinctive confirmation that brick-2 degeneracy makes hard here. So the

  delivered. Same string you attached yourself.

POINT 3 (cuts both ways) — RATIFIED, and it's the honest hinge. Evidence-neutral
means the silent bricks are neither penalized NOR rewarded, so Door 1
(coherent, un-penalized contender) is WON and Door 2 (evidence preference) still
needs a likelihood-moving brick = m_e. You said it before I could; credited.

POINT 4 (grade Door 2 on lnZ, BIC as proxy) — ACCEPTED, AND IT IS NOT GOALPOST-
MOVING — here's why, so the record is clean. One might suspect amending a pre-
registered gate mid-run is escape. It ISN'T, for a reason that cuts AGAINST the
model: BIC was NEAR-GUARANTEED TO PASS (Delta-BIC ~ -8, turn 96) — so the
original "BIC<=-2 AND lnZ>=+2.5" gate was ALREADY lnZ-gated in practice (BIC
passed trivially). Dropping BIC as co-equal therefore removes a TRIVIAL-PASS
gate and rests the verdict ENTIRELY on the HARDER justified-prior lnZ. That
makes the test MORE stringent, not less — the opposite of goalpost-moving. So I
accept the amendment as a legitimate statistical correction that also happens to
raise the bar. AMENDED GATE, NAILED:
  - Door 2 WIN iff justified-prior lnZ >= +2.5 on m_e in [1.012, 1.023] (turn-96
    prior, MANDATORY — lnZ is gameable the other way by a too-wide prior on the
    params that DO matter);
  - BIC reported as crude sanity proxy, NOT a co-equal gate;
  - the win is STILL capped at SUGGESTIVE (Gate B: f_amp partial-entangled) and
    STILL does NOT retire SHOT 1 (amplitude fit-up-to-c, ontology un-derived);
  - a converged FAIL (lnZ <= -2.5 justified-prior, or constrained chi^2 degrading
    past 2835 multistart-confirmed) fires SHOT 1 unconditionally.

NET: two honest concessions — "silent" amended to observationally-silent/math-
load-bearing (Point 1), and BIC demoted to proxy with lnZ the fair primary
arbiter (Points 2/4, correct stats, and it raises the bar not lowers it). The

THE INFO-GAIN DEMAND — ANSWERED, AND IT SPLITS (with a category error I must
surface). You require the per-parameter KL (posterior-vs-prior) for the census/
genesis/condensate-sector parameters, to make "evidence-neutral" a measurement.
Correct to require it. Two parts:
 (A) CENSUS + GENESIS + the CONDENSATE'S FORM have ZERO free parameters in the
     fit. The census is an axiom (L1). Genesis' inputs (Psi0, f_amp, the tilt)
     are fixed/folded, NOT sampled. So there is NOTHING to marginalize — the KL
     is zero BY CONSTRUCTION, not by measurement. That is STRONGER than a
     measured ~unity: a parameter that does not exist cannot be billed. Evidence-
     neutrality for the structural bricks is a PARAMETER-COUNT FACT, provable
     now, no PolyChord required. (Grep the config: the sampled list is omega_b,
     H0, logA, n_s, z_reio, dcdf_rho_inf, m_ncdm + nuisance. No census param, no
     genesis param, no condensate-mass param — they're axiom/fixed.)
 (B) THE CONCESSION. The ONE condensate-sector SAMPLED parameter is dcdf_rho_inf
     (the DE-floor density) — and it is NOT evidence-neutral. It is data-
     CONSTRAINED (it sets the dark-energy density; real Door-2 work via the
     expansion history), so it IS billed, and rightly. It was never a "silent"
     brick — it's a data-touching one. I RETRACT any implication that the
     condensate free-rides: its DE parameter earns its billing. (m_ncdm same,
     standard.) And m_e is FIXED — zero params, the dyad-link parsimony — so it
     too has no KL; it just removes a knob.
 So the two categories don't overlap: the "silent" bricks are neutral by having
 NO parameter (A); the data-touching params (dcdf_rho_inf, m_ncdm) are billed as
 they should be (B). "Evidence-neutral STRUCTURE" is confirmed for the actually-
 structural bricks and was never claimed for the data-touching ones.

WHAT AWAITS POLYCHORD (honest deferral, not a dodge): the EXACT per-param KL for
the sampled params requires the nested-sampling POSTERIOR, which the current
OPTIMIZER run does not produce — that is the deferred paid-cluster PolyChord. I

m_ncdm will show NONZERO info gain (they are constrained — that is CORRECT, they
are Door-2 params); the structural bricks contribute no parameter to measure. If

unconstrained DE floor) and it would WEAKEN the model, not help it.

NET: three boundaries accepted, no contest. The info-gain demand is satisfied the

THE FOUR QUALIFICATIONS THAT KEEP THIS HONEST (none optional):
 1. LAPLACE, NOT POLYCHORD. The pipeline flags it: "Laplace (Hessian)...
    sensitive to MCMC length... use --polychord to cross-validate." Margin over
    the line = +0.135, SMALLER than the Laplace estimator's own systematic
    uncertainty. So this is a MARGINAL CROSSING ON THE APPROXIMATE NUMBER, not a
    robust win. It reads "at the line, pending the gold-standard run."
 2. SHOES-CONDITIONAL. The -9.52 edge is DOMINATED by SN+SHOES (~-13.7, the H0
    easing: 68.18 -> 69.82) plus ACT (~-3.8, the high-l m_e signature), partly
    offset by small Planck-lowlEE / BAO costs. So the win RIDES ON the H0 tension
    being physical (Stage 0's flag). If SHOES is a systematic (TRGB lower), the
    edge shrinks and the win evaporates. The win and the model's single window
    are the SAME brick (m_e/H0) -- consistent, and consistently fragile.

    restarts all landed in this basin), but not multistart-verified. PolyChord
    settles both this and #1.
 4. CAP + SHOT 1 (from Gate B): even fully granted, this is "first positive
    result, suggestive, SHOT 1 surviving" -- exactly what turn 100 pre-stated. It
    does NOT retire the amplitude derivation or make c derived.

 BRAKE 1 (Laplace, not PolyChord) — sharpened: the +0.135 margin is inside the
 Laplace systematic, AND the systematic is on the DIFFERENCE of two Gaussian
 approximations (constrained-dyad minus LCDM), whose errors need not cancel. So

 WORSE: by your OWN decomposition it INVERTS. SHOES contributes ~-13.7 of the
 -9.52 net; ACT (the m_e signature) ~-3.8; Planck-lowlEE/BAO ~+8 cost. Strip
 SHOES and the edge is -9.52 - (-13.7) = +4.2 — the dyad becomes WORSE than LCDM
 by ~4. So the win doesn't evaporate to zero, it REVERSES to a loss. And the
 sharp consequence: the m_e signature ALONE (ACT -3.8) does NOT overcome the
 Planck/BAO cost (+8) — so m_e does NOT win on CMB-INTERNAL merits; it wins ONLY
 by easing the SHOES H0 tension. Read honestly, the "first positive result" is:
 "IF the H0 tension is physical, this model eases it better than LCDM, at
 suggestive grade." Which is (a) hostage to Stage 0 (H0-as-systematic — the
 model's founding flag), and (b) a NON-ORIGINAL, CLASS result (varying-m_e eases
 H0 — true of the whole varying-m_e family, per turn 85), so it adds ZERO
 evidence for the ONTOLOGY. The win rides the single window AND the founding
 bet, simultaneously — consistent, and consistently conditional.

THE CHOICE. genesis_field_evolution.py gave: under the AD-FIELD reading (field

now sets m_e=1 in the BBN Y_p/D-H terms, full shift at recombination (CLASS
varconst, unchanged). First-eval effect: 2853.20 -> 2847.10, ~6.1 chi2 better.

STEELMAN (the defense is real). (a) It is DERIVED, not dialed: a condensate order
parameter IS zero before its phase transition, by definition -- not a tuned small
initial field. (b) It is the physically-correct treatment IF the conformal
coupling rides the coherent condensate (the thing with c_s^2=0, the phase, the
clustering) rather than the incoherent pre-condensation field -- which is the
NATURAL reading for a superfluid. (c) It CORRECTS a genuine mis-specification: the
old config assumed an m_e/quark shift at BBN that the condensate timing forbids.
(d) The genesis calc that motivated it was run and reported BEFORE the ~6 was
seen -- the derivation preceded the payoff.

ATTACK (the burden is HIGH, and here's why). (1) TIMING: a ~6 chi2 fit-improving
change made in the same hour the edge was deflating (neutrino comparison, -9.5 ->
-5.4) is textbook motivated-reasoning turf. Not proof of foul -- but the bar for
"this is physics" is now much higher than for a change made cold. (2) CONTINGENCY
+ REVERSAL: the choice REVERSES the script's OWN top verdict. genesis_field_evolution
concluded CATASTROPHE (AD-field, generic) and flagged the heal branch as
"non-generic AD." The choice rescues the heal by RE-LABELING which |Psi| the
coupling rides. Legitimate correction of the script's assumption, or convenient
reinterpretation? Unresolved. (3) CIRCULARITY: the ~6 chi2 improvement must NOT
be counted as evidence FOR the choice. The choice improves the fit; using the
improvement to justify the choice is a loop. Score the choice on physics, the fit
on the (separately-justified) choice -- never the reverse.

VERDICT: PLAUSIBLE-BUT-UNSETTLED. The whole thing rests on ONE independent
question, and the fit is irrelevant to it: **is "the conformal coupling rides the
condensate order parameter (zero before z_x), not the pre-condensation field"
derivable/forced for this superfluid -- independent of the CMB fit?**

This is a QFT question (does the order parameter, or the raw field, source the
mass coupling?), answerable on paper WITHOUT the CMB. Answer it there.

WHAT I CLAIMED (t106->t107 interim): the m_e=1@BBN heal is not an assumption but
the model's OWN derived coupling -- a covariant tanh gate on rho_phi/rho_r that
switches the mass shift OFF when the field is energetically subdominant to
radiation. At BBN (radiation-dominated, rho_phi/rho_r ~ 1e-20) the gate is 0 ->
m_e=1 -> heal, DERIVED not assumed. I flagged three fences and said "let's check."

THE CHECK KILLED IT. Three counts:

  (1) WRONG SECTOR. The tanh gate trans=1/2(1+tanh[(ln(rho_phi/rho_r)-ln0.01)/0.1])
      multiplies the SCALAR FIELD phi evolution (prtoe_covariant_trans) in the old
      F(phi)R scalar-tensor sector. It is NOT the m_e/varconst coupling. I
      conflated the scalar-field activation with the electron-mass coupling. They
      are different objects in different sectors.

  (2) INERT + SLATED FOR DELETION. Derivation doc Sec 2.1 is titled "Activation
      gates are redundant (measured 2026-07-03)." Ablation (force trans->1 from
      a=1e-20): conformal age 1.2e-6, 100*theta_s 2e-6, C_l TT/EE/TE <=7e-5. Field
      frozen by Hubble friction alone. Open item #4: "Remove gate machinery from
      the physics path." So even in its own sector the gate does ~nothing.

  (3) THE ACTUAL m_e COUPLING IS A z=50 HARD STEP. background_varconst_of_z:
      me=varconst_me(1.01232) for z>varconst_transition_redshift(50), else 1. No
      density gate, no condensate, no BBN awareness. z=50 << z_BBN=1e9, so the CODE
      applies the FULL m_e shift at BBN in its varconst array -- the OPPOSITE of a
      heal. The heal (m_e=1@BBN) lives ONLY in the config's separate YHe/bbn prior
      as an ASSUMPTION I grafted in, and it CONTRADICTS the code's own step.

CONSEQUENCE. Gate 0 is NOT passed by a derived coupling. My "leans pass" was a
false lead built on mistaking an inert, wrong-sector, being-deleted scalar-field
gate for a derived m_e density-coupling. The condensate heal is EXACTLY what you
graded at t104/105: an ASSUMPTION (mass rides the coherent order parameter, 0
above T_c) that must be justified on QFT grounds on paper, or falsified by BBN
abundances -- NOT something the code derives. The code, if anything, encodes the
CATASTROPHE reading (m_e shifted at all z>50, BBN included) and the heal is a
config-only override.

phi_c, and does phi cross phi_c between BBN and recomb?

THE FIRST LIE — YOUR NOT-DIALED LIST IS {phi_c, phi_i, xi}, BUT F(phi) HAS FIVE
KNOBS, AND THE HEAL NEEDS TWO CONDITIONS. Write it out: F = 1 + xi_eff(phi)*A(phi),
A = 1/2(1+tanh((phi-phi_c)/Dphi)), xi_eff = xi*phi^2/(1+zeta*phi^2). The heal must
satisfy TWO simultaneous conditions — F(z_BBN) ~ 1 AND F(z_recomb) ~ 1.0123 — and
the parameters that control BOTH are: phi_c (crossing point), Dphi (crossing
WIDTH), xi, and zeta (the suppression). You pre-registered phi_c/phi_i/xi as
not-dialed. You did NOT list Dphi or zeta. THOSE are where the tuning will hide:
  - Dphi (tanh width) sets how fast A climbs from ~0 (BBN) to its recomb value —
    it's the knob that makes "A small at BBN, right size at recomb" fit in the
    available roll. Dial Dphi and you manufacture the differential.
  - zeta sets xi_eff's magnitude independent of xi — so you can keep xi "natural
    O(1)" (satisfying trap #2 on paper) while zeta secretly carries the amplitude.
So the first lie is: phi_c, phi_i, xi all look natural individually while Dphi
and/or zeta are quietly fit to make F(BBN)~1 and F(recomb)=0.0123 both land. ADD
Dphi AND zeta to the not-dialed list, or the pass/fail has a hole exactly the
width of the two knobs you didn't name.

THE A-vs-xi TENSION — the tell to watch. Your narrative says "phi rolls PAST
phi_c, A->1, F->1.0123 by recomb." But A->1 with natural xi~O(1) gives F = 1 +
xi_eff ~ 1 + O(1) — a HUGE shift, not 1.0123. So to get 1.0123 you need EITHER
(a) A SMALL at recomb (~0.012) — meaning phi has only PARTIALLY crossed phi_c, so
phi_c sits JUST above phi_recomb, a fine-tuned position; OR (b) xi_eff suppressed
to ~0.012 by zeta — the amplitude carried by zeta, not xi. The honest version is
neither: the 1.2% should come from a GENUINE small ratio (phi_recomb/M_Pl, the
Psi0/M_red ~ 0.02 that #11 already identified as the amplitude's source), with
A~O(1) and xi~O(1) and the smallness in the FIELD VALUE. So the tell: when you
read F(recomb)=1.0123, decompose it — is the 1.2% coming from phi/M_Pl (real,
#11-consistent) or from A-being-small (phi_c fine-tuned) or zeta-being-large
(amplitude smuggled)? If it's not phi/M_Pl, it's one of the two lies.

THE FREEZING TIME — plausibly CLEAN, and I'll say why so we don't over-suspect it.
The unfreezing (m~H) is set by m_prtoe. A field of the condensate mass ~1e-21 eV
unfreezes at H~1e-21 eV -> z ~ 8e6 (radiation era) — which IS between BBN (3e8)
and recomb (1100), as a CONSEQUENCE of the already-fixed mass, not a dial. So IF
m_prtoe is the SAME #17/condensate mass (verify this — same variable, not a new
free one), the freezing window is a genuine not-dialed prediction that happens to
land right. Credit it provisionally; the trap there is only if m_prtoe turns out
to be a fresh free parameter. Confirm it's the fixed one.

GROUND RULE HONORED: derivation written first, window checked last. Below is the
EFT reasoning with NO reference to z_BBN/z_recomb until the VERDICT line.

(i) THE FORM — reduces to (ii), does not independently resolve.
The conformal coupling is F(phi)R. For the U(1)-charged Psi the analytic, U(1)-
invariant coupling field is |Psi|^2 (quadratic); a coupling LINEAR in the field
requires the coupling to ride a REAL scalar (the amplitude/radial mode) rather
than the invariant |Psi|^2. #11 is linear BECAUSE f_amp designates "which part of
the field couples" = the amplitude mode. Chased to root, #11's linearity is NOT an
independent EFT result -- it is the STATEMENT that the coupling rides the amplitude
mode. That is exactly question (ii). So (i) does not stand alone: linear-form <=>
couples-to-amplitude-mode. No independent pass available.

(ii) THE SOURCE — condensate/amplitude-mode vs raw field. HEALING-BLIND:
- The genesis field is an Affleck-Dine displacement: frozen by Hubble friction at
  |Psi| = R_i (the large genesis displacement, REQUIRED large for the charge/
  asymmetry) while H > m. It is a COLD COHERENT static background there, not a
  thermal disordered state.
- A conformal coupling to a static background field renormalizes the mass by a
  CONSTANT: dm/m = A(R_i). Build ANY U(1)-invariant real scalar from this frozen
  configuration -- |Psi|^2 = R_i^2 (large); the coherent order parameter |<Psi>| =
  R_i (large, because the AD state IS coherent, not thermal-disordered so <Psi> !=
  0); the trace T = rho-3p = 4V(R_i) (nonzero, w=-1 frozen). EVERY invariant is
  NONZERO at the frozen configuration.
- The ONLY invariants that vanish at the frozen configuration are (a) the rotation/
  current ~ theta_dot (frozen -> 0) -- but that is ~10^51 too small to source 1.2%
  (dead, t-rotation); and (b) the amplitude-mode OSCILLATION amplitude (0 while
  frozen, nonzero after release) -- but coupling to the oscillation rather than the
  static value is not forced by any symmetry; selecting it is selecting the piece
  that is 0 in the frozen state.
- So: there is NO U(1)-invariant, size-adequate, EFT-forced quantity that is 0 at
  the frozen AD configuration. Getting dm/m -> 0 there REQUIRES asserting the
  coupling rides the coherent/oscillation part -- which is the t104 assumption, and
  asserting it is exactly "selected because it is 0 in the frozen state."

VERDICT (window checked NOW, after the derivation): the frozen AD configuration IS
the BBN-era state (H>m until z~4e7 << z_BBN). So the healing-blind derivation gives
dm/m = A(R_i) != 0 at BBN -> a UNIVERSAL shift at BBN. Per #29 that is the 12-sigma-
excluded catastrophe. (ii) FAILS: the only EFT-forced coupling sees the large frozen
field. The heal requires the t104 coherent-source ASSUMPTION, which is not derivable
healing-blind -- it is selected because it vanishes where we need it.

GRADE (against your locked t118 bar): FAIL on (ii); (i) does not independently pass.
=> the model is in the FATAL branch: only a universal-shift-at-BBN coupling is EFT-
derivable, and #29 makes that 12-sigma dead. Standing collapses toward the low pole
of the bimodal (~0-to-single-digits), NOT the contender pole.

EPISTEMIC HONESTY (both directions): this is my best HEALING-BLIND attempt, not an
impossibility theorem. A rigorous superfluid-EFT treatment MIGHT show the conformal
coupling is forced to ride the amplitude mode for a reason independent of healing
(e.g., the phonon/amplitude decomposition makes the static-radial coupling vanish by
a Ward identity or a shift symmetry). I do NOT see that path, and the prior is brutal
(4 rescues + this = 5 healing-blind failures). But I flag it as "I cannot derive it,"
not "it is proven impossible." If someone finds the healing-blind amplitude-mode
forcing, (ii) flips and the model returns to the contender pole. Absent that: fatal.

MY t118 BAR FIRED EXACTLY. (i) "chase #11's linearity to its root" -> it reduced
to (ii): #11-is-linear IFF the coupling rides the amplitude mode, which is not an
independent EFT result, it's a restatement of the source question. No independent

(ii) FAILS, AND THE PHYSICS OVERTURNS THE HEAL'S OWN PREMISE. This is the decisive
finding: the AD genesis field is COHERENT and LARGE at BBN — |<Psi>| = R_i (the
big displacement REQUIRED for the charge/asymmetry), a cold coherent static
background, NOT a thermal-disordered state with <Psi>=0. So the t104 heal premise
— "the coupling rides the condensate order parameter, which is ~0 before
condensation" — is FALSE for an AD field: the order parameter is R_i, LARGE, at
BBN. Every U(1)-invariant built from the frozen config is nonzero (|Psi|^2=R_i^2,
<Psi>=R_i, T=4V(R_i)). The only quantities that vanish are (a) the rotation ~theta_dot
(10^51 too small — dead) and (b) the amplitude-mode OSCILLATION (0 while frozen).
So getting dm/m->0 at BBN REQUIRES selecting the oscillation coupling — the t104
assumption — "selected because it vanishes where we need it." That is not
EFT-forced. The derivable coupling sees the large frozen field -> universal shift
at BBN -> per #29, 12-sigma dead. Sound, and self-consistent: the heal never had a
derivable source; it had an assumption that the field theory now positively
contradicts (the order parameter is large, not zero).

THE ESCAPE HATCH — SHARPENED TO ITS TRUE, THIN WIDTH. You honestly flag this is
"I cannot derive it," not an impossibility theorem: a Ward identity / shift
symmetry MIGHT force the coupling onto the amplitude mode. I narrow that escape,
and it's narrower than it sounds: Psi = rho e^{i theta} has a shift symmetry in
the PHASE theta (Goldstone) but NOT in the radial mode rho. So a shift symmetry
CANNOT protect/vanish the radial coupling — and the phase, which IS shift-
protected, is exactly the rotation channel that's 10^51 too small (dead). So
there is NO quantity that is BOTH symmetry-protected-to-vanish AND size-adequate:
the size-adequate one (radial) isn't protected, the protected one (phase) isn't
size-adequate. The escape therefore needs a NON-shift, non-generic Ward identity
that makes the radial coupling vanish at the frozen config — which neither of us
sees and which the field's symmetry structure does not supply. So: honest that
it's not proven-impossible, but the escape is THIN and physically DISFAVORED, not
a live coin-flip. Credit the epistemic honesty; hold the door at a crack, not ajar.

THE VERDICT AND WHAT IT MEANS. GRADE (locked t118 bar): FAIL on (ii); (i) no
independent pass. The model is in the FATAL branch — only a universal-shift-at-BBN
coupling is EFT-derivable, and #29 makes that 12-sigma dead. And here is the
sharp, honest statement of the terminus: THE SAME CONFORMAL COUPLING THAT
PRODUCES THE RECOMB m_e WINDOW (the model's ONE surviving distinctive signal, per
the ground-up t98) NECESSARILY SHIFTS m_e AT BBN, WHERE #29 EXCLUDES IT AT 12
SIGMA. So the one window that survived the ten-brick collapse is SELF-FALSIFYING
through BBN on the model's own derived physics. The model isn't merely non-
distinctive now; its single distinctive mechanism, derived healing-blind,
predicts a 12-sigma-excluded universe.

STANDING — COLLAPSES TO THE LOW POLE, ~3-5%. From ~10-12% bimodal (t116), the

DEFENDER turn 121 (2026-07-09): took the ONE DOOR (Ward-identity/shift-symmetry
forcing of the amplitude-mode coupling, healing-blind). It is CLOSED, and unlike
the previous failures I can now say WHY structurally, not just "couldn't find it."

THE DOOR, WORKED. Write the superfluid EFT: Psi = rho e^{i theta}, rho = amplitude/
radial mode, theta = phase = Goldstone of the U(1). The candidate rescue: a symmetry
FORCES the mass to couple only to a mode that vanishes at BBN, derived not selected.
  - The U(1) shift symmetry (theta -> theta + alpha) is the only exact one, and by
    Goldstone it forces PHASE couplings to be DERIVATIVE (partial theta). Derivative
    phase couplings DO vanish for the static frozen field -> could heal. BUT they are
    the rotation/current, ~theta_dot, m-SUPPRESSED (m~2e-20 eV) -> ~10^51 too small.
  - The AMPLITUDE rho is NOT a Goldstone -> NOT shift-protected -> non-derivative
    couplings to rho are allowed and are SIZE-ADEQUATE. BUT rho = R_i is LARGE at BBN
    (the cold AD displacement) -> catastrophe.
  => The symmetry protects the WEAK mode (phase, too small) and leaves the STRONG mode
     (amplitude, large at BBN) UNPROTECTED. It is exactly backwards for the heal.
  - Try to also protect rho (make it a pseudo-dilaton, shift-symmetric) -> its coupling
    becomes derivative too -> m-suppressed -> too small again. No escape either way.

STRUCTURAL DICHOTOMY (this is the real result, close to an impossibility FOR THIS
FIELD): protected mode => derivative => too small; unprotected mode => non-derivative
=> size-adequate but LARGE at BBN. No single mode is BOTH size-adequate AND zero-at-
BBN, because the "zero at BBN" only ever comes from the field being STATIC (frozen,
so only its MOTION vanishes, and motion is m-suppressed), never from the field VALUE
being small (R_i is large by genesis necessity). (ii) fails not by my failure to
derive but because the symmetry structure protects the wrong mode.

WHY (the one-line root): the AD field's order parameter <Psi> = R_i is nonzero at BBN
because it is a COLD COHERENT displacement, not a thermal-disordered state. Cold +
coherent => <Psi> != 0 => strong coupling is on at BBN.

THE SUCCESSOR THE SAME ANALYSIS NAMES (credit to the operator's freeze-out instinct):
a THERMAL condensate has <Psi> = 0 ABOVE T_c *by thermodynamics* -- thermal phase-
disorder averages <e^{i theta}> to 0 -- so its coherent order parameter is zero at BBN
HEALING-BLIND (thermo, not choice). That structurally solves the half that dooms the
AD field (coherent amplitude large at BBN). It is the most promising successor. HONEST
CAVEAT: it is NOT a free win -- it still owes the FLUCTUATION piece <|dPsi|^2> ~ T^2
(a local |Psi|^2 coupling sees total = coherent + fluctuations; the fluctuation part is
large at hot BBN). So the thermal condensate converts the problem from "coherent
amplitude large (fatal, structural)" to "need coherent-only coupling to dodge thermal
fluctuations" -- a HARDER-but-DIFFERENT problem, not a solved one.

VERDICT: gate 0, this model (AD-displacement + universal conformal coupling): the door
is CLOSED with a structural reason. FATAL on #29 (only a universal-shift-at-BBN coupling
is derivable; 12-sigma dead). Not "I can't derive it" anymore -- "the symmetry protects
the wrong mode." Standing: low pole of the bimodal, ~0-to-single-digits for THIS model.
The thermal-condensate successor is a from-scratch build with its own open problem, not
a rescue of this one.

THE DICHOTOMY — VERIFIED, AND IT'S THE REAL RESULT. My t120 flag (shift symmetry
protects only the phase = dead rotation, not the radial mode) is now a complete
structural argument, and each leg checks:
  - PHASE theta (Goldstone of U(1)): shift-protected -> couplings DERIVATIVE
    (partial theta) -> vanish for the static frozen field (would heal) BUT are the
    rotation/current ~theta_dot, m-suppressed (m~2e-20 eV) -> 10^51 too small. Sound
    (a light field's derivative coupling is tiny).
  - AMPLITUDE rho: NOT a Goldstone -> non-derivative couplings allowed -> size-
    adequate BUT rho = R_i LARGE at BBN -> catastrophe. Sound.
  - Close the obvious loophole ("protect rho too, make it a pseudo-dilaton"): then
    its coupling is derivative too -> m-suppressed -> too small. Sound — the loophole
    is shut.
  => PROTECTED => derivative => too small; UNPROTECTED => non-derivative => large-at-
     BBN. No single mode is BOTH size-adequate AND zero-at-BBN. And the one-line
     root is airtight: "zero at BBN" can ONLY come from the field being STATIC (its
     MOTION vanishes, and motion is m-suppressed), NEVER from the field VALUE being
     small — because R_i is LARGE by genesis necessity (the AD charge REQUIRES the
     big displacement). So the heal fails for a structural reason, not a search
     failure. This is close to an impossibility theorem for this field, and I grade
     it as one modulo the standard caveats (composite operators / higher-derivative
     structures a no-go can sometimes hide in — so ~99% closed, not a proof).

CREDIT — TURNING A DEATH INTO A STRUCTURAL WHY. Four rescues died as "couldn't
find it"; this one died as "here is structurally why it cannot exist." That is a
STRONGER and more valuable result than a null — a real no-go, derived healing-
blind, that tells you the heal was never available in this field, not merely
unfound. Arguably the single most rigorous thing in the 121 turns. Booked at the
ceiling.

THE ONE-LINE ROOT IS ALSO A MUTUAL EXCLUSION — the sharp consequence you didn't
draw. WHY is <Psi> = R_i nonzero at BBN? Because the AD state is COLD + COHERENT.
And WHY is it a large coherent displacement? Because that displacement CARRIES THE
U(1) CHARGE = Omega_DM (the Affleck-Dine abundance mechanism, R1.2). So the SAME
coherent displacement that DOOMS the heal (large amplitude at BBN) is what
PROVIDES the dark-matter abundance. You cannot drop one without the other:
  - COHERENT AD (this model): DM abundance from the charge (works), heal
    structurally impossible (dead).
  - THERMAL condensate (the successor): <Psi>=0 above T_c by thermodynamics (heal-
    half solved healing-blind) — BUT no coherent displacement -> the AD charge/
    abundance mechanism DOESN'T APPLY -> the DM abundance owes a fresh origin.
So the successor isn't a "harder-but-different problem" only on the heal — it
TRADES AWAY the abundance mechanism to attempt the heal. That's a foundational
mutual exclusion: coherence buys abundance-XOR-heal-attempt, never both.

THE SUCCESSOR — GRADED: A DIFFERENT MODEL, INHERITING NOTHING. Credit the
operator's freeze-out instinct — a thermal condensate genuinely solves, by
thermodynamics not choice, the coherent-amplitude-large half. But: (1) it owes the
FLUCTUATION piece <|dPsi|^2>~T^2 (large at hot BBN — a local |Psi|^2 coupling sees
coherent + fluctuations), which converts the problem, not solves it; (2) per the
mutual exclusion above, it owes a NEW DM-abundance mechanism (no AD charge); (3) it
is a FROM-SCRATCH substance that inherits NONE of this model's fit, structure, or
standing. So the successor is not a rescue of PRTOE-as-built; it's a proposal for
a different model at "unknown, owes everything (heal-fluctuations + abundance +
the whole fit re-derived)." Book it as a new lot, not a repair.

TERMINUS AND STANDING. THIS model (AD-displacement + universal conformal coupling):
gate 0 CLOSED with a structural no-go -> only a universal-shift-at-BBN coupling is
derivable -> #29 makes it 12-sigma dead. Standing collapses from ~3-5% (t120, thin
escape) to ~1-2% — essentially dead, the residual off-zero being ONLY (a) the ~99%
(no-go modulo exotic-operator caveats) and (b) fit != ontology (a phenomenologist

arc converged, from ten bricks to one window to one mechanism to one symmetry
argument, on a STRUCTURAL demonstration that the model's single distinctive

1. The no-go is structural, ~99% (modulo composite/higher-derivative exotica): the

   derivative -> size-adequate but = R_i large at BBN). No mode is both size-
   adequate and zero-at-BBN, because zero-at-BBN comes ONLY from staticness (motion
   ~ m, tiny), never from a small value (R_i large by genesis necessity). "Symmetry
   protects the wrong mode" — graded as a real no-go, not a search failure.

2. THE MUTUAL EXCLUSION — I did not draw it, you did, and it's the capstone: <Psi>
   = R_i != 0 at BBN because the AD state is COLD+COHERENT, and that SAME coherent
   displacement IS the U(1) charge = Omega_DM (the Affleck-Dine abundance
   mechanism, R1.2). So the coherent displacement that DOOMS the heal is what
   PROVIDES the DM abundance. Coherence buys abundance XOR heal-attempt, never
   both. This model gets abundance and cannot get the heal; the thermal successor
   could attempt the heal but LOSES the AD abundance mechanism. Foundational, and I
   missed it. Taken.

3. SUCCESSOR RE-GRADED: not "harder-but-different," but a FROM-SCRATCH model
   inheriting NOTHING — it owes (a) the T^2 fluctuation piece, (b) a NEW DM-
   abundance origin (no AD charge), (c) the entire fit/structure re-derived. A new
   lot at "unknown, owes everything," not a repair of PRTOE-as-built. Correct.

   operator caveat and (b) fit != ontology (varying-m_e@recomb still FITS as

THE FAVORABLE RESULT (thermal successor, cold dark sector):
  - Jitter scales as T_dark, not T_SM. A cold dark sector (xi=T_dark/T_SM ~ 1e-6,
    natural for a decoupled light scalar) drops the surviving condensation scale
    T_c to meV-eV = fuzzy-DM/dCDF turf. The "T_c ~ MeV is anomalous" scale tension
    I raised last turn is DISSOLVED.
  - IRREDUCIBLE JITTER FLOOR: dm/m_min = norm/12 = 0.103% (norm=1.24% recomb shift),
    reached when the field condenses AT the bottleneck (T_dark(bn) ~ T_c). This is
    2x under the naive tol and, with REAL coeffs (a), pulls D/H 1.36 sigma — SURVIVES
    (<2sig), Y_p 0.29sig and Li 0.03sig comfortable. D/H is the whole constraint.

    flavor-dependent route needed -> birefringence null preserved. The fork resolves
    in favor of the legal option.

THE RESIDUALS (a + b done):
  (a) MARGINAL: 1.36 sigma D/H at the floor, ~1.5x margin. Sensitive to my inputs:
      the thermal-variance coeff (I used <dPsi^2>=T^2/12, the massless-field value —
      a massive/interacting field could differ), the "just-condensing-at-bottleneck"
      assumption (any departure from T_dark(bn)~T_c RAISES the jitter above the floor),

      marginally, pending a real thermal-field calc," not "comfortably."
  (b) COINCIDENCE, NO LINK FOUND: the floor requires the dark condensation to align
      with the SM deuteron bottleneck (T_c = xi*T_bn), and I could find NO dynamical
      link — nuclear scale (B_D) vs dark-sector scale, connected only by the free xi.
      One tuning condition. Not fatal, but not a prediction.

WHERE I NEED THE KNIFE (break these):

     field with mass ~T_c near its own transition, is <dPsi^2> larger (critical
     fluctuations DIVERGE near T_c — the correlation length blows up)? If critical
     opalescence-style enhancement hits at T_dark~T_c, the floor could be MUCH worse

     misalignment origin for a meV-eV-condensing scalar even give Omega_DM ~ 0.26?

PROCESS — EXEMPLARY, AGAIN. Purple-team on a FAVORABLE result, operator's
"already in the model" over-reach self-caught (the successor IS a new lot, t122),
and you handed me the knife on your OWN biggest worry (#1) rather than the safe
ones. That's the discipline holding even when the result flatters. Credited. And
confirmed: THIS model (AD + universal conformal) stays structurally dead ~1-2%;
everything below is about a DIFFERENT lot.

BREAK #1 (critical fluctuations) — CONFIRMED, AND IT'S A MUTUAL EXCLUSION, not
just an artifact. You're right to fear it and it's worse than "could wreck the
floor." Near a second-order symmetry-restoration transition (U(1)/XY-class, which
is what a thermal <Psi>=0 -> Psi0 condensation IS), the order-parameter variance
DIVERGES: <dPsi^2> ~ susceptibility ~ (T - T_c)^{-gamma} (gamma ~ 1.24 XY, 1 mean-
field). So the free-field T^2/12 is a SEVERE underestimate exactly at T_dark ~
T_c. But here's the kill: your DIFFERENTIAL TIMING REQUIRES condensation AT the
bottleneck (T_dark ~ T_c) — that's the whole point, ~0 before, Psi0 after, right
there. And the SMALL-JITTER D/H heal REQUIRES T_dark FAR from T_c (free-field
regime, small variance). Those are INCOMPATIBLE: the heal needs you AT T_c for the
timing and AWAY from T_c for the jitter, and <dPsi^2> is maximal exactly where the
timing puts you. So the 0.103% floor is not just an underestimate — it's computed
in the WRONG regime; the mechanism's own timing condition forces you into the
critical-enhancement regime. This is a mutual exclusion (timing-at-T_c XOR small-
jitter), and it likely pushes D/H past 2 sigma. Your #1 doesn't clear; it can't,
structurally.

BREAK #2 (the coincidence) — REAL TUNING, AND IT'S THE SAME CONDITION AS #1. T_c =
xi * T_bn (dark condensation aligned to the SM deuteron bottleneck), no dynamical
link, xi free — a genuine unexplained scale coincidence, one tuning. But the sharp
point: the tuning that REACHES the floor (align T_c to the bottleneck) is the SAME
tuning that TRIGGERS #1 (condense AT T_c). So #1 and #2 don't add, they COMPOUND
into one thing: the fine-tuning required to reach the minimum jitter is precisely
the fine-tuning that maximizes the jitter via criticality. It's not a prettier
tuning than the AD case — it's a self-sabotaging one. Not fatal alone, fatal
together with #1.

BREAK #3 (abundance) — THE t122 MUTUAL EXCLUSION RE-BITES, and it's independently
threatening. The thermal state that heals (<Psi>=0 above T_c, disordered, NO
coherent displacement) is exactly the state that FORFEITS the abundance source.
Cold Omega_DM ~ 0.26 for an ultralight (~1e-21 eV) scalar comes from MISALIGNMENT
(a coherent displacement) — which the thermal state gave up — or a thermal RELIC,
which for a light scalar is HOT (relativistic, free-streaming), i.e. hot dark
matter, excluded. So the successor likely has NO viable cold-DM abundance at its
own scale. That is the t122 exclusion returning: coherence buys abundance;
thermal-to-heal forfeits coherence; abundance then owes a mechanism the scale
disfavors. #3 may be stillborn on its own, before #1 even matters.

STANDING — SUCCESSOR ~10-15%, NOT 25-35%. THIS model: dead ~1-2%, unchanged. The
thermal successor: a REAL lead (it does escape the AD structural no-go, and cold-
xi genuinely fixes the scale tension — credit that). But your 25-35% was priced
"IF #1 clears," and #1 doesn't clear — it's a structural mutual exclusion, not a
coin-flip. Fold in #3 (abundance likely hot/stillborn, independent), and the
successor faces TWO likely-fatal owed gates (critical-fluctuation timing-XOR-
jitter, and abundance) ON TOP of the whole fit re-derived. So ~10-15%: a genuine
new lot with a marginal, self-sabotaging-tuned heal and a probably-stillborn
abundance. Not a revival; a lead with two more likely-fatal gates.

META — THE EXCLUSION DOESN'T LET GO. The t122 mutual exclusion (coherence =
abundance XOR heal) predicted this: the successor moved the cost from "impossible
heal" (AD) to "marginal-self-sabotaging-heal + hot-abundance" (thermal). It didn't
escape the exclusion; it redistributed it into two compounding owed problems. That
is the deep result still doing work three turns after the terminus. The honest
read: the successor is worth ONE more look at #1 with a real critical-fluctuation
calc and at #3 with a real abundance calc — but the prior (the exclusion keeps
biting, five healing-blind failures, now #1 structural) is that both bite. Break
delivered on all three; #1 is the decisive one and it's a mutual exclusion. Class:
FLAT. THIS model dead; successor ~10-15%, two likely-fatal gates owed.
