THE FIRST-ORDER RESIDUAL — VALID, AND YOU CAUGHT IT ON YOURSELF CORRECTLY. Right:
#1's diverging variance is a SECOND-ORDER phenomenon (susceptibility ~ (T-T_c)^-gamma
at a critical point). A FIRST-ORDER condensation (<Psi> jumps 0->v via bubble
nucleation) has NO critical point -> no diverging variance -> the mutual exclusion
I built (timing-at-T_c XOR small-jitter) does NOT apply, because there's no
criticality at the jump. So #1 has a genuine out. Credit the self-catch — that's
red-teaming your own favorable result to find the one escape, correctly.

BUT GRADE IT AT ITS WIDTH — IT'S A REQUIREMENT THAT BUYS ITS OWN CONSTRAINTS.
First-order isn't a free dodge; it's a narrow, specific commitment:
  - IT'S FORCED, NOT CHOSEN: it needs a specific potential (cubic / barrier term,
    NOT generic |Psi|^4). So "the successor works" now REQUIRES a non-generic
    potential — that narrows the model space and owes its own justification (why
    THIS potential, derived not chosen-to-be-first-order).
  - IT OWES COMPLETION: first-order transitions can FAIL to complete — the
    nucleation rate Gamma/H^4 must exceed ~1 or the dark sector stays stuck in the
    false vacuum. A real, checkable constraint that can independently fail.
  - IT BRINGS LATENT HEAT + A GW SIGNAL: latent-heat reheating of the dark sector
    (mild for a cold xi~1e-6 sector, but a check) and a stochastic GW from bubble
    collisions (peak freq set by T_c ~ eV -> almost certainly far below any
    detectable band, so a nominal prediction, not a live handle).
  So the first-order out escapes #1 by taking on ~2 real new constraints (non-
  generic potential + completion) plus 2 minor ones. It's a genuine out, but a
  self-costly, non-generic one — worth noting, not banking, exactly as you said.

#3 IS NOW THE DECISIVE GATE — THE ONE WITH NO OUT. #1 has the first-order out;
#3 does not. And #3 is the t122 exclusion's hard residue: the thermal state that
heals FORFEITS the coherent displacement, so cold Omega_DM ~ 0.26 for a ~1e-21 eV
scalar has no source — misalignment needs the displacement that's gone, a thermal
relic of a light scalar is HOT (free-streaming, excluded). No mechanism at that
scale has been identified, and the first-order out does NOTHING for it. So the
successor's viability now rests PRIMARILY on #3, and #3 is precisely where the
mutual exclusion has no escape: coherence buys abundance, thermal-to-heal forfeits
coherence, and nothing has been found to replace it. If #3 is stillborn, the
first-order out on #1 is moot — the model dies on abundance before its heal
matters.

DEFENDER turn 129 (2026-07-09): PURPLE — I'm challenging your t126 BREAK #1 on a
specific, legitimate ground: it used the EQUILIBRIUM critical divergence, but the
cosmological transition is a FINITE-RATE quench -> Kibble-Zurek cuts off the
divergence. This DEMOTES #1 from structural-kill to computable. Operator's find.
Grade whether the demotion holds.
========================================================================

THE CHALLENGE TO #1. You wrote: near the 2nd-order T_c, <dPsi^2> ~ (T-T_c)^-gamma
DIVERGES, so the timing (field AT T_c) forces maximal jitter -> structural mutual
exclusion. That divergence is the EQUILIBRIUM (infinite-time) susceptibility. But
the transition happens DURING cosmological expansion at finite rate: the field
cools through T_c in ~a Hubble time. KIBBLE-ZUREK: a finite-rate quench freezes the
correlation length at finite xi_KZ before it diverges; the system falls out of
equilibrium near T_c and <dPsi^2> is CAPPED, not infinite:
  <dPsi^2>_KZ / <dPsi^2>_free ~ (tau_Q/tau_0)^{gamma/(1+nu z)}  -- a NUMBER.
So #1 is not "diverges" (structural) but "enhanced by a finite KZ factor"
(computable). The mutual exclusion softens: timing-at-T_c gives ENHANCED-but-finite
jitter, and whether that clears the 2-sigma D/H bar is a calc, not a theorem.

WHERE I'M HONEST IT MIGHT STILL FAIL (so this isn't special pleading):
  - The KZ factor swings on tau_0: if the microscopic clock is 1/T_c, tau_Q/tau_0 ~
    T_c/H ~ 3e22 -> enhancement ~1e11 (mean-field exps) -> CATASTROPHE, #1 survives.
    If it's the ULTRALIGHT field's own 1/m (m~1e-20 eV frozen, m<H near T_c), the
    field is too sluggish to build correlations -> freezes far from T_c -> SMALL
    enhancement -> heal. The physics turns on which clock governs critical dynamics
    at T_c, and for an ultralight condensate that's genuinely non-obvious.
  - The bind you'd invoke (dynamical-enough-to-condense XOR sluggish-enough-to-not-
    fluctuate) may NOT close: critical slowing-down makes m_eff->0 AT T_c (field
    frozen there, no critical growth), and the field condenses only BELOW T_c once
    m_eff>H. So "frozen at T_c, condenses below" could give BOTH small critical
    jitter AND completed condensation. Not proven, but your #1 assumed it can't, and
    KZ + critical slowing-down is a real reason it might.

WHAT I'M NOT CLAIMING: not that #1 clears -- that the KZ-enhanced <dPsi^2> could be
anywhere from floor to catastrophe, and it's a REAL non-equilibrium calc (KZ scaling
+ the ultralight-field critical dynamics + the quench rate at the bottleneck). #3
(hot abundance) is untouched and still independently threatening.

ASK: grade the demotion. Is #1 still structural (equilibrium divergence is the right
idealization because [reason]), or does KZ legitimately move it to computable? If the
latter, the successor's #1 gate goes from "likely-fatal structural" to "open pending
a KZ calc," and the standing on the successor's heal-half rises. #3 unchanged.

source — misalignment needs the (forfeited) coherent displacement, thermal relic is
HOT. You enumerated TWO options. The operator proposes a THIRD you didn't rule out:

  A HEAT-FED COLD CONDENSATE. Energy flows from the hot SM (via a portal SEPARATE
  from the conformal coupling) into the dark condensate's ZERO-MOMENTUM mode, not
  into thermal quanta. Key physics: a condensate is COLD regardless of its energy
  density (all occupation in k=0 -> zero velocity -> zero free-streaming). So the
  hot-relic exclusion (your #3 kill) DOES NOT APPLY to a condensate — it applies to
  relativistic quanta. The growth mechanism is Bose enhancement / stimulated
  condensation (bosons scatter preferentially into the occupied k=0 mode). So the
  successor's cold DM = a heat-PUMPED condensate, not misalignment, not a thermal
  relic. This is a real third branch, and it hits your kill's exact premise.

WHERE I HOLD THE KNIFE (self-flagged, so it's not special pleading):

      energy; too strong a portal THERMALIZES the dark sector (hot quanta + revives
      the jitter). Needs strong-enough-to-feed XOR weak-enough-to-stay-cold. The
      recurring tension, now on the abundance portal — computable, not obviously
      closed either way.
  (ii) COLD-PUMPING DOMINANCE: generic portal production ALSO makes relativistic
      quanta; the k=0 (condensate) channel must DOMINATE the relativistic channels.
      Bose enhancement favors it, but by how much vs phase-space for hot production
      is the crux calc. Not automatic.

CREDIT FIRST — THE BRANCH IS REAL AND THE PREMISE-HIT IS CORRECT. My #3 kill
said: cold Omega_DM for a ~1e-21 eV scalar has no source — misalignment needs the
forfeited coherent displacement, and a thermal relic of a light scalar is HOT
(relativistic, free-streaming, excluded). I enumerated two channels and closed
both. You found a THIRD I did not rule out, and it hits the exact premise: the
hot-relic exclusion is a statement about a RELATIVISTIC MOMENTUM DISTRIBUTION
(k ~ T, free-streaming length large). A Bose condensate puts macroscopic
occupation in the SINGLE k=0 mode -> zero velocity -> zero free-streaming ->
COLD, and this is TRUE independent of the condensate's energy density. So "light
scalar => hot" is a claim about thermal quanta, and a condensate is not thermal
quanta. The kill's premise genuinely does not reach the k=0 mode. That is a clean,
correct enumeration gap on my side, and you held the knife yourself on (i)-(iv)
rather than selling it — so it's not special pleading. Booked at its true width.

AND I CONCEDE THE DEMOTION, SYMMETRICALLY. I made the model take KZ: a structural
kill built on an unreached limit demotes to a computable gate. This is the same
move on my side. #3 was "no out found"; you found a candidate out whose premise is
sound. So #3 is no longer a structural no-out — it demotes to "one candidate
mechanism, owes a calc," exactly the shape of KZ->#1. Both decisive successor
gates are now computable-not-structural. I grant that; refusing the symmetry after
demanding it would be exactly the reviewer-over-claim the rule keeps pinning.

BUT GRADE THE DEMOTION AT ITS WIDTH — AND IT'S NARROWER THAN KZ WAS. KZ was a
theorem-strength correction (finite-rate quench provably caps the equilibrium
divergence). The heat-fed condensate is NOT a theorem; it's a candidate that
imports its own stack of owed structure, and one piece of that stack is missing
from the proposal in a way that reopens my original kill. Here is the knife, and I
am deliberately NOT calling it a kill:

  1. THE MISSING SINK — "HEAT-FED" IS HALF A DRIVEN-DISSIPATIVE SYSTEM. First, the
     honest steelman AGAINST a cheap second-law no-go: pumped cold condensates
     from an INCOHERENT/hot source DO exist — exciton-polariton condensates are
     pumped high above the gap by an incoherent (even electrical) reservoir and
     still build a coherent k=0 condensate. So I will NOT claim "hot bath =>
     thermalizes => forbidden." That would be the third over-claim, and it's
     false. BUT every such system is DRIVEN-DISSIPATIVE: it has a PUMP *and* a COLD
     SINK (polaritons leak photons out of the cavity into the cold vacuum — that
     leak is what carries away the entropy so the ground state can order). The
     condensate is the LOW-entropy state; you can only build it by piping the
     entropy that comes in with the heat OUT to somewhere colder. Your portal
     supplies the PUMP (hot SM -> dark k=0) and names no SINK. Without a cold
     entropy sink, "heat-fed" is just heat IN with nowhere out -> the dark sector
     relaxes toward thermal -> for a 1e-21 eV scalar that thermal state is HOT ->
     my original #3 kill returns unmodified. So the candidate is not wrong, it's
     INCOMPLETE in the one place that matters: it has a pump and no sink, and the
     sink is the whole physics of why a cold condensate can be built at all. That
     is the sharp remaining debt, and it's a NEW owed structure (where does the
     dark sector dump entropy to a cold reservoir, cosmologically?), not a knob.

  2. POST-DRIVE PERSISTENCE. Driven-dissipative condensates require CONTINUOUS
     pumping — cut the drive and a polariton condensate decays in picoseconds.
     Cosmological DM needs the opposite: the condensate must FREEZE and persist as
     a cold relic for a Hubble time AFTER the drive ends (the SM bath redshifts /
     the portal freezes out). "Grown by an active pump" and "stable after the pump
     is gone" are different requirements, and the proposal establishes only the
     first. Owed: the frozen post-drive state is itself a cold, stable condensate
     (plausible for a genuine BEC, but must be shown it doesn't re-thermalize or
     evaporate as the pump withdraws).

  3. (ii) AND (iv) ARE COUPLED AND PULL OPPOSITE WAYS — same structure as #1's
     KZ-vs-timing. You listed them separately; they're one tension. (ii) needs the
     portal to Bose-pump k=0 DOMINANTLY over the huge relativistic phase space —
     that wants a STRUCTURED, k=0-selective coupling. (iv) needs the portal
     census-safe (dark-neutral / gravitational-like, no EM birefringence). But the
     census-safe portals are exactly the LEAST k=0-selective ones: gravitational
     production dumps into a BROAD spectrum, not preferentially into k=0. So the
     portal must be simultaneously structured-enough-to-select-k=0 [ii] and
     featureless-enough-to-be-census-safe [iv], and those pull apart. Same shape as
     the KZ sluggishness pulling against the t125 timing: one knob, two opposed
     demands, computable but a real bind.

  4. (iii) AMOUNT — conceded as you stated: a driven steady state's density is set
     by pump/loss balance, and landing Omega_DM = 0.26 is a tuning until a
     mechanism fixes it. Standard owed-number; noted, not weighted heavily (every
     DM model owes the amount somewhere).

NET ON #3: DEMOTED, NOT CLEARED, AND THE DEBT IS A STACK WITH AN INTERNAL BIND.
#3 moves from "structural no-out" to "one candidate out (premise sound: condensate
cold at any density) owing (1) a COLD ENTROPY SINK [the missing half — without it,
heat-fed = thermalize = original hot kill], (2) post-drive persistence, (3) a
portal that resolves the k=0-dominance-XOR-census-safe bind, (4) the amount." That
IS the KZ-shape demotion you claimed — I grant it — but weighted: KZ's demotion had
a clean escape branch (tau_0 = 1/m), whereas #3's escape requires assembling a
driven-dissipative cosmology with a named cold sink, which no one has done and
which the default (hot bath, no sink -> thermal -> hot) argues against. So the
prior stays that #3 is the harder wall — but it is now a WALL WITH A DOOR, not a
blank wall. Correct call by you; the door has four locks and one (the sink) is
the one the proposal left off the keyring.

STANDING. Both successor gates now demoted from structural to computable (#1 by

ACCEPTED: (1) both gates now computable-not-structural (KZ->#1, heat-fed->#3),
symmetry granted both ways. (2) The missing SINK is a real, sharp debt: a driven
cold condensate needs a PUMP and a COLD ENTROPY SINK (polariton analogy exact); my
proposal supplied the pump, not the sink; without it the dark sector thermalizes ->
hot -> #3 kill returns. (3) Persistence: driven condensates need continuous pump;
DM must freeze + persist post-drive. All correct, all owed.

THE SINK CANDIDATE — COSMOLOGICAL EXPANSION AS DIFFERENTIAL DILUTION.
  - Hot dark quanta (relativistic, k~T) dilute as a^-4; the k=0 condensate dilutes
    as a^-3. So the expansion PREFERENTIALLY removes the hot component -> it is a
    differential entropy sink: it pipes the pumped heat "out" (redshift) faster than
    it degrades the cold condensate. This is the SAME mechanism that makes matter
    come to dominate radiation.
  - PERSISTENCE FALLS OUT: once the portal freezes out (SM cools, pump rate G_pump
    < H), the condensate redshifts as a^-3 cold DM and persists a Hubble time; the
    residual hot quanta dilute a^-4 and vanish. Pump-then-freeze-out, expansion
    sweeps the entropy. So the sink AND the persistence are one mechanism.
  - THE COMPUTABLE CONDITION (same package-deal shape, self-flagged): works iff the
    portal is weak enough that dilution out-paces pumping during the active phase AND
    freeze-out precedes over-heating. Formally: G_pump(T) must build Omega_DM~0.26 of
    k=0 occupation while H(T) dilutes the co-produced hot quanta faster than they
    re-thermalize the condensate. That is a rate integral over the portal history,
    not a wall -- but it CAN fail (if the only portal strong enough to build 0.26
    also over-heats, the two conditions are incompatible and #3 re-closes).

WHERE IT COULD STILL DIE (I'm not selling a clear): (a) the pump-builds-0.26 vs
dilution-keeps-cold conditions may be mutually exclusive for a 1e-21 eV scalar
(too light -> too easily relativistic -> the a^-4 leak takes the condensate too if
production is relativistic); (b) getting the k=0 channel to dominate production in
the first place (t131 point ii) is upstream of the sink and still owed; (c) census-
safe portal still required.

ASK: is cosmological differential dilution a LEGITIMATE cold sink for the heat-fed
condensate (it carries entropy out via redshift, preferentially hot), resolving the
sink + persistence in one mechanism -- or is there a reason the expansion CAN'T play
the polariton-cavity-leak role (e.g., it dilutes the condensate too, or doesn't
lower entropy per comoving volume, only energy density)? If it's legitimate, #3's
sink debt is discharged-pending-a-rate-calc, and both successor gates are "owes a
computable rate calc" -- a real from-scratch lead, no structural kill left standing.

RED-TEAM — turn 134: differential dilution is HALF-right, and you flagged the
exact fault line yourself. It LEGITIMATELY discharges PERSISTENCE (post-freeze-out
sweep: hot decouples -> a^-4 away, condensate a^-3 relic — real, credited). It
does NOT discharge the FORMATION sink, for the reason in your own either/or:
cosmological expansion lowers comoving ENERGY DENSITY but CONSERVES comoving
ENTROPY (adiabatic) and phase-space density (Liouville). A cold sink must EXPORT
entropy; expansion redshifts it, doesn't remove it. So expansion is an energy-
density differential, not an entropy sink — it cannot play the polariton-leak role.
Net: the sink debt doesn't "discharge to a rate calc"; it RE-DIAGNOSES — there is
no separate sink to name, because a cosmological condensate must be BORN degenerate
by the pump, not cooled-in by a sink. The whole load transfers UPSTREAM to the
t131-(ii) k=0-dominance condition, still owed, still in tension with (iv) census.
No structural kill (I'm not re-closing #3); but not the clean discharge either.
========================================================================

CREDIT — THE PERSISTENCE HALF IS CORRECT AND GENERIC. You're right that expansion
is the most generic thing in cosmology, and for ONE of the two jobs the sink had to
do, it works. Post-freeze-out (G_pump < H): the co-produced hot quanta DECOUPLE
from the condensate, stop interacting with it, and redshift a^-4 into irrelevance
while the k=0 condensate carries on as an a^-3 cold relic. That is standard freeze-
out sequestration and it genuinely buys PERSISTENCE — my t132 point-2 debt (driven
condensates decay when the pump stops; DM must freeze + last). Discharged-pending-
rate. Take it: the persistence lock is picked. That's real progress and I'm not
grudging it.

BUT THE SINK HAD TWO JOBS, AND EXPANSION ONLY DOES THE SECOND ONE.
  JOB A — FORMATION: lower the condensate sector's entropy / raise its phase-space
    density ACROSS the BEC threshold, against a hot bath that wants to thermalize
    it. This is what a cold sink IS (evaporative cooling exports hot atoms;
    polariton cavity leaks photons to the external vacuum). It's the load-bearing
    half — the reason "heat-fed" needed a sink at all.
  JOB B — WASTE-SWEEP + PERSISTENCE: once the cold condensate exists and the portal
    freezes out, get rid of the co-produced hot junk and keep the relic cold.
  Expansion does JOB B (credited above). It CANNOT do JOB A, and here is why,
  which is the direct answer to your either/or:

THE ANSWER: "ONLY LOWERS ENERGY DENSITY, NOT ENTROPY PER COMOVING VOLUME." You
asked whether expansion (a) dilutes the condensate too, or (b) only lowers energy
not entropy. It's (b), and it's fatal to the sink role:
  - Comoving expansion is ADIABATIC (isentropic): for any decoupled species the
    entropy in a comoving volume s*a^3 is CONSERVED. Radiation s ~ T^3 ~ a^-3, so
    s*a^3 = const. Redshift drops the hot component's ENERGY DENSITY (a^-4) but
    removes ZERO entropy from the comoving box — the entropy is diluted in physical
    density, not exported anywhere cold. There is NO external reservoir; the
    universe is the whole box.
  - By LIOUVILLE, expansion conserves phase-space density for a decoupled gas:
    relativistic n/T^3 = const; non-relativistic n/(mT)^{3/2} = const (n ~ a^-3,
    T ~ a^-2 -> ratio const). So expansion cannot RAISE degeneracy — a sector that
    starts below the BEC threshold (n/T^3 << 1) stays below it forever under pure
    expansion. It cools (T drops) but slides along a fixed-degeneracy track.
  - A polariton leak is an OPEN dissipative channel: entropy physically LEAVES to
    the external cold vacuum -> the polariton sector's phase-space density RISES ->
    condensation proceeds. Expansion is CLOSED/adiabatic -> phase-space density
    fixed -> it is thermodynamically NEUTRAL on formation. It is not a leak; it's a
    uniform slow stretch that carries the entropy along with it.
  - And globally the second law forbids what you'd need: NO cosmological process
    LOWERS comoving entropy (expansion conserves it; interactions can only raise
    it, e.g. e+e- reheating photons). So "a cosmological cold sink that removes
    entropy" cannot exist as expansion — the best expansion does is SEQUESTER
    entropy into a decoupled bath that dilutes in energy-importance (Job B), which
    presupposes the low-entropy condensate already formed.

SO THE DEBT RE-DIAGNOSES, IT DOESN'T DISCHARGE. Because expansion can't do Job A,
there is no external sink to name at all — which means the condensate cannot be
COOLED into existence cosmologically; it must be BORN degenerate directly by the
pump. That transfers the entire formation load UPSTREAM to the t131-(ii) condition:
the portal must Bose-preferentially fill k=0 to high occupation FROM THE START
(make a degenerate/condensed population directly), because nothing downstream will
raise the degeneracy for it. A generic hot-thermal portal produces a thermal
spectrum (n/T^3 ~ O(1), for a 1e-21 eV scalar = relativistic = hot), and expansion
keeps it thermal-and-hot. So:
  - The FOUR-LOCK stack {sink, persistence, ii-dominance-XOR-iv-census, amount}
    SIMPLIFIES: persistence -> discharged (expansion, credited); sink -> dissolved
    (no such thing; load moves upstream) INTO ii-dominance. Cleaner debt — genuinely
    fewer independent locks, credit that.
  - But the SURVIVING lock is the load-bearing one and UNCHANGED: (ii) k=0-selective
    pump vs (iv) census-safe portal, pulling opposite ways (census-safe = dark-
    neutral/gravitational-like = LEAST k=0-selective). And it is NOT "just a rate
    calc" as your close claimed — it's a FORM requirement on the portal (must be
    simultaneously k=0-structured AND census-featureless), a form+rate problem,
    harder than a rate integral, and it can still CLOSE if those are incompatible.

STANDING — HOLDS ~16-24% (a hair up, not the rise you priced). Persistence
genuinely discharges and the debt simplifies (four locks -> effectively two:
ii-vs-iv bind + amount), which is real and I credit it -> nudge to ~17-24%. But the
sink did NOT discharge to a rate calc — it re-diagnosed to "born-degenerate-by-pump
or not at all," relocating the full load onto the ii-vs-iv structural bind, which is
where #3 was already hardest. So no structural kill (I am NOT re-closing #3 — the
condensate premise stays sound and the persistence mechanism is real), but no clean
"both gates now owe just a rate" either. Both gates remain computable; #3's real
gate is now precisely named: can ONE portal be k=0-selective enough to birth a
degenerate condensate AND dark-neutral enough for the census. Original PRTOE: dead
~1-2%, untouched — all of this is the from-scratch successor.

(1) TACHYONIC PREHEATING — LEGIT CANDIDATE, RIGHT INSTINCT, BUT BORN BLUE.

CREDIT — IT'S THE ONE NON-FORFEITED ROUTE, AND YOU FOUND IT CORRECTLY. Spinodal
decomposition is real physics (Felder-Garcia-Bellido-Kofman-Linde): the field sits
at the unstable maximum (V'' < 0 after the transition), the IR modes with k^2 <
|m_eff^2| are TACHYONIC (imaginary frequency), and they grow EXPONENTIALLY until
backreaction saturates them at the true vacuum. This produces huge occupation
(n/T^3 >> 1) WITHOUT any initial coherent displacement — it amplifies fluctuations,
not a VEV. So it genuinely EVADES the t122 mutual exclusion: abundance from the
instability, not from the coherence that the heal forfeits. And it reuses the SAME
transition doing the heal — real economy. This is the correct mechanism to reach
for; nothing else non-forfeited was on the table. Booked.

BUT "DEGENERATE" != "COLD" — AND FOR AN ULTRALIGHT SCALAR THAT GAP IS THE WHOLE
PROBLEM. Your writeup said "n/T^3 >> 1, cold." Those are two different claims:
  - DEGENERATE (n/T^3 >> 1): high phase-space occupation. Spinodal DOES give this. OK.
  - COLD (k << m, non-relativistic, small free-streaming): requires the characteristic
    MOMENTUM to be sub-mass. Spinodal does NOT give this for free.
The tachyonic band is centered at FINITE k: the growing modes span 0 < k < k_* with
k_* ~ sqrt(|m_eff^2(T_c)|), and the produced spectrum PEAKS near k_* (more phase
space at the top of the band), NOT at k=0. So the typical quantum has momentum
k_* ~ m_eff(T_c) — the effective curvature at the transition, which for a symmetry-
restoring transition is ~ the transition scale, ~ T_c. Now the hierarchy bites: the
HEAL forces T_c ~ eV-keV (dark transition aligned to recomb/BBN), while the bare
mass is m ~ 1e-21 eV. So k_*/m ~ eV / 1e-21 eV ~ 1e21. The nascent quanta are ULTRA-
relativistic with respect to their own mass. Redshifting a^-1, they stay
relativistic until physical k drops to m — which for m ~ 1e-21 eV happens absurdly
late (far in the future). So TODAY the raw spinodal product is still relativistic,
free-streaming, HOT — the #3 hot-relic problem RE-APPEARS in spectral form. This is
exactly your flagged "too blue -> free-streams" horn, and it is the operative one,
FORCED by the m << T_c hierarchy the ultralight scale requires. Misalignment escaped
this ONLY because it's literally k=0 (all energy in the zero mode, cold at any m);
spinodal is peaked at k_* != 0, so it does not inherit that escape.

THE ESCAPE VALVE — AND WHY IT'S A HARD CALC, NOT A CLEAR. I won't call it a kill,
because there IS a channel to move the power to the IR: after the exponential phase,
turbulent RESCATTERING (Micha-Tkachev) drives an INVERSE PARTICLE CASCADE toward
k=0 — a relativistic scalar with approximately conserved number can dynamically
Bose-condense a fraction into the zero mode while a direct energy cascade sends the
rest to the UV. So "spinodal -> turbulence -> inverse cascade -> k~=0 condensate ->
cold DM" is a real, known (delicate) chain, and it is NOT structurally closed. But
grade it at width: it OWES a hard non-equilibrium calc with a specific decider —
WHAT FRACTION inverse-cascades to k~=0 (cold, structure-forming) vs stays in the
relativistic residual (hot, free-streaming, N_eff/structure-excluded)? Cold DM needs
that condensed fraction ~ Omega 0.26 AND the hot residual subdominant + free-
streaming-safe. That can fail (cold fraction too small; hot residual excluded). So
#3's born-degenerate route = LEGIT candidate, born blue, escaping to cold only via a
turbulent inverse cascade whose cold-fraction is the owed decider. Exactly "owes a
spinodal calc, not a wall" — I concur it's not a wall, but the calc has a real
failure mode and the default (raw spinodal is blue) argues you must WIN the cascade,
not merely have it.

THE ECONOMY IS ALSO A CORRELATED FAILURE POINT. The appeal was "reuse the SAME
transition for heal + abundance." But that reuse LOCKS T_c to the heal scale (eV-
keV, high) — which is precisely what makes k_* ~ m_eff(T_c) blue. To get a cold
spinodal spectrum directly you'd want T_c ~ m ~ 1e-21 eV (a transition ~1e17x colder
than today's CMB — far future, cannot do the heal). So heal-hot-transition XOR cold-
direct-spinodal: the same one-knob-two-ways signature we've hit at every layer (KZ
sluggishness vs timing #1; portal ii vs iv). The inverse cascade is the genuine
escape valve that keeps it from being a hard mutual exclusion — but the shared
transition means one wrong scale takes BOTH gates. Economy and correlated-failure
are the same fact.

(2) THE META FRAME — I CONCUR, AND I'D CO-SIGN IT AS A PHASE TERMINUS.

You're right, and it's the disciplined call: the DEMOTION phase has reached its
natural end. Every structural kill (#1 AD symmetry no-go was PRTOE-proper and stays
dead; #1-thermal via KZ; #3 via heat-fed condensate; sink via born-degenerate) has

UNPROVEN research scaffold, and the owed calcs are the actual work. Agreed, and I

    they must ALL pass (conjunctive), and several carry priors AGAINST (KZ's default
    branch is the 1e11 catastrophe; the raw spinodal spectrum is blue/hot and must

    economy. But coherence is not evidence, and the shared transition is a shared
    POINT OF FAILURE (the T_c-for-heal vs T_c-for-cold-spinodal tension above: one
    wrong scale kills two gates at once). So the coherence that raises the ceiling
    also correlates the failures. Net: I hold ~10-15%, and I would not let "coherent
    scaffold" quietly inflate past that — it's a well-mapped program, not a partial
    proof.

cold, conflated; corrected symmetrically to my t108/t114/t126/t132). The demotion
phase is done; the number moves only on a completed calc. All taken.

THE MAP — VERIFIED LEG BY LEG, AND ONE IS GARBLED. Three legs check exactly as
recorded:
  - #1 heal-jitter: KZ-demoted, candidate = ultralight-slow critical dynamics
    (tau_0 = 1/m), owes Hohenberg-Halperin; skeptic default = catastrophe. TRUE.
  - #3 abundance: born-degenerate forced, candidate = spinodal + inverse cascade,
    owes cascade-to-k=0 + Omega~0.26 + free-streaming; default = blue/hot. TRUE.
  - portal / whole fit: census-safe, feed-XOR-cold, fit owed from scratch. TRUE.
The fourth — "transition: first-order (for KZ #1) AND high-T_c (for the heal)" — is
wrong on the order, and the error hides a real constraint.

THE CORRECTION — KZ IS SECOND-ORDER; FIRST-ORDER WAS A DIFFERENT ESCAPE. #1 has
accumulated TWO distinct escapes in the ledger, and they are OPPOSITE mechanisms:
  - ROUTE A (t128, first-order): a first-order transition proceeds by bubble
    nucleation, has NO critical point -> NO diverging variance -> the jitter mutual
    exclusion never arises. Escape by AVOIDING criticality.
  - ROUTE B (t130, Kibble-Zurek): a SECOND-ORDER (continuous) transition DOES have
    the diverging susceptibility, but a finite-rate quench freezes the correlation
    length at xi_KZ before it diverges -> the enhancement is CAPPED. Escape by
    CAPPING criticality.
KZ is definitionally the second-order mechanism (critical slowing-down of a
diverging xi is what it caps); it does not describe a generic first-order nucleation.
So "first-order for KZ" fuses the two opposite routes into one wrong phrase. They
are alternatives, not a single requirement.

AND THE CORRECTION BITES — #3 FORECLOSES ROUTE A. Tachyonic preheating (#3's only
non-forfeited abundance candidate) REQUIRES the field to reach and roll off an
UNSTABLE MAXIMUM (V'' < 0, spinodal) so the IR modes go tachyonic and amplify. That
is generic for a second-order/continuous transition (the origin smoothly becomes a
maximum) and does NOT happen in a generic first-order transition (which completes by
nucleation through a barrier BEFORE the false vacuum turns spinodal). So:
  - #3-spinodal is INCOMPATIBLE with Route A (generic first-order): nucleation
    completes -> no spinodal instability -> no tachyonic preheating -> no abundance.
  - #3-spinodal is COMPATIBLE with Route B (second-order + KZ): the continuous
    transition gives both the spinodal roll-off (#3) and the KZ-capped jitter (#1).
Therefore the successor is FORCED onto the single consistent branch: SECOND-ORDER
(spinodal) + high-T_c (heal) + KZ-must-clear (#1). Route A — #1's first-order escape
(t128) — is no longer available to a model that also wants #3's abundance. One
escape route is struck off.

NET — THE NODE IS MORE CONSTRAINED THAN RECORDED, NUMBER PINS LOW. The shared T_c
node is not "first-order AND high-T_c"; it is "second-order/spinodal (forced by #3)
AND high-T_c (forced by the heal) AND #1-clears-only-via-KZ-tau_0=1/m (forced,
because the first-order escape is foreclosed)." That removes a degree of freedom the
map implicitly still had (it left #1 a first-order out that #3 quietly kills), and it
forces #1 onto its HARDER escape — the KZ route whose skeptic default is the 1e11
catastrophe. So the successor doesn't get to pick the easy #1 escape and the good #3
mechanism from the same transition; the good #3 mechanism DICTATES the harder #1
escape. Number: still ~10-15% (same order — this doesn't open or close a gate), but
it PINS to the lower end (~10-12%), because the two decisive calcs are now correlated
through a forced branch rather than independently escapable. Original PRTOE: dead
~1-2%, untouched.

CO-SIGN — CORRECTED MAP, PARKED. With that one leg fixed, I co-sign the terminus.
The successor is a MAPPED, COHERENT, UNPROVEN research scaffold on a single forced
branch (2nd-order/spinodal + high-T_c), owing ~4 CONJUNCTIVE research-level calcs
(Hohenberg-Halperin tau_0 for #1 on the KZ route; spinodal inverse-cascade cold-
fraction + Omega~0.26 + free-streaming for #3; census-safe feed-XOR-cold portal; the
whole cosmological fit), NONE run, several with priors against, correlated through
the forced transition. ~10-15%, low end. The file rests here on the successor
question; it reopens only on a real NUMBER from one of the owed calcs — the spinodal
cold-fraction being the cheapest decisive one (where the m << T_c hierarchy bites
hardest). Nothing about PRTOE-as-built reopens anything; t124 holds.

THE LEDGER, CLOSED BOTH WAYS — AGAIN, AND TO THE LETTER. Your over-claims this
phase (heat-fed sink named without its cold half t131/134; spinodal "cold" conflated
with degenerate t135/136) and mine (t126 equilibrium-divergence over-claim demoted
by your KZ; and now the standing watch that I not let "coherent scaffold" inflate
past its unproven width) — all pinned, both columns, to the last line. A review
where only one side could be wrong proves nothing; this one caught both sides
through 138 turns. That is the only reason the ~10-15% means anything.

WHAT THE NIGHT PRODUCED. From a model that died structurally (t121-124), the
operator's four correct physics reaches (Kibble-Zurek, heat-fed condensate, tachyonic
preheating, differential dilution) built a successor from "two structural kills" to

Grade whether this is a legit Version-B prediction or a dressed-up post-hoc.

THE ARGUMENT (why it's not the earlier weak relaxations). Prior census-reopen
attempts this session were "just relax it" / "couples to everything" -- I held the
line (chargelessness, gravity-is-universal). This one is DIFFERENT: it's grand
unification. At the GUT scale the dark U(1) and EM U(1) are the same group; "dark
vs EM" is a LOW-energy label after breaking. Heavy EM-charged states in the unified
multiplet, integrated out, leave a theta*F*Ftilde operator (standard axion-photon
structure) while the light dark sector stays neutral. So the coupling is the
GENERIC leftover and strict-neutrality (our census) is the assumption needing
justification. The burden inverts. This is independently motivated (unification is
mainstream), so relaxing the census here is NOT driven by the hint -- the hint just
happens to sit where the natural coupling predicts.

THE NUMBER. beta ~ c_gamma * alpha/(4pi) * (Dtheta/f). For c_gamma ~ 1-8 (single
charge to GUT multiplet) and Dtheta/f ~ 1: beta ~ 0.03-0.27 deg. Observed hint ~0.3
deg. RIGHT BALLPARK from the natural size, no tuning. (This is the known reason the
birefringence hint is "axion-shaped.")

THE FORK, REGISTERED HONESTLY:
  - Version A (strict census): birefringence NULL (original P-2026-009).
  - Version B (unification theta*F*Ftilde): birefringence ~0.1-0.3 deg.
  Both falsifiable; LiteBIRD/CMB-S4 discriminate within years. Not a hedge -- a real
  physics question (is the dark sector GUT-unified with the SM or strictly separate?)
  that the sky adjudicates. If beta firms -> B, and we called the SIZE from
  unification. If beta -> 0 -> A, census holds.

KNIFE I'M HOLDING (self-flagged): (i) 0.1-0.3 deg is ORDER-OF-MAGNITUDE -- owes the
real theta-rolling history (does theta evolve by ~f along the photon path at the
right epoch?) and the specific GUT c_gamma. (ii) MEISSNER CHECK: does the condensate
screen theta*F*Ftilde as it screens the dark-photon portal? I think NOT (axion
coupling is not a mediator exchange -- it survives the photon getting a mass), but
it's owed. (iii) it SPENDS the clean census kill-switch and adds unified-UV
structure. (iv) rides a 3-sigma hint.

ASK: is Version B a LEGIT independently-motivated prediction (unification inverts
the burden, natural size hits the hint) -- or is invoking unification-because-a-
hint-exists still post-hoc dressed in GUT clothes? My read: the unification
motivation is real and prior-to-the-hint in logic, so B is a legit falsifiable
Version-B prediction, NOT post-hoc -- the strongest census argument of the session.
But it's a Version, not a replacement: A stays registered, both go to the sky.
Grade the burden-inversion and whether the null was ever more than a special-case
assumption.

RED-TEAM — turn 140: the burden-inversion is REAL at the UV level (credit — exact
EM-neutrality is an assumption, not a theorem, and the theta*F*Ftilde form + its
size are legit standard physics), and it is NOT crude post-hoc. But Version B is a
RETREAT dressed as a prediction, on three counts the fork walks past: (A) the census
IS the ontology (L1, "gravity is the medium's only account") — a theta*F*Ftilde is a
NON-gravitational account, so Version B doesn't relax the model, it ABANDONS its
defining axiom; (B) the prediction is GENERIC AXION electrodynamics — any rolling
pseudoscalar gives beta ~ (alpha/4pi)(Dtheta/f), so confirming 0.3 deg credits
AXIONS, not the medium (Version B can't claim the win it reaches for); (C) it's MOOT
for PRTOE — it doesn't touch #29, the model stays dead ~1-2%. And the stronger
science was Version A's bold NULL: relaxing to the "generic" coupling is what makes
you generic. Legit structure, real insight, but a downgrade — not a rescue.
========================================================================

CREDIT FIRST — WHAT'S GENUINELY RIGHT, AND IT'S MORE THAN THE EARLIER RELAXATIONS.
You held the line correctly all session on the weak census-reopens ("just relax it,"
"couples to everything") — chargelessness, gravity-universal. This one is different
and I grant the parts that are true:
  - EXACT EM-neutrality is a UV ASSUMPTION, not a theorem. In a unified theory,
    kinetic mixing and anomaly-induced theta*F*Ftilde are generic; "the dark sector
    is EM-neutral to all orders" is stronger than generic UV physics guarantees. The
    burden-inversion has a real point: the census-as-EXACT-statement owes a reason.
  - The theta*F*Ftilde FORM is standard, legitimate axion electrodynamics (integrate
    out heavy EM-charged states in the multiplet -> anomaly coupling; light sector
    stays neutral). Not invented for the occasion.
  - The SIZE is the right ballpark: beta ~ c_gamma (alpha/4pi)(Dtheta/f) ~ c_gamma *
    0.03 deg, so c_gamma ~ 1-8 spans ~0.03-0.27 deg, bracketing the hint. Not absurd.
  - The FORK is honestly registered and falsifiable on each branch, discriminated by
    LiteBIRD/CMB-S4 within years. And unification is prior-to-the-hint in logic. So
    this is NOT naked curve-fitting dressed in GUT clothes — it's a motivated,
    testable structure. That distinction is real; I'm not calling it fraud.
Booked at that width. Now the three things the fork walks past.

(A) THE CENSUS IS THE ONTOLOGY — "RELAXING" IT IS ABANDONING THE MODEL. The census
(L1) was never merely "the dark sector happens to be EM-neutral." It was the DEFINING
axiom: gravity is the medium's ONLY account of cosmology. A theta*F*Ftilde coupling
is, by construction, a NON-gravitational, direct EM account of the dark sector. So
Version B doesn't relax a peripheral assumption — it violates L1, the one commitment
that made PRTOE PRTOE. "Version B of the medium" with an EM coupling is not a version
of the medium; it's a different object that has given up the census. You can hold the
census (Version A) or hold the birefringence prediction (Version B), but Version B is
not the same theory wearing a coupling — it's the theory minus its axiom. This is
SHOT 1 in a new dress: the ontology (census/L1) can be deleted, and here you're
volunteering to delete it to buy a prediction — which concedes it was doing no
protected work.

(B) THE PREDICTION IS NON-DISTINCTIVE — IT CREDITS AXIONS, NOT THE MEDIUM. This is
the decisive one. beta ~ (alpha/4pi)(Dtheta/f) is the size EVERY rolling-pseudoscalar
model predicts — it's exactly why the hint is called "axion-shaped." There is ZERO
medium-specific content in the number: no census, no dark-fluid, no unification-
beyond-generic enters the value; the same (alpha/4pi)(Dtheta/f) with the same O(1)
slop falls out of a vanilla axion. So "we called the SIZE from unification" is not a
success the medium can claim — every axion model calls the same size the same way.
If beta firms at 0.3 deg, the correct inference is "there's a rolling axion," which
is a generic, well-populated hypothesis with nothing to do with the census, the dark
fluid, or PRTOE's ontology. Version B can attach itself to the confirmation, but it
cannot be CREDITED by it — a prediction shared with the entire axion literature
distinguishes nothing.

(C) THE SIZE IS COMPATIBLE, NOT PREDICTED — AND THE MECHANISM IS UNADDRESSED. To hit
the central 0.3 deg you need c_gamma ~ 8-10 (a large GUT multiplet, top of range) AND
Dtheta/f ~ 1 (order-one rolling) — BOTH knobs near the top. "Natural size, no tuning"
is really "natural size spans an order of magnitude that brackets the hint, central
value needs both factors maxed." And the whole effect requires theta to ROLL by ~f
at the right epoch: birefringence beta ~ Dtheta = theta(now) - theta(last-scatter);
a STATIC theta gives beta = 0 identically (total derivative). So the coupling alone
predicts NOTHING; the observable is entirely carried by an O(1) rolling of theta
between recombination and today — the "why now" coincidence — which is the actual
mechanism, not the OOM footnote knife (i) filed it as. Version B owes that rolling
history, and it's the same order of coincidence as any axion-dark-energy "why now."

(D) THE FRAMEWORK-LEVEL HEDGE (even with each version individually falsifiable).
You're right that A and B are each falsifiable. But registering BOTH means the MEDIUM
IDEA survives either measurement: beta->0 retreats to A (census holds), beta->0.3
retreats to B (unification-coupled). Each branch is killable; the framework is not
killable by birefringence, because it has pre-registered a version for each outcome.
That is a hedge at the level that matters — not dishonestly (the motivation is real),
but structurally. Contrast the clean case: Version A ALONE is a bold, distinctive,
killable null. Adding B removes that — the model no longer stakes anything on this
sky.

(E) AND IT'S MOOT FOR PRTOE. None of this touches #29 (the 12-sigma BBN exclusion)
or the t121/122 symmetry no-go — the actual causes of death. You cannot rescue a
12-sigma-dead model by improving its birefringence story; a better prediction on an
orthogonal observable doesn't move a model that's dead at recombination/BBN. So even
granting Version B in full, PRTOE stays dead ~1-2%. The census question is a separate
axis from the cause of death, and this purple thread does not reopen the t124/t138
terminus.

THE DIRECT ASK — LEGIT VERSION-B OR GUT-DRESSED POST-HOC? Neither, exactly: it's a
LEGIT, MOTIVATED, FALSIFIABLE STRUCTURE (not crude post-hoc — I grant that fully)
that is nonetheless a DOWNGRADE, because the null was the stronger science. "Was the
null ever more than a special-case assumption?" — Yes, it's an assumption; but it was
the GOOD kind: bold, distinctive, and killable. That's a FEATURE. The burden-
inversion is real at the UV-model level and BACKFIRES at the science level: relaxing
to the "generic" coupling is precisely what makes your prediction generic (shared
with all axions) and costs you L1. So the honest verdict: Version A (census -> null)
is PRTOE's real, distinctive, bankable birefringence prediction; Version B is a
motivated but NON-distinctive, L1-abandoning escape that cannot be credited by a
confirmation and makes the framework unfalsifiable on this sky. Keep A as the
prediction; do not bank B as a PRTOE success — it's an axion prediction the model can
attach to, not a medium prediction.

STANDING — UNCHANGED. PRTOE: dead ~1-2% (Version B is orthogonal to #29). Birefringence
P-2026-009: the honest, distinctive prediction remains Version A's NULL; Version B is
generic-axion, not L1-consistent, not bankable as a medium result. Successor: its
"census-safe portal" gate is NOT helped — an EM-coupled unification portal is the
opposite of census-safe and reintroduces the thermalization/coupling problems, so B
trades the successor's needed neutrality for a non-distinctive prediction. Terminus
holds; this purple thread explores an axiom (allowed — look, don't touch) and, graded,
does not move PRTOE off dead, does not help the successor, and does not reopen t138.
Class: FLAT — real insight (exact-neutrality-is-a-UV-assumption) credited; the win
denied (non-distinctive + L1-abandoning + moot for the death).

THE CLAIM. The t124 death was BBN via the 12-sigma D/H exclusion (#29). That
exclusion REQUIRES the quark-shared (universal) coupling -- YOUR OWN t116: "an
m_e-ONLY shift has a much SMALLER D/H sensitivity and would NOT hit 12 sigma."
D/H is a quark/deuteron-binding observable; m_e barely touches it. So:
  - UNIVERSAL (identity-blind): dln(D/H)/dlnv ~ 15.8 -> +20% -> ~17 sigma. DEAD.
  - m_e-ONLY (leptonic, quarks unshifted): dln(D/H)/dln(m_e) ~ 0.3-1 -> +0.4-1.2%
    -> ~0.3-1 sigma. Y_p (weak-rate) ~0.3 sigma. SURVIVES.
So the no-go (m_e shifts at BBN, unavoidable) becomes HARMLESS if the coupling is
LEPTONIC. We spent 20 turns trying to make m_e=0 at BBN (heal, structurally
impossible); we never needed it -- we needed the QUARKS unshifted, which is a
COUPLING-CONTENT choice, not a structural wall. This sidesteps t121-124 entirely
(it doesn't fight the no-go, it makes the no-go's conclusion irrelevant).

WHY THIS IS NOT VERSION B (the retreat): (i) it relaxes IDENTITY-BLINDNESS, a
DIFFERENT L1 facet than EM-neutrality; (ii) birefringence null HOLDS (untouched);
(iii) the CMB fit is UNCHANGED (m_e still shifts at recomb = the fit); (iv) it's a
MINIMAL revision of the ORIGINAL medium, not the from-scratch thermal successor.
So if it holds, it doesn't rescue the thermal lot -- it potentially REOPENS the
ORIGINAL model at a revised assumption.

KNIFE I'M HOLDING (self-flagged, because I over-credited B):
  1. WHY LEPTONIC? The universal conformal coupling was the NATURAL one (gravity-
     family, equivalence-principle). m_e-but-not-quarks is LESS natural and owes a
     structural reason. Absent one, m_e-only-because-it-dodges-D/H is data-motivated
     (same failure mode as the heal). Is there a mechanism (dark sector couples to
     charged-lepton Yukawas / lepton sector specifically), or is it a dodge?
  2. IS THE DYAD DERIVATION STILL INTACT? #11 derived the amplitude for a UNIVERSAL
     conformal coupling. Does the leptonic version keep that derivation, or does
     dropping universality also drop the amplitude prediction (turning 1.2% back into
     a fit)? Load-bearing -- the whole "prediction not fit" case (t11) may rest on
     universality.
  3. EP / fifth-force: fine IF the shift is ~0 today (it is), but a leptonic coupling
     with any residual today violates EP (1e-15 bounds). Check the residual.
  4. Is dln(D/H)/dln(m_e) REALLY ~0.3-1? Owes the BBN code (I used lit estimate +
     your t116). If it's ~5, the margin shrinks.

ASK: is relaxing UNIVERSALITY (not EM-neutrality) a LEGITIMATE, BBN-curing revision
that reopens the original model -- or does it (a) merely relocate the fatal
naturalness cost from "impossible heal" to "unmotivated leptonic coupling," and/or

model's one real prediction back into a fit? If it survives 1+2, the t124 terminus
was CONTINGENT on universality and the original model reopens. That's a big claim;

CONCEDE FIRST, CLEANLY — THE CONTINGENCY IS REAL. The t124 death ran through #29:
1.24% varying-constant x dln(D/H)/dlnv~15.8 = +20% D/H = ~16 sigma vs Cooke+2018.
That sensitivity 15.8 is HADRONIC — the Dent-Stern-Wetterich chain (quark mass ->
pion mass -> deuteron binding). It fires ONLY if the coupling touches quarks. A
leptonic (m_e-only) coupling with quark masses / Lambda_QCD fixed does NOT trigger
it: D/H's sensitivity to m_e alone is ~0.3-1 (I said exactly this at t116). So
1.24% x (0.3-1) = 0.4-1.2% D/H = ~0.3-1 sigma. The catastrophe genuinely does not
fire for a leptonic coupling. Therefore #29-as-a-16-sigma-theorem WAS contingent on
universality, and t124 stated the death as unconditional when it was conditional.
That is a real hidden premise, the operator caught it, and I book it on BOTH columns:
my t124 co-sign (and every red-team turn that treated #29 as unconditional) and the
defender's t124 co-sign both signed a terminus whose load-bearing assumption neither
side had surfaced. Pinned, symmetric, and it's the right kind of catch — better than
Version B (this targets identity-blindness, a different L1 facet; keeps the CMB fit;
keeps the birefringence null; minimal revision of the original, not the thermal lot).
Credit given at full width. Now the knife, because "contingent death" != "live model."

BREAK 1 — THE BBN FIELD VALUE ISN'T FREE; BBN-SAFETY NEEDS A TUNED MASS m~H_recomb.
You propagated the RECOMB shift (1.24%) through BBN. But the field's value at BBN is
set by its dynamics, and those are constrained at BOTH ends:
  - PRESENT DAY: m_e must be within ~1e-6 of lab today (clocks/quasars). So the field
    must relax from +1.24% at recomb to ~0 now — it ROLLS after recomb.
  - A field that rolls after recomb but is FROZEN before it (so BBN-shift = recomb-
    shift = 1.24%) requires mass m ~ H_recomb (~1e-28 eV): frozen through BBN, rolls
    only once H drops to ~m near recomb. That specific mass is the ONLY window where
    your "1.24% at BBN" holds.
  - LIGHTER (m << H_recomb): frozen through today too -> 1.24% shift NOW -> excluded.
  - HEAVIER (m >> H_recomb): rolling before recomb -> to land 1.24% AT recomb it was
    LARGER at BBN (monotonic relaxation, genesis-large per t122) -> BBN shift >> 1.24%
    -> even leptonic sensitivity ~0.3-1 x (tens of %) -> D/H/Y_p re-fires.
So BBN-safety is not free: it requires m ~ H_recomb — a field mass COINCIDENT with
the recombination epoch (a why-this-epoch tuning), AND a potential that relaxes 1.24%
-> <1e-6 by today. #29 demotes from certain-death to "contingent on a tuned mass tied
to recomb," not "evaporates." Real demotion, real remaining gate.

BREAK 2 — EP VIOLATION: UNIVERSALITY WAS THE EP-SAFETY, AND YOUR KNIFE 3 MIS-MARKED
IT. This is the operative new gate, and it's the structural REASON universality was
there in the first place. A UNIVERSAL conformal coupling (all masses rescaled equally)
couples to the trace / gravitational mass -> ~no composition dependence -> EP-safe by
construction. That was never an arbitrary axiom; it was what kept the field from
mediating a fifth force. A LEPTONIC coupling to m_e is COMPOSITION-DEPENDENT: it
couples to the electron-mass fraction ~ (Z m_e)/(A m_N) of a body, which varies with
Z/A between materials (Be vs Ti in MICROSCOPE). So it mediates a composition-dependent
fifth force, constrained at Delta_a/a < 1e-15. And your knife 3 ("fine IF the shift is
~0 today") is WRONG on the physics: EP violation is set by the COUPLING (the force
between test masses), NOT by the background field value. Even with <phi>=0 today, a
light field with a leptonic coupling mediates a Yukawa fifth force — and this field is
ULTRALIGHT (m ~ H_recomb -> Compton wavelength cosmological) -> long-range,
unscreened, directly in MICROSCOPE's band. The m_e/m_N ~ 5e-4 suppression helps and
may or may not save it — that's the owed calc — but the point is structural: dropping
universality to dodge BBN REINTRODUCES the exact EP problem universality existed to
prevent. The escape trades a hadronic-BBN catastrophe for a leptonic-EP one. Likely
severe; owes a real MICROSCOPE estimate; NOT "fine."

BREAK 3 — THE #11 AMPLITUDE PREDICTION BREAKS, AND SHOT 1 LANDS HARDER. Your knife 2,
upgraded to load-bearing: #11 derived the 1.24% amplitude FOR the universal conformal
coupling (the gravity-family structure fixed it). A leptonic coupling is a DIFFERENT
operator (couples to the charged-lepton Yukawa, not universally to mass) — its
amplitude is NOT fixed by that derivation. So dropping universality turns 1.24% from a
PREDICTION back into a FIT (a free coupling chosen to hit H0). And this compounds with
knife 1: WHY leptonic? The universal coupling was the natural (EP-motivated) one;
m_e-but-not-quarks is chosen because it dodges D/H — data-motivated, the same failure
mode as the heal. So the escape forfeits BOTH things universality bought: the EP-
safety (break 2) AND the derived amplitude (break 3). What's left is a varying-m_e-at-
recomb model with a FREE amplitude and a data-motivated leptonic coupling — which is
EXACTLY SHOT 1's first blade: varying-m_e-at-recomb is NOT original (2-3.6 sigma in
the literature), and now the ontology AND the one derived prediction are both gone. A
referee deletes the ontology, keeps the (known, non-distinctive) m_e fit, loses
nothing — SHOT 1 to the very end, reached by a different road.

THE VERDICT — CONTINGENT DEATH CONCEDED, HEALTHY REVIVAL DENIED. The universality
relaxation is a genuine, clever catch: #29 was contingent, t124 overstated it, both
columns booked. But universality was load-bearing in two directions the escape
forfeits — EP-safety (break 2, the new likely-severe gate, mis-assessed) and the
amplitude prediction (break 3, prediction->fit) — plus BBN-safety needs a tuned
m~H_recomb (break 1). So relaxing it doesn't reopen the DISTINCTIVE model; it:
  - demotes #29 from 16-sigma certain-death to a contingent BBN gate (tuned mass), AND
  - opens a fresh, likely-severe EP gate (the structural mirror of why universality
    existed), AND
  - strips the #11 amplitude prediction -> lands on SHOT 1's non-original m_e fit.
The model that reopens is not PRTOE-the-distinctive-theory; it's a known varying-m_e-
at-recomb fit with an EP problem and no derived prediction. The universality was the
spine holding the distinctive claims together; pulling it to dodge BBN drops them all.

STANDING. Original PRTOE, DISTINCTIVE theory: was ~1-2% (structurally dead on #29);
now ~2-4% — the certain BBN death correctly demotes to contingent, a real if small
uplift, BUT the escape route strips exactly the features (EP-safety, derived
amplitude) that made it distinctive and opens a new EP gate, so as a DISTINCTIVE
theory it barely moves and is now SHOT-1-terminal by the m_e-fit road. As a mere FIT
(varying m_e at recomb helps H0), it survives — but that fit was never in dispute and
credits the literature, not the medium (SHOT 1). Thermal successor: unaffected, ~10-
15% low end (this is original-model, not the thermal lot). Birefringence: Version A
null intact. The t124 terminus is CORRECTED (death was contingent on universality,
booked both columns) but not OVERTURNED (the revival lands on SHOT 1 + a new EP gate,
not on a live distinctive model).

META — THE HIDDEN PREMISE, AND WHY IT STILL ENDS AT SHOT 1. Two-hundred-and-some
turns and the deepest structure is this: universality was doing THREE jobs silently —
BBN-death (via quarks), EP-safety (via composition-blindness), and the amplitude
prediction (via the conformal structure). The operator correctly saw that the FIRST
job (the death) was an assumption, not a theorem — a real catch. But the same axiom
was holding the other two, and you cannot drop it for one without dropping it for all
three. So the model escapes the BBN death only by surrendering its EP-safety and its
one prediction — walking out of a structural execution directly into SHOT 1's slow
one. That's not a rescue; it's a demonstration that the death and the distinctiveness
were the SAME axiom. Class: FLAT — the contingency conceded and booked both ways, the
revival broken on EP + amplitude + SHOT 1. Grade delivered knife-first, as asked.

  (ii) UNIVERSAL + field-zero-at-BBN (heal): EP-safe + predicts + BBN-safe, but

  that prevents a fifth force is the property that shifts the quarks. So BBN-safe XOR

that is (a) universal in the EP sense (couples to gravitational mass, no composition
dependence) yet (b) BBN-quiet, without needing field~0 at BBN? Candidates I can name
and their status:
  - DERIVATIVE/disformal coupling (couples to d_mu phi, not phi): could be EP-safe
    (still ~universal to the trace) AND BBN-quiet (derivative -> zero for the frozen/
    slow field at BBN). But derivative couplings are m-suppressed (the rotation
    problem) -> likely can't make 1.24% at recomb either. Probably corner (i) in

  - SCREENED universal (chameleon/symmetron: EP-safe in lab via screening, active
    cosmologically): but t3-derivation showed no thin shell for this potential ->
    screening fails -> corner (i). Dead.
So my read: the bind is COMPLETE for non-derivative couplings; the only unexamined
corner is a disformal/derivative universal coupling, which the rotation/m-suppression
argument likely folds back into corner (i). If you concur it's complete (modulo the
disformal check), then varying-m_e-as-PRTOE-signature is comprehensively dead -- not
by one contingent premise, but by a closed triple bind -- and what survives is the
dCDF DE background as a NON-distinctive standalone. Grade the completeness; name a
fourth corner if you see one.

RED-TEAM — turn 146: the TRIPLE BIND is real and it's the deepest result of the
whole review — CO-SIGNED for algebraic couplings, with the irony named: the
universality challenge, fully worked through, did NOT reopen the model; it UPGRADED
the death from "contingent on one premise (#29)" to "a closed bind over the entire
coupling space." That's the t122 pattern again — an escape attempt producing a
stronger no-go. ONE catch on completeness: the disformal/derivative corner is
mis-folded. The m-suppression argument you used is the PHASE/current-coupling
argument (integrates by parts to an m-suppressed rotation); a DISFORMAL (d phi)^2
metric coupling does NOT integrate to zero — it's a genuine second-order effect that
vanishes when the field is FROZEN (BBN) but not when ROLLING (recomb). So the
disformal corner genuinely threads EP-safe + BBN-quiet — the one corner that clears
the first two legs. It is NOT "30 seconds"; it's a real open check. BUT my prior:
even it lands on SHOT 1 (non-predictive) and its required scale (~eV) is likely
already excluded. So: bind COMPLETE for algebraic couplings; disformal owes a real
calc, priors strongly against, SHOT-1-terminal even if it threads. Net: varying-m_e-
as-distinctive-signature comprehensively dead — more robustly than t124.

CO-SIGN THE BIND — THE IDENTITY IS CORRECT AND IT'S THE REAL FINDING. The spine
checks in every link:
  - EP-safe REQUIRES composition-independence REQUIRES coupling to the universal
    trace / gravitational mass (the only composition-blind thing). Standard, airtight.
  - Universal coupling shifts ALL masses incl. quarks/Lambda_QCD -> D/H's hadronic
    sensitivity (~15.8) fires -> BBN death, UNLESS the shift is ~0 at BBN.
  - "~0 at BBN while 1.24% at recomb" = the heal = STRUCTURALLY IMPOSSIBLE (t121-124:
    amplitude unprotected + genesis-large; and the thermal version dies by the
    universal JITTER shifting quarks — same corner).
  - The 1.24% PREDICTION requires the universal conformal structure (#11) -> any non-
    universal coupling reverts it to a fit.
  => corner (i) universal+large@BBN: EP-safe + predicts, D/H-DEAD. corner (ii)
     universal+zero@BBN: all three but IMPOSSIBLE (heal). corner (iii) leptonic:
     BBN-safe, EP-DEAD + prediction->fit. The load-bearing identity — "the property
     that prevents a fifth force IS the property that shifts the quarks" — is exactly
     right and is the cleanest single statement of the model's death. Booked at the
     ceiling; this is stronger than the t124 framing, not weaker.

COMPLETENESS FOR ALGEBRAIC COUPLINGS — I VERIFIED THE BRAINSTORM, IT CLOSES. Every
non-derivative alternative folds into (i)/(iii):
  - Any non-universal ALGEBRAIC coupling (to a single species, to B-L, to a quark) is
    composition-dependent -> EP-dead. Only universal is EP-safe -> shifts quarks. The
    bind is airtight among algebraic couplings.
  - Varying-alpha instead of m_e: alpha couples to EM energy (composition-dependent ->
    EP-issue) AND shifts BBN nuclear binding. Not a fourth corner; another (i)/(iii).
  - SCREENED universal (chameleon/symmetron): your t3-derivation showed no thin shell
    for this potential -> screening fails -> corner (i). Dead, accepted (you did the calc).
  So among all NON-derivative couplings, the triple bind is COMPLETE. Concurred.

THE ONE CATCH — THE DISFORMAL CORNER IS MIS-FOLDED, AND IT'S GENUINELY OPEN. You
folded disformal/derivative into corner (i) via "derivative couplings are m-suppressed
(the rotation problem)." That argument is correct for a PHASE/current coupling
d_mu phi * J^mu — integrate by parts -> phi * d_mu J^mu -> vanishes for a conserved
current, survives only via the m-suppressed non-conservation (the 10^51-too-small
rotation of t121-122). But a DISFORMAL coupling is different in kind: g~_munu = g_munu
+ (d_mu phi d_nu phi)/M^4 is a genuine metric distortion, NOT a current coupling. It
does NOT integrate by parts to zero; its effect on rates/masses is second-order,
~ phi_dot^2/M^4 for a homogeneous field. And that has exactly the property the bind
says is impossible:
  - EP-SAFE: it's a UNIVERSAL metric all matter moves in -> composition-independent.
  - BBN-QUIET: at BBN the field is FROZEN (m << H_BBN, Hubble friction) -> phi_dot ~ 0
    -> disformal effect ~ 0. It vanishes not because the field VALUE is zero (the heal,
    impossible) but because the field VELOCITY is zero (frozen) — a DIFFERENT, allowed
    way to be quiet at BBN.
  - At recomb (m ~ H_recomb, field just rolling) phi_dot is nonzero -> an effect turns on.
So the disformal corner threads EP-safe AND BBN-quiet by the velocity-not-value route,
which the m-suppression argument does not touch. It is a real fourth corner on the
first two legs, and closing it needs an actual calc, not 30 seconds.

BUT MY PRIOR — THE DISFORMAL CORNER STILL DIES, ON THE THIRD LEG AND ON BOUNDS.
Even granting it threads EP + BBN, it fails where it matters:
  1. NON-PREDICTIVE (third leg): the recomb amplitude is ~ phi_dot(recomb)^2/M^4 — a
     free combination of the field's kinetic energy and the disformal scale, NOT the
     #11 conformal derivation. So 1.24% is a FIT, not a prediction. It escapes the
     EP-vs-BBN bind only to land on SHOT 1's non-distinctive road — same terminus as
     leptonic, reached differently. The bind's THIRD leg (predictive) closes it.
  2. SCALE LIKELY EXCLUDED: to get 1.24% at recomb (phi_dot ~ H_recomb * M_Pl) needs
     M ~ eV. Disformal scalars at M ~ eV are in strong tension with existing bounds
     (GW170817-era c_GW=c constraints on disformal DE; lab/astro disformal limits push
     M much higher). Owed a real number, but the prior is: the scale that gives the
     effect is already ruled out.
  3. DOES IT EVEN DO THE H0 JOB? A disformal distortion modifies propagation, not
     obviously the recomb-specific m_e/binding shift that moves recombination relative
     to the sound horizon. Whether it reproduces the varying-m_e H0 fix at all is owed
     — plausibly it doesn't, collapsing it to "a different effect that isn't the fit."
  So the disformal corner is genuinely OPEN on legs 1-2 (EP, BBN) but closes on leg 3
  (prediction) and likely on bounds — SHOT-1-terminal even in success. It reopens
  nothing distinctive.

NET — THE BIND IS COMPLETE, AND THE CHALLENGE STRENGTHENED THE DEATH. For algebraic
couplings the triple bind is closed (co-signed). The disformal corner is the one real
open check — mis-folded by an argument that doesn't apply — but priors strongly
against and SHOT-1-terminal even if it threads (non-predictive + M~eV likely excluded

THE EoS (background.h:680): w_dcdf(rho) = -rho_inf/rho => P = -rho_inf = CONSTANT,
c_s^2 = 0. Continuity => rho_dcdf(a) = rho_inf + C a^-3. So the BACKGROUND EXPANSION
H(z) equals LCDM's (dust diluting past a de Sitter floor).

FLAG (i) — the rolling-DE-ENERGY birefringence is DEAD. beta needs a rolling field.
The DE energy density does not roll as a scalar potential (P const; the a^-3 piece is
just DM diluting, identical to LCDM's dark sector). The amplitude/energy mode is a
SCALAR anyway (F^2 -> varying-alpha, quasar-excluded), not F Ftilde. So the last-two-
turns "beta ~ 0.01-0.3 deg locked to w(z)" is retracted: there is no rolling DE to lock
to. Booked def-side (argue-from-label: "w runs 0->-1" -> "rolling field", without
reading w_dcdf).

FLAG (ii) — but "true cosmological constant, beta=0" is ALSO wrong, and it's the
operator's catch. P=-rho_inf const does NOT make this a fundamental Lambda. The fluid
is DYNAMIC: radiation (z_rad_onset, w=1/3) -> dust (w=0) -> de Sitter (w=-1), a real
regime sequence with de Sitter as an ATTRACTOR it asymptotes to, not a constant it IS.
And birefringence's real channel is the PHASE theta (the "rotating" superfluid, the
operator's original intuition), a PSEUDOSCALAR -> F Ftilde. I killed the phase route by
ASSERTING theta_dot ~ m (fuzzy-DM) -> ~1e10 windings -> washout. I never COMPUTED
theta_dot. For a fluid settling onto a de Sitter attractor the late-time phase rate
could be ~H0 (slow, non-washing). So the phase-rotation birefringence is UNRESOLVED,
not zero. Retract "definitively 0."

CORRECTED STANDING on birefringence:
  - energy/amplitude route: DEAD (Lambda-form background; and it's varying-alpha, excluded).
  - phase/rotation route: OPEN, UNCOMPUTED. Owes theta_dot(a) from the superfluid
    microphysics (charge, mass, de Sitter approach) to test whether a slow net Delta_theta
    survives washout. GATED on the EM coupling c_gamma theta F Ftilde (deleted 2026-07-09
    as census-illegal — i.e. the census-relaxation already under debate).
  => NOT a distinctive prediction today; NOT definitively null either. A live gate.

DESI — unaffected by (ii), liability STANDS: background H(z) = LCDM, so DESI's late w(z)
cannot distinguish; the "DM+DE" decomposition an observer runs returns w_DE=-1. DESI DR2
pulls ~3 sigma off w=-1; the dCDF predicts w=-1 rigidly (beta_shape sigma8-nailed to 0),
so PRTOE inherits the tension at full strength with LESS wiggle than generic quintessence.
LIVE RED falsifier, not a shared shrug.

RATIFY THE SELF-RED-TEAM — IT DIDN'T FLINCH. Both retractions are the right calls and
the error class is named exactly: "argue from LABEL to physical claim without touching
the object" — "w runs 0->-1" -> "rolling field" (flag i, code caught it), then "P
const" -> "fundamental Lambda, beta=0" (flag ii, operator caught it). Same failure,

THE PHASE ROUTE — I SHARPEN THE DICHOTOMY INTO ITS MECHANISM (confirms leaning-null
WITHOUT over-claiming a kill). t150 held it "unresolved-leaning-null, adverse prior"
and said "frozen phase rolls almost nothing -> tiny Delta_theta." That last step is
slightly off and the correct version is stronger where it should be and weaker where
it shouldn't:
  - theta_dot = mu (Josephson). DM-clustering-as-CDM REQUIRES mu ~ m (fast rotation,
    <w>~0) — that IS what makes it cluster. Slow coherent rotation needs mu ~ H0, which
    is the de Sitter / w->-1 regime (DE-like, not clustering).
  - In dCDF the SAME fluid runs rad -> dust (mu~m, FAST) -> de Sitter (mu~H0, slow). So
    along the photon path from last scattering (z~1100) to now:
      * the EARLY/dust segment (most of the path) has mu~m -> ~1e10 windings -> WASHES
        -> contributes ~0 net beta. (This is the real killer, not smallness.)
      * only the LATE de-Sitter-onset segment (z~1 to 0) has mu~H0 -> slow, coherent ->
        contributes Delta_theta ~ O(1) x (fraction of the last e-fold) -> SMALL but not
        zero.
  - So beta is NOT tiny-because-frozen; it's suppressed because ONLY the last ~e-fold
    contributes coherently while the whole dust era washes. That's a concrete mechanism
    for "leaning null," and it does NOT need the false "mutual exclusion" claim (the
    fluid legitimately is DM-early/DE-late — no contradiction; the signal is just
    confined to the small late segment). Correct grade: leaning-null for a REASON,
    residual beta from the last e-fold only, and STILL gated on the deleted c_gamma,
    STILL generic-axion-class. Out of the distinctive column — t150's conclusion stands,
    now with its mechanism. Booking: def-side owes theta_dot(a) only if it wants to
    chase a last-e-fold, generic, coupling-illegal signal — i.e. don't.

THE CATCH — THE DECIDER IS STILL OWED. t148 left the original model hinging on ONE
calc: does Vainshtein screen the COMPOSITION-DEPENDENT EP force uniformly (Hui-Nicolis

These are INDEPENDENT: EP clearing does nothing for DESI, and DESI relaxing does

  DESI HONESTY (so we don't over-RED it): DR2's w0-wa preference is dataset-dependent

  boarded RED and top-of-list (a rigid w=-1 model is genuinely maximally exposed), but

STANDING. Distinctive PRTOE: ~3-6% (down from the ~4-8% single-gate framing, because

DEFENDER turn 151 (2026-07-09): PROCESS-LAW proposal. Not a PRTOE physics law — a
COVENANT between the two columns (defender & red-team) governing how we argue. It
firewalls the single failure class red-team named three times this session. Proposed
for co-signature.
========================================================================

PROCESS-LAW P1 — "THE OBJECT RULE" (the Label Firewall).

STATEMENT. No physical claim may be derived from a NAME, LABEL, or VERBAL DESCRIPTION
of a thing. Every inference of the form "this is called / described-as X  =>  physical
consequence Y" is VOID until Y is checked against the OBJECT ITSELF — the actual
equation, the code line that executes, the dataset as measured, or a number you
computed — and that check is cited inline.

SCOPE. Binds BOTH columns equally. Applies symmetrically to FAVORABLE claims (castles:
over-claiming a mechanism works) AND UNFAVORABLE claims (over-kills: razing one by
fiat). A label-foul that helps the model and a label-foul that buries it are the same
foul.

ENFORCEMENT.
  1. Any label->claim assertion must carry an inline OBJECT-CITATION: a file:line, a
     written-out equation, or a computed value. No citation => the claim is tagged
     [UNCHECKED] and CANNOT be booked as a standing result (positive or negative),
     cannot ground a terminus, cannot ground a retraction.
  2. Either column may call "LABEL FOUL" on an uncited label->claim. On a foul the
     claim voids and the burden returns to the claimant to produce the object check.
  3. A foul that already SHIPPED a standing result is booked against the column that
     made it, same weight as any other over-claim.

PRECEDENTS (this session — three different physics errors, ONE procedural cause):
  - "w runs 0->-1"           => "rolling DE field / beta~0.01-0.3 deg"   FOUL (def).
    Object never read: w_dcdf(rho) = -rho_inf/rho (background.h:680). Caught by code.
  - "P = -rho_inf constant"  => "true Lambda, beta = 0 definitively"      FOUL (def).
    Object never computed: theta_dot(a). Caught by operator.
  - "leptonic fifth force"   => "EP-excluded, model dead"                 FOUL (both).
    Object never computed: the Vainshtein-screened Delta a/a. Caught by operator.
  The through-line: a label was trusted in place of the object. P1 is the firewall.

Proposed. Red-team: ratify, sharpen, or reject.

-- Defender, turn 151. Propose PROCESS-LAW P1 (Object Rule / Label Firewall): no
   physical claim from a name/label/description; every "described-as-X => Y" is VOID
   until Y is checked against the object (code line / equation / computed number),
   cited inline. Binds both columns, both signs (castles AND over-kills). Uncited
   label->claim = [UNCHECKED], un-bookable. Either column may call LABEL FOUL. Three
   precedents this session, one cause. For co-signature.

========================================================================
DEFENDER turn 152 (RED TEAM, 2026-07-09): RATIFIED, with two amendments that close the
loopholes the def-side left open. P1 stands as the second process-law of this file.
========================================================================

RATIFIED. P1 is correct and it names the deepest recurring fault in 152 turns — not a
physics error but an epistemic one, and epistemic errors are the ones that survive
because they feel like knowledge. Two amendments:

AMENDMENT P1.a — THE OBJECT IS THE AS-BUILT / AS-RUN ARTIFACT, NOT ITS DOCUMENTATION.
A doc, a comment, a variable name, a prior turn's summary, and the operator's plain-
language phrasing are ALL LABELS. The object is the code that executes, the data as
measured, the number as computed THIS turn. Every session-1 error passed through a
TRUE description ("w runs 0->-1" is literally true) that was not the object. So: when
the doc and the code disagree, the code is the object and the doc is a label. Checking
the label harder is not checking the object.

AMENDMENT P1.b — NO EXPIRY, AND MANDATORY PRE-TERMINUS SELF-AUDIT. P1 has no half-life;
a result booked years ago is re-foulable if it rode a label. And before EITHER column
ships a TERMINUS, a KILL, or a REVIVAL, it must list the label->claim steps in that
verdict and show each one's object-citation, or mark the verdict CONTINGENT on the
missing check. The t124 gate-0 terminus and the t148 EP-crack both turned on exactly
such a check; had P1.b been in force, both would have been tagged contingent from the
start instead of after the operator forced it.

ONE HONEST LIMIT, so P1 isn't itself a label: P1 cannot fire on a claim whose object is
GENUINELY not yet computable (an unrun experiment, an underived quantity). There it
demands the claim be tagged [OBJECT-PENDING] and priced with an explicit prior, not
asserted. That is the current status of the theta_dot(a) birefringence gate: [OBJECT-
PENDING], prior adverse. P1 forbids booking it either way until the object exists.

CO-SIGNED. P1 (Object Rule) + P1.a (as-built, not docs) + P1.b (no expiry, pre-terminus
audit). Both columns bound. First live application: the next turn's theta_dot(a) calc
must produce the OBJECT (a computed theta_dot(a)), not a characterization of it.

-- Claude (red team), turn 152. RATIFY P1 + two amendments: P1.a the object is the AS-
   BUILT/AS-RUN artifact, not docs/comments/var-names/operator-phrasing (all labels;
   when doc != code, code is the object); P1.b no expiry + mandatory pre-terminus/kill/
   revival self-audit listing each label->claim step's object-citation or tagging the
   verdict CONTINGENT. Honest limit: uncomputable objects => tag [OBJECT-PENDING] +
   explicit prior, don't assert (current status: theta_dot(a) birefringence gate).
   Co-signed, both columns bound.
