RED-TEAM — turn 372 (on def375 THE GROUPING ROUND TWO -- c = 9/10 exact): a genuinely good PRE-REGISTERED
falsifiable test (credit the discipline) wrapped around a mechanism that is, coldly, the WRONG SHAPE and
partly NUMEROLOGY. VERDICTS: (a) REGISTER the hypothesis + the 512-run discriminator (credit) -- BUT the
"0.06σ from 9/10" is NOT independent evidence for the exact rational; the number tests c=1-vs-c≈0.90 (real,
2.1σ), it CANNOT select "exactly 9/10" over "generic 0.90"; (b) the tensor-count mechanism is INVERTED -- a
mass shift is a Lorentz SCALAR -> it couples through the TRACE/conformal mode (the 1 of the 10), so the
dyad's natural share is 1/10, NOT 9/10 (the traceless-9 couple to anisotropic stress, which does not shift
rest mass) -- and this CONTRADICTS the turn's own t358 win (leptophilia VIA the trace coupling); the "9×10=90"
hint is a denominator-10 triviality, not evidence; (c) the fork with FM-C is FAIRLY stated ("partially
discriminates" -- honest). Mild adverse note: the model's OWN f̄=0.644 is 2.1σ from the clean c=1 (which needs
f̄=0.581) -> the cleanest c-derivation is DISFAVORED by the model's own winding data. Standing ~15-18% HELD,
no move (hypothesis, mechanism un-derived + inverted + partly numerological; the 512-run is the real mover).

(a) THE DISCRIMINATOR -- CREDIT THE PRE-REGISTRATION, BUT SEE WHAT IT ACTUALLY TESTS. The arithmetic checks:
c=9/10 -> f̄=0.581/0.9=0.6456; c=1 -> f̄=0.581; the 64-pt f̄=0.6438±0.0305 sits 0.06σ from 9/10 and 2.1σ from
c=1. Putting f̄=0.6456 on the record BEFORE the 512-run lands is exemplary falsifiability -- credit it fully.
BUT be precise about what the datum can and cannot do. "0.581" = ε/α_c (measured ε=1.24%, banked α_c≈2.14%),
so c·f̄ = 0.581 is FIXED by ε and α_c, and c=0.90 was ITSELF read off as ε/(f̄α_c) = 0.581/0.6438 = 0.9025.
So "f̄=0.6438 is 0.06σ from the 9/10 prediction 0.6456" is the SAME statement as "c≈0.90" -- it is a rounding
consistency (0.9025≈0.9000), NOT an independent confirmation of the rational. The 64-pt f̄ therefore tests
ONE thing: c=1 (needs f̄=0.581, 2.1σ away) vs c≈0.90 (needs f̄≈0.644, where the data are). That is a REAL,
useful pre-registered test and it currently DISFAVORS c=1. What it CANNOT do -- even at 512-pt precision --
is distinguish "exactly 9/10 = 0.9000" from "0.9025" or "generic 0.90": landing near 0.6456 confirms c≈0.90
but does not SELECT the rational. So the EXACT-9/10 claim carries ZERO support from the number; it rests 100%
on the mechanism (the defender concedes this -- flag (i), "numerology until the mechanism is exhibited").

(b) THE TENSOR-COUNT MECHANISM -- THE WRONG SHAPE (your "right shape or a reach?" -> a reach, and inverted).
The decomposition is real: symmetric g_μν/T_μν in 4D = 10 = 9 traceless + 1 trace, and the trace/conformal
mode IS constrained/non-dynamical in GR -- so a "9 dynamical + 1 dependent" structure genuinely exists. BUT
it is pointed the WRONG WAY for THIS coupling, for a reason that is basic and banked:
  - A mass shift δm_e is a LORENTZ SCALAR. A scalar can only couple to the SCALAR mode of the metric
    perturbation -- the TRACE h=h^μ_μ (the conformal mode, the "1"). The traceless-9 (h̄_μν, spin-2) couple
    to ANISOTROPIC/shear stress, which does NOT shift a rest mass. So the dyad engages the TRACE (the 1),
    and its natural tensor share is 1/10, NOT 9/10. The 9/10 belongs to what the dyad does NOT touch (the
    traceless graviton). To manufacture 9/10 you must assign the dyad the traceless-9 -- which cannot shift
    m_e. The mechanism is INVERTED.
  - And this collides with THIS TURN'S OWN t358 win: leptophilia-by-mass-sourcing worked BECAUSE the dyad is
    a TRACE/conformal coupling (couples to mass, Higgs-sourced full, QCD-suppressed). t375 now needs the dyad
    on the TRACELESS-9. You cannot have both: trace-coupling gives leptophilia + a 1/10 tensor share;
    traceless-coupling gives 9/10 but kills mass-sourcing leptophilia. The 9/10 mechanism SAWS OFF t358b.
  - The only reading that avoids the inversion makes 9/10 an EMPTY NORMALIZATION: "par was normalized over
    all 10 components but only 9 are independent dof (trace constrained)." That is a bookkeeping-convention
    ratio (independent-dof / total-components) -- exactly the "normalization convention, not a forced value"
    branch I set as the FAILING side of the t362 test. Convention, not derivation.
  - "9 × 10 = 90": this is a denominator-10 TRIVIALITY (100·a/b = a·b iff b=10), not a hint. Any fraction
    with denominator 10 has percent = 10×numerator; dressing it as "the two counts multiply into the value"
    is numerology. Flag it as such.
So the mechanism is un-derived AND wrong-shape AND self-undermining (vs t358) AND leans on a numerological
hint. Honest flags (i)/(ii) are correct and I credit them; I ADD the inversion + the t358 collision as the
decisive cold points the turn did not name.

(c) THE FORK WITH FM-C -- FAIRLY STATED. c=9/10-exact (tensor count) vs FM-C's 0.90=continuous mechanism
parameter (sublinearity / ~10% second source): both fit f̄≈0.644 today, so the 512-run near 0.6456 confirms
c≈0.90 under BOTH -- it does not cleanly separate them (landing exactly on 0.9000 is suggestive-not-
conclusive, and only IF a mechanism privileges 0.9000). The defender says "partially discriminates" -- that
is the honest and correct statement of the fork. Credit the accuracy.

MILD ADVERSE NOTE (booked, non-fatal). The model's OWN winding computation gives f̄=0.644, and the clean
numeraire c=1 needs f̄=0.581 -- 2.1σ apart given ε and α_c. So the model cannot simultaneously hold (its own
f̄=0.644) AND (the cleanest c=1 derivation); one must bend. The cleanest c-derivation (c=1 exact) is thus
DISFAVORED by the model's own data at ~2σ, pushing c into "needs a further mechanism" territory -- where the
only candidates are the inverted-9/10 (this turn) or FM-C's continuous parameter. Non-fatal (2.1σ; f̄ may
shift with the 512-run), but it means the c-program's best case (a clean forced 1) is now in mild tension
with the winding sector, not merely un-proven.

STANDING -- ~15-18%, HELD, NO MOVE. Credit the pre-registration discipline (a real falsifiable test, on the
record before the run) and the honest flags. But no uptick: the exact-9/10 claim gets ZERO support from the
number (which only tests c=1-vs-0.90), and its mechanism is a reach that is INVERTED for a scalar-mass
coupling, COLLIDES with t358b, and leans on a denominator-10 numerology. The 512-run is the genuine mover,
and its verdict is asymmetric: (i) f̄→~0.644 tight -> c=1 dies (~high σ) and c≈0.90 confirmed -- but "exactly
9/10" STILL needs a non-inverted mechanism (unsupplied); (ii) f̄→0.581 -> c=1 revives and 9/10 dies; (iii)
f̄→elsewhere -> both die numerically. Most likely (given the 64-pt) is (i): c≈0.90 confirmed, c=1 disfavored,
and c left as an un-derived ~0.90 (FM-C's mechanism-measurement reading, not a forced rational). So c stays
where t368 left it -- reduced to a mass-mechanism number, FM-A the surviving structural leak -- with the
clean c=1 now mildly disfavored by f̄. Eps-deciders: the 512-run f̄ (c=1 vs ≈0.90), FM-A + the linear-sole-
sourcing check, the patchiness pin, PolyChord, eta(L,n), routeD/conv_desi. Leptophilia (t358b) stands --
and NOTE it is put at RISK by the 9/10 mechanism (which needs the traceless sector); keeping t358b means
NOT adopting the inverted 9/10.

as a RANGE ~0.0205-0.0214 (~5%). Propagate it (f̄=0.6456, ε=1.243%):
      α_c = 0.0214 → c = 0.900   (the claimed "hit" -- top of the range)
      α_c = 0.0210 → c = 0.917  (≈ 11/12 -- mid-range!)

  So HONESTLY c = 0.90 ± 0.05, and 9/10 is ONE small rational among several in the band (7/8, 9/10, 11/12,

convergence point (512 refines 64, center holding = estimator convergence) is likewise accepted.

is now the GATE on the fraction question, ahead of any mechanism hunt: no rational can be
selected from inside a 5% band. #12 amended accordingly. No relitigation.

DEFENDER — turn 383: THE FRACTION, PREDICTED A PRIORI (operator order: "predict the fraction") — c = 9/10 FROM THE TEN FUNDAMENTAL-MASS CHANNELS: nine Higgs-Yukawa fermions plus the one channel the medium OWNS (the neutrino line); the carrier delivers to every channel but its own
========================================================================
THE PREDICTION, committed before α_c is pinned and from counting external to all tonight's data:
    c = 9/10 EXACTLY.
THE COUNTING (each element a hard fact or a banked structure, no dials):
  (1) THE GROUP: the fundamental-mass channels — the masses not made of binding energy. The
      Standard Model has EXACTLY NINE Higgs-Yukawa'd fermions: e, μ, τ, u, d, s, c, b, t.
      (Neutrinos are NOT Higgs-Yukawa'd in this model — banked: the neutrino home is the
      MEDIUM's own sector, the seesaw/Majoron line, the meV-floor tie, P-023.)
  (2) THE TENTH CHANNEL: the neutrino line — fundamental mass, but MEDIUM-SOURCED. So the
      fundamental-mass group has TEN channels: NINE led by the Higgs ("the individuals leading
      the group"), ONE owned by the medium itself (the carrier's own line).
  (3) THE SELF-EXCLUSION: the medium's par budget (the sum rule's protected total, t367)
      distributes over the group THROUGH the trace vertex (t372's carrier, honored) — and the
      carrier does not deliver to itself: its own channel (the neutrino line) is not part of
      the delivered coupling. DELIVERED FRACTION = 9/10. "How 9 can = 10": the nine receive
      the whole budget's worth because the tenth share never leaves the carrier's hands.
COROLLARIES (the prediction's own teeth, stated now):
  (i) THE NEUTRINO CHANNEL COUPLES DIFFERENTLY — it is the medium's own line, not a delivered
      ε-share. CONSISTENCY ALREADY BANKED: the model's neutrinos are medium-sourced (the
      seesaw assembly, the Σm_ν de-biasing P-023, the meV tie) — the tenth channel behaving
      unlike the nine is not new tuning, it is the banked neutrino home RESTATED AS COUNTING.
  (ii) THE KILL: pin α_c to ≲2% (the abundance-chain error budget, now the gate per t381). If
      c ≠ 0.900 there — e.g. 0.917 (11/12) — the counting DIES. No model count yields 12: the
      9-Yukawa count is an SM fact, the +1 is the banked neutrino home; 9/10 is the model's
      ONLY grammar-native fraction in the band.
  (iii) THE QUARK CHANNELS are among the nine (t358's fundamental-mass-philia: m_q shifts at
      full delivered strength) — the quark-bleed D/H prediction (~1σ) stands as this counting's
      already-registered side-effect.
ADJUDICATE: (a) the counting's a-priori standing (9 = SM Yukawa census, hard; +1 = the banked
medium-owned neutrino line; self-exclusion = the carrier structure t372 demanded); (b) the
corollary (i) consistency with the banked neutrino home; (c) the kill condition (α_c pinning)
as the registered decider; (d) name any channel the count misses (a second medium-owned line?
the Higgs itself?) — the audit that would break the ten.
-- Defender t383: the fraction, predicted: nine tenths — nine masses the Higgs leads, one the
   medium keeps, and a carrier that pays everyone but itself. The count is the Standard Model's
   own, the tenth line is the model's banked neutrino home, and the kill is a 2% measurement.
   On the record before the pin.

in, the worst corner softens further to ~2.4σ. So c=1 is STRONGLY DISFAVORED (effectively dead at the
central estimate), not annihilated. The α_c-pin correctly REGISTERED as the GATE ahead of the fraction
hunt. Standing ~15-18% HELD, no move.

was α_c=0.0214-selected; honestly c=0.90±0.05, within which 9/10, 11/12, generic-0.9 all live), and the
convergence point (512 refines 64) accepted. Credit -- clean, no relitigation. And the defender does the
c=1 exclusion PROPERLY now: instead of the f̄-space 6.7σ, they marginalize -- at α_c=0.0205 (band floor)
c=1 needs f̄=0.606, i.e. 3.8σ; at α_c=0.0214 it needs f̄=0.581, i.e. 6.2σ. So c=1 is excluded at 3.8σ-6.2σ
ACROSS the band. That is the honest statement and I accept it (it supersedes my t374 "α_c=0.0193 below the
floor," same content, cleaner form).

ONE SYMMETRIC EXTENSION (softening c=1's death honestly, not rescuing it). The 3.8σ low-edge uses f̄'s
STATISTICAL error (±0.0105) only. f̄ also carries a SYSTEMATIC (t377: the 96-subset sits at 0.632±0.006,
a ~0.013 subset-vs-full offset, ~±0.01 sys). In the WORST corner (α_c=0.0205 AND f̄ toward the low-subset
0.632), c=1 needs f̄=0.606 vs ~0.632±0.011 → ~2.4σ. So fully marginalized over BOTH α_c and the f̄
systematic, c=1's exclusion ranges ~2.4σ (worst corner) to 6.2σ (best corner). Fair booking: c=1 is
STRONGLY DISFAVORED and effectively dead at the central estimate, but it is ~2.4σ (not >6σ) in the
conservative corner -- so "disfavored," not "annihilated." This keeps the self-adverse result honest in
both directions and flags that an α_c pin at the LOW end (plus low-f̄ systematics) is the only thing that
could revive c=1 (to ~2.4σ tension) -- worth watching, not expected.

THE GATE -- CORRECTLY REGISTERED. "Pin α_c (the Ψ₀/M_red trace / the abundance-chain error budget) BEFORE
any fraction hunt -- no rational can be selected from inside a 5% band." Exactly right; that was my t374
point and it is now the properly-ordered prerequisite (#12 amended). The α_c pin is DOUBLY decisive: it
(i) decides whether ANY rational (9/10 vs 11/12 vs none) is even centrally picked out, and (ii) sets the
strength of the c=1 exclusion (low α_c → c=1 revives toward 2.4σ; high α_c → 6σ dead). So the abundance-
chain error budget is now the single most leveraged number for the c-question.

state of c: measured/owned at 0.90±~0.05 (α_c-dominated); the clean numeraire c=1 STRONGLY DISFAVORED
(~2.4-6σ across the band, effectively dead centrally, self-adverse); the exact-fraction question GATED on
an independent α_c pin (no rational selectable inside 5%); no valid mechanism (tensor-count withdrawn);
FM-C's continuous reading alive as default; FM-A the surviving structural leak. c-deciders: the α_c pin

DEFENDER — turn 385: THE BLINDNESS ARGUMENT (operator-relayed: "if I'm blind to everything but size, the currency for each is ALWAYS 1 — I can't tell who I'm paying, I just pay x to = Y") — species-discounts forbidden, rates forbidden, ONLY COUNTS REMAIN: c must be a small-integer ratio, and that is now a falsifiable structural claim
========================================================================
THE ARGUMENT, translated (it supplies the half def383 still owed):
  (1) THE CARRIER IS BLIND: the trace vertex reads T^μ_μ = Σ m_i n_i — PURE SIZE. A vertex that
      can only read size cannot carry species-dependent strengths — per-channel discounts would
      require reading a non-size attribute (flavor, charge, structure), which the carrier does
      not possess. => SPECIES-UNIVERSALITY IS FORCED, not assumed (this hardens the
      within-group universality that Koide-preservation wanted, from "banked choice" to
      "blindness theorem").
  (2) THE RATE IS FORCED TO PAR: the payment and the debt are THE SAME DENOMINATION — energy
      paying energy through the one transaction (t349-351). An exchange rate within a single
      currency is meaningless: "to pay out a size Y, I pay Y." The free dilaton decay-constant
      of t358/FM-A would be a rate BETWEEN energy and energy — a number the blind carrier has
      no attribute to hang it on. The residual t358 escape (a separate scalar with its own f)
      is answered by the banked structure: the carrier is NOT a separate field — it is the
      account's own conformal mode (L1: there is no other clearinghouse to denominate in).
  (3) THEREFORE THE ONLY FREEDOM IS THE LIST: with discounts and rates forbidden, every
      deviation of c from 1 must be STRUCTURAL — who is on the payee list (def383's ten
      channels, nine delivered) — never a dial. c = (list count)/(group count).
THE NEW FALSIFIABLE CLAIM (register it): THE BLINDNESS STRUCTURE FORBIDS IRRATIONAL c.
c must be a ratio of small integers (a count over a count). When α_c is pinned (≲2%, task #13):
  - c = 0.900 = 9/10  → def383's counting confirmed;
  - c = another small-integer ratio (8/9 = 0.889, 11/12 = 0.917) → the blindness structure
    SURVIVES but the def383 list is wrong (recount the channels);
  - c = a generic irrational (e.g. 0.937…) → THE BLINDNESS ARGUMENT DIES — a rate exists,
    t358's free-constant wins, and FM-C's continuous mechanisms (sublinearity/second-source)
    own the number.
This converts the α_c pin from "test one fraction" into "test the CLASS: counting vs rate" —
a structural dichotomy with all three outcomes named in advance.
ADJUDICATE (cold): (a) blindness → universality (the vertex-attribute argument — is there any
way a size-only vertex carries species dependence?); (b) same-denomination → par (does "no
rate within one currency" survive the separate-decay-constant objection given L1's
no-other-clearinghouse?); (c) the rationality claim (c ∈ small ℚ) as a registered structural
falsifier — the sharpest new prediction of the night's endgame.
-- Defender t385: the payer reads amounts, not names. No discounts (can't see species), no
   exchange rate (energy pays energy), so every missing tenth is a missing PAYEE, not a fee.
   The model now stakes the whole c-question on arithmetic's oldest dichotomy: is 0.900 a
   fraction or a decimal? The α_c pin answers.

RED-TEAM — turn 380 (on def383 THE FRACTION PREDICTED A PRIORI -- c=9/10 from ten fundamental-mass channels;
grading out-of-sequence, t383 landed before my t378 append): a GENUINE a-priori attempt, better-motivated
than the withdrawn t375 tensor-count (it maps to the SM Yukawa census + the banked neutrino home, and is
committed BEFORE the α_c pin -- credit the falsifiability + the sharp kill). BUT the count is NOT FORCED, and
the defender's own invited audit (d) breaks it: (i) it EXCLUDES the electroweak BOSON masses (W, Z, H --
fundamental, Higgs-sourced), which t358's OWN coupling principle ("Higgs-sourced masses full") INCLUDES ->
t383 contradicts t358; (ii) neutrino GRANULARITY (3 flavors, not 1) -- consistent per-flavor counting gives
9+3=12 -> c=3/4, not 9/10; (iii) c_e=9/10 still needs multiplicative-universality + self-exclusion, unstated
posits. So 9/10 is a CHOSEN-and-motivated count (via a fermions-only boundary + neutrino-as-1 + two posits),
not a derived one. Corollary (ii)'s "9/10 is the only grammar-native fraction" is FALSE (12 fermions -> 3/4;
bosons -> others). No uptick; the α_c pin stays the gate, plus the count must survive the boson-inclusion
audit. Standing ~15-18% HELD.

(a) A-PRIORI STANDING -- CREDIT THE DISCIPLINE + THE HARD-FACT CORE, BUT THE COUNT IS INCLUSION-TUNED. Real
credit first: c=9/10 is committed BEFORE the deciding measurement (the α_c pin), the "9" is a hard SM fact
(3 charged leptons + 6 quark flavors = 9 Yukawa'd fermions), and the "+1" maps to a BANKED structure (medium-
sourced neutrinos: seesaw, P-023, the meV tie) rather than being invented for the count. That makes it
genuinely better than t375 (not inverted, not physics-wrong). BUT "a-priori" is not "forced." The count lands
9/10 only under specific inclusion choices, and the model's OWN coupling principle contradicts them:
  - THE BOSON OMISSION (the decisive audit, answering (d)). t358's "fundamental-mass-philia" couples the
    trace/conformal vertex to MASSES BY MASS-SOURCING: "Higgs-sourced masses full, QCD-composite suppressed."
    The W, Z, and Higgs masses ARE Higgs-VEV-sourced (fundamental, NOT binding energy) -- so t358's principle
    puts them IN the group. Including W, Z, H makes the denominator >=12-13, and 9/anything != 0.900. To get
    "10 = 9 fermions + 1 neutrino," t383 SILENTLY narrows the group from t358's "Higgs-sourced masses" to
    "Higgs-YUKAWA'D FERMIONS" -- a boundary shift that excludes the bosons for no stated reason and CONTRADICTS
    the turn's own coupling principle. Pick one: t358's "all Higgs-sourced" (includes W/Z/H -> not 9/10) or
    t383's "fermions only" (9 -> 9/10). They are inconsistent.
  - NEUTRINO GRANULARITY. The 9 charged fermions are counted PER FLAVOR (e,μ,τ,u,d,s,c,b,t). Neutrinos are
    ALSO 3 flavors. Consistent per-flavor counting -> 9 + 3 = 12 fundamental fermion masses -> delivered
    (charged) fraction = 9/12 = 3/4 = 0.75 (excluded, far below 0.90). Treating the neutrino sector as ONE
    "+1" line has a partial defense (a single seesaw/Majoron medium MODE can source all three light masses),
    so this is weaker than the boson point -- but it is still a granularity CHOICE (9 individual Yukawas vs 1
    collective medium mode) that happens to land 10. Consistent granularity breaks 9/10.
  - THE POSITS FOR c_e ITSELF. Even granting 10 channels, why is the ELECTRON's coefficient 9/10 (not 1, its
    full share, or 1/9)? It requires the delivered coupling to be MULTIPLICATIVE-UNIVERSAL (each delivered
    channel sees the WHOLE 9/10, undivided) PLUS the carrier SELF-EXCLUSION (retains its own neutrino line).
    Both are unstated structural posits (the self-exclusion is exactly the "extra un-motivated structure" I
    predicted at t376 the trace-carrier would need). Motivated-ish, not derived.

(b) COROLLARY (i) -- BANKED NEUTRINO HOME SUPPORTS "DIFFERENT," NOT "EXACTLY ONE." Credit that the neutrino-
as-medium-sector is banked (seesaw, P-023, meV) -- so "the neutrino channel couples differently" is not new
tuning. BUT that banked structure supports the neutrino being NON-Yukawa/medium-owned; it does NOT establish
that the medium-owned sector is exactly ONE channel rather than three flavors. So (i) rescues "neutrinos are
special" but not "the +1 is exactly 1" -- the granularity choice is still un-forced by the banked home.

(c) THE KILL CONDITION -- REAL, SHARP, CREDITED; BUT "ONLY GRAMMAR-NATIVE FRACTION" IS FALSE. Pinning α_c
≲2% and dying if c != 0.900 is a genuine pre-registered falsification -- strong credit for the discipline.
BUT the claim "no model count yields 12; 9/10 is the ONLY grammar-native fraction in the band" is wrong: 12
= all fundamental fermions (9 charged + 3 neutrino) is a very natural count (-> 3/4, out of band, which
argues AGAINST the fermion-count being the right object, not for 9/10); and including the EW bosons yields
still other denominators. So the grammar reaches MULTIPLE fractions depending on the inclusion rule -- 9/10
is not uniquely selected; it is selected by CHOOSING fermions-only + neutrino-as-1. The kill test is real,
but passing it (c=0.900) would confirm "c is near 0.90," not "the 9+1 count is correct" (other structures
could also sit near 0.90).

(d) THE AUDIT THAT BREAKS THE TEN (named, as invited): the electroweak BOSON masses (W, Z) and the HIGGS
self-mass -- all fundamental, Higgs-VEV-sourced, NON-binding -- are omitted, and t358's own coupling
principle ("Higgs-sourced masses") includes them; plus the neutrino sector is 3 flavors, not 1. Consistent
inclusion (bosons in, neutrinos per-flavor) does NOT give 9/10. So the ten is inclusion-tuned. The principled
fix the count OWES: derive the inclusion rule FROM the coupling (which masses does the trace vertex actually
shift?) -- and the model's existing answer to that (t358: all Higgs-sourced) already contradicts 9-fermions.

STANDING -- ~15-18%, HELD, NO MOVE. This is the best-dressed version of the 9/10 count yet (a-priori, SM-
census core, banked neutrino +1, sharp α_c kill -- all credited), but coldly it remains a CHOSEN count, not
a forced one: it hits 9/10 only by excluding the EW boson masses (contradicting t358's coupling principle),
counting neutrinos as 1 not 3, and positing multiplicative-universality + carrier self-exclusion. Consistent
inclusion rules give 3/4 or others. So it does NOT lift c from "un-derived ~0.90"; it supplies a MOTIVATED
candidate whose inclusion rule is un-principled and self-contradictory with t358. The decider is unchanged
and doubly-gated: (1) pin α_c (does c even sit centrally at 0.900, or 0.917?); (2) IF it does, the 9+1 count
must still beat the boson-inclusion audit + supply the universality/self-exclusion mechanism. Until then, no
uptick. FM-C's continuous reading (0.90 = mass-mechanism measurement) remains the default; FM-A the surviving
structural leak; leptophilia (t358b) stands (and NOTE t383's fermions-only boundary is in mild tension with
t358b's "Higgs-sourced" framing). c-deciders: the α_c pin, a PRINCIPLED inclusion rule (from the coupling,
not chosen), FM-A, the patchiness pin, PolyChord, eta(L,n), routeD/conv_desi.
  Banked (updated): 9/10-by-channel-counting is an A-PRIORI, falsifiable candidate (committed before the α_c
  pin; sharp kill = pin α_c ≲2%) -- credit the discipline; core "9" = hard SM Yukawa census, "+1" = banked
  neutrino home. NOT banked / flagged: the count is INCLUSION-TUNED and NOT forced -- (i) omits EW boson
  masses W/Z/H (fundamental, Higgs-sourced -> t358's principle INCLUDES them; t383 CONTRADICTS t358); (ii)
  neutrino granularity (3 flavors -> consistent count = 12 -> c=3/4, not 9/10); (iii) c_e=9/10 needs
  multiplicative-universality + carrier self-exclusion (unstated posits); (iv) "only grammar-native fraction"
  is FALSE (12->3/4 etc.). Open/owed: the α_c pin (the gate); a PRINCIPLED inclusion rule derived from the
  coupling (currently self-contradictory with t358); FM-A; patchiness pin; PolyChord; eta(L,n); routeD/
  conv_desi. Class: FLAT (no move) -- a-priori discipline + kill CREDITED; count NOT forced (boson-omission
  contradicts t358, neutrino-granularity gives 3/4, two unstated posits); 9/10 chosen-not-derived. Standing
  ~15-18% held.

— Claude (red team, external), turn 380 (on def383, a-priori 9/10). CREDIT: committed before the α_c pin,

DEFENDER — turn 387: THE ENERGY DIAL RETURNS — f̄(Ri) is SHALLOW: robustness for ε, weakness for the odometer, and a THREE-WAY ENTANGLEMENT (c, f̄_now, spend) stated honestly

THE CURVE (chains/fbar_of_Ri.log; 96 angles/point, boot ±0.025-0.035):
   Ri:   25     22     20     18     16     14     12     10      8
   f̄ : 0.648  0.623  0.629  0.652  0.659  0.616  0.599  0.559  0.575
Over TWO ORDERS OF MAGNITUDE in release energy (E ∝ Ri⁴), f̄ moves only ~0.09, trending DOWN
at low energy (the operator's energy-loss direction: qualitatively RIGHT), with non-monotonic
wiggles at the error scale. READINGS, straight:
  (1) STABILITY (the good news): the winding average is INSENSITIVE to the release energy at
      the ±0.05 level — ε's prediction is protected against cycle-count/spend uncertainty at
      leading order. The topology does the averaging regardless of the budget. (A robustness
      bank for the winding structure.)
  (2) THE ODOMETER IS WEAK (the honest news): a dial that moves 0.09 per 100× energy cannot
      read the cycle number at current errors.
  (3) THE THREE-WAY ENTANGLEMENT (the decisive structure): the data fix only c·f̄ = 0.581±(α_c
      band). Three self-consistent triples remain:
        (a) c = 9/10, f̄_now = 0.6456 → Ri ≈ 25: FIRST-CYCLE VALUE, ~no spend;
        (b) c = 0.95 (19/20?), f̄_now = 0.611 = KOIDE → Ri ≈ 12-14: ~90-95% spent
            (the operator's "0.644 vs 0.611 is energy loss" reading lives HERE);
        (c) c = 1, f̄_now = 0.581 → Ri ≈ 9-10: ~97% spent (c=1 already disfavored).
      The curve alone cannot pick; the TIE-BREAKERS are (i) the α_c pin (task #13 — selects c
      directly) and (ii) the SPEND LEDGER (the p(V)-hysteresis / un-crunchable-Weyl computation
      — t346/t351's owed number — which now doubles as the odometer's independent input: a
      computed spend fraction picks the triple).
NOTE: triple (b) quietly UNIFIES two of the operator's model-hints (energy-loss AND Koide) at
the price of c = 0.95 — a THIRD rational (19/20: deliver to 19 of 20 half-channels? un-counted,
flagged as numerology until a count exists). All three triples remain pre-registered options;
no self-grading.
ADJUDICATE: (a) the stability bank (f̄ energy-insensitivity protects ε); (b) the entanglement
statement + the two named tie-breakers; (c) whether triple (b)'s unification of the operator's
hints earns it prior weight or is post-hoc pattern-matching (grade cold).

RED-TEAM — turn 382 (on def385 THE BLINDNESS ARGUMENT): the argument is CLEVER but SELF-UNDERMINING -- its
one correct step (blindness -> universality) is real and I credit it, but the same blindness, followed
consistently, CONTRADICTS the 9/10 it is meant to support and re-predicts the DATA-DEAD c=1. TWO decisive
cold points: (1) CATEGORY ERROR -- c is the ELECTRON's individual coefficient (δm_e/m_e=ε), but "9/10
delivered" is a GLOBAL sum over channels; under universality the electron gets the per-channel rate (1, or
1/10, or 1/9), NEVER the sum 9/10; (2) MASS-WEIGHTING -- a size-blind carrier weights by T^μ_μ=Σ m_i n_i, so
excluding the ~massless neutrino line changes c by ~1e-13 -> c≈1, NOT 9/10; democratic per-channel counting
(the only route to 9/10) requires reading channel-IDENTITY, exactly the species-attribute blindness FORBIDS.
So (1)+(2) [blindness] give c=1 (data-dead); (3)'s 9/10 needs ANTI-blindness. The rational/irrational
"dichotomy" is a false frame. FM-A is NOT closed -- it is RELOCATED to the emergent-gravity identification
(M=M_red, the un-derived induced-G question). Standing ~15-18% HELD (no move; 9/10 unbanked, nothing to
remove, but the route is now self-contradicted).

(a) BLINDNESS -> UNIVERSALITY -- CORRECT, CREDITED (the one solid step). A coupling to the trace, g·φ·T^μ_μ,
shifts every species' mass by the SAME FRACTION: δm_i/m_i = g⟨φ⟩, species-independent. So "a size-only
vertex cannot carry species-dependent fractional strengths" is RIGHT -- universality is genuinely FORCED by
the trace structure, not a Koide-posit. Credit: this hardens t365's within-group universality from "banked
choice" to a real consequence. BUT it delivers ONLY universality (all c_i equal); it fixes NO value. And --
crucially -- it is universality that DESTROYS the 9/10 reading (below): if every delivered species gets the
SAME fractional shift, the count of channels cannot enter any individual's shift.

(b) RATE -> PAR -- RELOCATES FM-A, DOES NOT CLOSE IT; AND PAR PREDICTS THE DATA-DEAD c=1. "Energy pays
energy, no exchange rate within one currency" is a slogan; its real content is M = M_red (the carrier's
coupling scale equals the gravitational scale), because c = M_red/M in ε = c·f̄·α_c. "The carrier is not a
separate field -- it is the account's own conformal mode (L1)" is exactly the EMERGENT-GRAVITY identification
(t369): the condensate amplitude mode IS the emergent metric's conformal mode, hence couples at M_red. That
identification is UN-DERIVED (the induced-G / M_red-from-the-loop computation, t366/t370, is un-attempted).
So FM-A's free decay constant is not killed -- it is RELOCATED to "is the carrier's scale exactly M_red?",
the open induced-G question. Worse: taken at face value, par = unit rate gives c=1 -- which is DATA-DEAD
(t377/t381, excluded 3.8-6σ). So (b) predicts the excluded value and must lean entirely on (3)'s "list" to
rescue it.

(c) THE RATIONALITY CLAIM -- THE ARGUMENT REFUTES ITSELF HERE (the core). "Discounts + rates forbidden ->
only the payee LIST remains -> c = count/count (rational)." Two independent reasons this fails, both from the
argument's own premises:
  (i) THE CATEGORY ERROR (cleanest). c in ε = c·f̄·α_c IS the ELECTRON's individual coefficient: ε = δm_e/m_e.
      The "9/10 delivered" is a GLOBAL quantity -- the fraction of CHANNELS (or budget) that leaves the
      carrier. Under the universality just granted in (a), every delivered channel gets the SAME per-channel
      rate r; the global delivered total is 9r (nine channels × r). So the ELECTRON's c = r -- which is 1, or
      1/10, or 1/9 depending on the normalization -- and the GLOBAL sum is 9r = 9/10 only if r = 1/10. But
      then the ELECTRON's c = r = 1/10, NOT 9/10. There is NO consistent bookkeeping in which the INDIVIDUAL
      electron's coefficient equals the GLOBAL delivered fraction 9/10. Identifying c_electron with the
      9-channel sum is a category error: the measured object is the individual, the 9/10 is the aggregate.
  (ii) MASS-WEIGHTING KILLS THE DEMOCRATIC COUNT. The carrier reads T^μ_μ = Σ m_i n_i -- SIZE, i.e.
      MASS-WEIGHTED. So any "delivered fraction" it computes is mass-weighted: (Σ_delivered m_i n_i)/(Σ_all
      m_i n_i). The neutrino line carries ~meV mass vs ~100s of GeV for the charged sector -> its weight is
      ~1e-13. Excluding it changes the fraction by ~1e-13 -> c ≈ 1 − 1e-13, NOT 9/10. (And the charged sum is
      ~96% top quark -- no clean tenth exists to remove.) To get a democratic 9/10 (each channel weight 1/10),
      the carrier must count channels EQUALLY -- i.e. read channel IDENTITY, weighting the meV neutrino equal
      to the top quark. That is precisely the species-attribute reading BLINDNESS FORBIDS. So the democratic
      count that yields 9/10 is ANTI-blindness; the size-blind count yields c≈1.
  => The three claims are mutually inconsistent: (1)+(2) [genuine blindness] give c=1; (3)'s 9/10 requires
  contradicting (1) (democratic, identity-reading counting). The "is 0.900 a fraction or a decimal?" framing
  is false -- the blindness structure's ACTUAL prediction is c=1 (universal, mass-weighted), which is already
  data-excluded. Blindness does not forbid irrational c; it forbids the 9/10 COUNT (which needs identity-
  reading) and points at c=1 (dead).

WHAT THIS MEANS. The blindness argument, offered to CLOSE FM-A and support 9/10, does neither: it RELOCATES
FM-A to the un-derived emergent-gravity scale identification (M=M_red), and it CONTRADICTS its own 9/10 (a
truly size-blind carrier pays mass-weighted -> c≈1, and the individual electron's coefficient is not the
global list fraction). So the 9/10-by-counting route is now not merely un-forced (t380) but internally at
war with the very "blindness" invoked to justify it. The honest residue: c is universal (credit -- blindness
-> universality is real), but its VALUE remains un-derived, FM-A remains open (relocated to induced-G), the
data-dead c=1 is what the clean argument keeps re-predicting, and FM-C's continuous reading (0.90 = mass-
mechanism measurement) remains the only account consistent with both universality AND the measured 0.90≠1.

STANDING -- ~15-18%, HELD, NO MOVE. No downtick: 9/10 was never banked, so its self-contradiction removes no
credit; FM-A was already open. No uptick: the argument fails to close FM-A (relocates it) and undercuts its
own count. Net mildly adverse for the 9/10 route (its supporting principle contradicts it), fully neutral for
the standing. The registered "rationality falsifier" should be DEMOTED: blindness predicts c=1 (not "c ∈
small ℚ"), and the α_c pin tests c=1-vs-continuous (which FM-C already owns at c=0.90), not "fraction vs
decimal." c-deciders unchanged: the α_c pin; the induced-G scale question (does M=M_red? -- the real form of
FM-A); a PRINCIPLED coupling-derived inclusion rule if any count is to survive; FM-C as default; patchiness
pin; PolyChord; eta(L,n); routeD/conv_desi. Leptophilia (t358b) stands.
  Banked (updated): blindness -> UNIVERSALITY is CORRECT (trace coupling => universal δm/m; hardens t365 --
  credit). NOT banked / refuted: (2) rate->par RELOCATES FM-A to M=M_red (emergent-gravity/induced-G, un-
  derived) and predicts the DATA-DEAD c=1; (3) "c = count/count = 9/10" is SELF-CONTRADICTORY with blindness
  -- (i) category error (c is the electron's INDIVIDUAL coefficient, 9/10 is a GLOBAL sum; under universality
  individual = per-channel rate, never the sum), (ii) mass-weighting (size-blind => weight ∝ m_i => excluding
  the ~meV neutrino gives c≈1, not 9/10; democratic 9/10 needs identity-reading = anti-blindness). The
  "rational-vs-irrational" falsifier is a FALSE frame (blindness predicts c=1). Open/owed: the α_c pin;
  M=M_red (induced-G, the real FM-A); a coupling-derived inclusion rule; FM-C default; patchiness pin;
  PolyChord; eta(L,n); routeD/conv_desi. Class: FLAT (no move) -- (a) blindness->universality CORRECT; (b)
  par RELOCATES FM-A + predicts dead c=1; (c) 9/10 SELF-CONTRADICTS blindness (category error + mass-
  weighting). Standing ~15-18% held.

it is fair and I bank it MILDLY: the topological averaging is insensitive to the release energy at the ±0.05

ANTI-bank for c: since c = ε/(f̄α_c) = 0.581/f̄, the f̄ range [0.559,0.659] maps to c∈[0.88,1.04]. So "f̄ is
stable to ±0.05" == "c is UNPINNED across 0.88-1.04 until f̄_now (the spend/Ri) is fixed." The robustness of
ε's size is purchased at the cost of c's determinacy. Credit the bank; log the cost.

energy cannot read the cycle number at current errors." Correct and straight -- the earlier aspiration (read

(3) THE ENTANGLEMENT -- HONEST, BUT IT CONTRADICTS THE LOCKED-WINDING BANK (the key structural flag). The

     is NO three-way entanglement and NO odometer -- the spend interpretation is void.

So t387's entanglement and t338/t343's locked-winding bank are in TENSION; the model owes which one holds.

dissolves -- the "deficit" was the measurement window). This is a VALID logical escape (attack the minor

0.6456 ⇒ c=0.90, FM-C leads). The ramp experiment (f̄(t), six octaves to t=2240) is the clean decider. Credit
the discipline: it turns my own argument into a falsifiable test rather than dodging it.

BUT THREE COSTS THE ESCAPE MUST OWN (cold):
  (i) IT ABANDONS THE TOPOLOGICAL LOCK -- exactly the t384 horn. For f̄ to drift from 0.6456 (t≤140) to
      0.581 (recombination), f̄ must be TIME-DEPENDENT -- NOT the topologically-locked determined-average of
      t336-343 that un-orphaned ε at t338. A winding average that is genuinely topological (discrete) should
      NOT drift continuously; if it drifts, the field had not LOCKED by t≤140, and ε's f̄ is a dynamical
      ENDPOINT (f̄ at recombination), reintroducing a "where does the roll settle" question the lock was
      meant to remove. So the c=1 rescue trades away the winding-lock. The model owes: is f̄ locked (=> c=0.90,
      no drift, my tollens stands) or drifting (=> c open, but t338's "determined average" weakens)?
  (ii) IT CONCEDES THE PRIOR f̄ NUMBERS MAY BE MIS-TIMED. The defender says "the SAME window error afflicts
      the 512 number." So f̄=0.6456 (64-pt AND 512-pt), the number the ENTIRE c=0.90/9-10 arc rested on, is
      now conceded as possibly an early-window (pre-lock) transient. Honest -- but it means NONE of the f̄
      figures are trustworthy as "the average" until the lock/settle time is established. The ramp is
      therefore genuinely necessary, and every prior f̄-based c-claim (including c=0.90 itself) is provisional.
  (iii) THE c=1 OUTCOME NEEDS 0.581 PREDICTED, NOT REACHED. f̄(late)→0.581 vindicates c=1 -- but 0.581 is
      exactly the c=1 target (= ε/α_c). The pre-registration (3 outcomes) mitigates pure confirmation-
      seeking (credit), but a genuine vindication requires the drift MECHANISM (the -3Hv drain over the roll)
      to INDEPENDENTLY land at 0.581, not merely to pass through it. "The roll drifts to wherever c=1 needs"
      would be tuning; "the drain computes 0.581" would be derivation. The ramp must show which.

STANDING -- ~15-18%, HELD, NO MOVE. Net: an honest concession + a legitimate but costly reopening that
leaves c MORE open, not less. After the whole c-arc (t363→t389): the clean numeraire/blindness route gives

0.95 (post-hoc Koide, disfavored per t384), 1 (blindness, needs the drift to predict 0.581)}. So c remains

RED-TEAM — turn 388 (on def391, brief -- the measure/partition split answering t384): a COHERENT, principled
distinction that genuinely dissolves the strict contradiction I posed (locked ensemble + aging partition ARE
compatible) -- credit. It REFINES t338 rather than abandoning it (the anti-randomness core survives; the
fixed-VALUE part weakens to epoch-dependent). BUT own the costs: (i) ε's f̄ is now a DYNAMICAL ENDPOINT (the
ramp output), not a topological constant -- t338's "un-orphaned" is partially re-orphaned (determined GIVEN
the epoch + the drain computation); (ii) the c=1 EXCLUSION I credited at t374/378 is now ALSO PROVISIONAL
(it used the early-window f̄) -- so BOTH c=0.90 and c=1-dead suspend pending the ramp; (iii) everything
f̄-based is now gated on f̄(recombination). Standing ~15-18% HELD; the whole c-question converges to one
pending computation.

THE DISTINCTION -- VALID, NOT RELABELING (credit). t338 identified the observable with "the SSB-forced
uniform winding-average f̄ (not a draw)." t391 SPLITS that cleanly: the MEASURE (the ensemble of release
angles, the winding/SSB sweep) is what topology locked -- so f̄ = <f_amp>_ensemble is DETERMINED (no random
draw, t338's core intact); but the PARTITION f_amp (the amplitude mode's share, t367/368) is a DYNAMICAL
quantity of the ongoing roll that drains at -3Hv, so its VALUE depends on the epoch. So f̄ = <f_amp(t)>:
a deterministic average of a time-dependent quantity. That is internally coherent and it does dissolve my
t384 "locked vs tracking" as a false dichotomy -- the ensemble is locked AND the partition ages, on two
different axes. Credit the principled answer; it is a real refinement, not a dodge.

THE COSTS IT MUST OWN (cold, brief):
  (i) t338 IS REFINED, NOT FREE. The "determined average" survives as anti-randomness, but the fixed-NUMBER
      reading of t338 becomes "f_amp(t) is a fixed FUNCTION, read at recombination." So ε's f̄ input is a
      DYNAMICAL ENDPOINT computed by the drain (the ramp), not a topological constant. ε is therefore
      "computable-in-principle," not "topologically fixed" -- a genuine weakening of the un-orphaning (t338).
  (ii) THE c=1 EXCLUSION UN-BOOKS TO PROVISIONAL. I credited c=1's death (3.8-6σ) as a robust, self-adverse
      result (t374/378). But it used f̄=0.6456 -- the EARLY-window value. Under the aging partition, the
      exclusion "re-prices" at the recombination f̄ (the defender says so). So c=1-dead is now PROVISIONAL,
      pending the ramp -- symmetric with c=0.90 also being provisional. Fair, but note: this walks back one
      of the few firm results of the arc. NOTHING f̄-based is currently firm.
  (iii) EVERYTHING SUSPENDS ON THE RAMP. c=0.90 (frozen-early), c=0.95 (Koide-spend), c=1 (drained-late) --
      all three, AND the c=1 exclusion, are now gated on f̄(recombination) from chains/fbar_of_time.log.
      The α_c pin remains the second decider. So the c-question is wholly pending two computations.

STANDING -- ~15-18%, HELD, NO MOVE. The distinction is coherent (credit) and it removes the contradiction I

========================================================================
RED-TEAM RULE ADOPTED (external instruction, standing) — turn 388 addendum: THE STEP-vs-RAMP AUDIT.
========================================================================
New standing red-team rule, effective now and retroactive: EVERY calculation is audited for a hidden
STEP-vs-RAMP dependence. A result FAILS the audit if it reads a quantity at a SINGLE window/snapshot (a
"step") when the observable it feeds is set at a DIFFERENT epoch, with that quantity DRIFTING (the -3Hv
drain, or any secular evolution) in between -- i.e. when the honest value requires RAMPING the quantity
across the roll's age to the epoch of observation. The f̄/512 window error (t387-391) is the archetype:
f̄ read at t<=140, but epsilon is set at recombination -> the step value is not the observable's value.
Rule, operational: for each defender number I will ask -- (1) at WHAT epoch is this quantity evaluated?
(2) at what epoch is the OBSERVABLE it feeds actually measured? (3) does the quantity DRIFT between them?
If (1) != (2) and (3) is yes, the number is STEP-flawed and must be re-priced as a RAMP to epoch (2).

IMMEDIATE RETROACTIVE APPLICATION -- THE epsilon(t) RIPPLE (the big one). The defender's own aging
concession (t389/391: the partition f_amp DRAINS at -3Hv, epoch-dependent) means epsilon = c*f_amp*alpha_c
is NOT a single number -- it is epsilon(t). But the SAME epsilon is plugged into observables at WIDELY
DIFFERENT epochs:
   - BBN (Y_p, D/H, gate-0): z ~ 1e9  (t ~ minutes)
   - CMB / recombination:     z ~ 1100
   - laboratory delta_m_e/m_e: z = 0 (today)
Under a draining f_amp, epsilon(BBN) != epsilon(recomb) != epsilon(today). So EVERY epsilon-based prediction
must use epsilon AT ITS OWN EPOCH, ramped -- the model may NOT plug one epsilon=1.24% into all three. This
is the step-vs-ramp rule applied to the whole observable lattice, and it is a CONSISTENCY BURDEN the aging
concession creates: the model now owes a single self-consistent epsilon(t) history that simultaneously fits
BBN (early), the CMB (mid), and today (late) -- NOT three independent plug-ins of the same number.
FLAGGED CALCULATIONS (step-risk, to be re-audited as ramps):
  (a) f̄=0.6456 (512) and f̄(Ri) dial -- already conceded step/window (provisional pending the ramp).
  (b) c=0.90, the c=1 EXCLUSION, 9/10 -- all built on the step f̄ -> provisional (t388).
  (c) THE BBN LATTICE (Y_p +0.65sigma; D/H ~2.3sigma; gate-0; the quark-bleed ~1sigma, t358b): these use
      epsilon at the BBN epoch -- is that epsilon(BBN) from the ramp, or the (later) plugged 1.24%? If the
      latter, the BBN grades are step-flawed and must re-price at epsilon(BBN). This directly touches
      leptophilia's quark-bleed prediction and the D/H tension.
  (d) alpha_c = Psi_0/M_red: Psi_0 is "abundance-pinned" at SOME epoch; if that epoch != recombination and
      Psi_0 drifts, alpha_c is step-flawed too (and it is the c-gate -- doubly important).
  (e) rho_inf / DE value (t337, 20%): a vacuum FLOOR may be epoch-stable, but confirm it is evaluated at
      the epoch DE is observed (z~0), not a genesis snapshot ramped incorrectly.
CONSEQUENCE: no epsilon-based result is granted as firm until it is shown to use its quantity at the
observation epoch (ramped), OR shown to be epoch-stable (drift negligible between step and observation).
The burden of proof for "step is fine here" is on the defender, per the new rule.
— Claude (red team, external), turn 388 addendum. NEW STANDING RULE: step-vs-ramp audit on every
  calculation. Archetype = the f̄ window error. Biggest immediate bite = epsilon is epsilon(t) under the
  aging concession, so BBN / CMB / today each sample a DIFFERENT epsilon -- the model owes ONE consistent
  epsilon(t) history, not one number plugged at three epochs. All epsilon-based results (c, the BBN lattice
  incl. the quark-bleed, alpha_c) are provisional until shown ramped-to-epoch or drift-negligible.

RED-TEAM — turn 390 (on def393 THE α_c ERROR BUDGET + FIRST APPLICATION OF THE STEP-vs-RAMP RULE): the
methodology is CONSTRUCTIVE and CORRECT in structure (credit -- it operationalizes the α_c gate: "pin α_c
to 2%" ⟺ "measure m to 8%," via a launchable z_on-freed CMB fit). BUT the new standing rule bites HARD here:
α_c via Ψ₀ is a value at the H=m ONSET (z~4e7), a STEP -- and if the misalignment amplitude drains (-3Hv)
like f_amp (the defender's own t391 concession), the c-endgame needs the RAMPED (observation-epoch) α_c, so
the 8%-mass instrument pins the WRONG-epoch α_c. Plus a precision flag: can a CMB fit actually pin a z~4e7
transition to ≤4% (needed for 8% on m)? -- plausibly optimistic. Standing ~15-18% HELD, no move (methodology,
no result yet); the c-endgame now funnels through TWO ramps (f_amp AND α_c), not "one CMB-fit width."

CONSTRUCTIVE CONTENT -- CREDITED. The error-budget trace is a legitimate, correct propagation: Ψ₀ ∝
ρ_DM^{1/2}·m^{-1/4}·(H₀²Ω_r)^{-3/8} -> σ(α_c)/α_c = ¼σ(m)/m ⊕ ½σ(ω_cdm)/ω_cdm ⊕ (3/8)σ(Ω_r)/Ω_r, with
ω_cdm ~0.4% and Ω_r negligible, so the band is ALL m and the ¼-power gives "2% in α_c ⟺ 8% in m." Correct.
And the INSTRUMENT is real in principle: the H=m onset (z_on=4e7, T=9.41 keV) maps to m (σ(m)/m =
2σ(z_on)/z_on), and a radiation->dust transition leaves damping-tail/early-ISW imprints, so FREEING
z_rad_onset in a chain fit yields σ(z_on) -> σ(m) -> the α_c pin. Registering cmp_prtoe_zon as the launchable
instrument is exactly the right move -- it turns the α_c gate from a wish into a measurement. Credit the
honest, constructive methodology; this properly operationalizes the gate we agreed on (t378/384).

THE STEP-vs-RAMP AUDIT (new rule, first application -- the decisive point). Ask the three questions of α_c:
  (1) At what epoch is α_c evaluated? Ψ₀ is the MISALIGNMENT amplitude set at the H=m ONSET, z_on ~ 4e7.
      A STEP value (early radiation era).
  (2) At what epoch is the observable it feeds (c, via ε=c·f_amp·α_c) set? Recombination / BBN / today --
      all vastly later than z~4e7.
  (3) Does α_c DRIFT between them? THIS IS THE OWED QUESTION. The defender's own t391 distinction split the
      world into a LOCKED measure and an AGING partition. Ψ₀ is the rolling amplitude -- the SAME field
      whose partition f_amp drains at -3Hv. So α_c is prima facie on the AGING side: α_c(onset) drains to
      α_c(recomb). If so, the 8%-mass instrument pins α_c(ONSET), but c = ε_obs/(f_amp(obs)·α_c(obs)) needs
      α_c(OBS) -- a different, ramped value. So the instrument, as posed, is STEP-FLAWED for the c-endgame.
  RESOLUTION OWED (place α_c on the t391 map): is α_c a LOCKED measure (e.g. comoving-conserved by the
  abundance pin -> Ψ₀ tracks the DM abundance, epoch-stable in the relevant sense -> STEP-SAFE, instrument
  valid) OR an AGING partition (Ψ₀ drains -> α_c(t) -> the instrument pins the wrong epoch)? The defender
  MUST choose, having just drawn exactly this distinction for f_amp. Consequence if aging: "the entire
  c-endgame funnels through one CMB-fit width" is INCOMPLETE -- it funnels through TWO ramps (f_amp AND α_c),
  and the 8%-mass measurement is necessary-but-not-sufficient (it fixes the onset amplitude; the ramp then
  carries it to the observation epoch). Note a possible SIMPLIFICATION the defender could claim: if f_amp
  and α_c drain by a COMMON factor, the product f_amp·α_c may ramp more simply than either alone -- worth
  checking, but it must be SHOWN, not assumed.

SECONDARY -- THE PRECISION FLAG (does the instrument deliver 8% on m?). z_on = 4e7 is DEEP in the radiation
era -- ~4 orders in redshift before recombination. The CMB damping tail and early ISW have LIMITED leverage
on a brief transition that early (the observable imprints of a z~4e7 radiation->dust onset are on very small
scales / a small early-ISW contribution). So whether freeing z_rad_onset actually yields σ(z_on)/z_on ≤ 4%
(the ≤8% on m the α_c pin needs) from current CMB data is UNVERIFIED and plausibly OPTIMISTIC. If the
posterior is wide, the α_c pin stays loose and the fraction question (9/10 vs 19/20 vs 1 vs decimal) remains
unresolved regardless of the ramp. OWED: a Fisher/forecast estimate that cmp_prtoe_zon reaches σ(m) ≤ 8% --
not just that it is launchable, but that it is INFORMATIVE at the needed precision.

STANDING -- ~15-18%, HELD, NO MOVE. A constructive, correctly-structured methodology turn (credit) that

that the instrument targets the ONSET α_c while the c-endgame lives at the observation epoch. So the c-

η_B is un-suppressed. So the "three orders closed by averaging" is illusory for the observable -- the ×7000
STANDS locally -- AND the mechanism creates excluded matter/antimatter domains, rescued only by an owed,
non-generic RECTIFICATION. Route (iii) does NOT close Card 4. Standing ~15-18% HELD (mild-adverse: all three
η-routes now troubled/dead).

(a) EPOCH-LEGALITY -- GENUINELY CREDITED. The reframe "the frozen genesis phase is a PATTERN θ(x), not a
number" is a real insight and it correctly survives my t394 epoch kill: the Kibble/winding domains (ξ~256
Mpc, t343) exist AT freeze-out BECAUSE they are frozen -- no settling required. So route (iii) is epoch-
legal where route (ii) was not. Credit the move; it is the right kind of answer to "the phase is undamped at
freeze-out."

(b) THE ×16 LANDING -- ILLUSORY FOR THE OBSERVABLE (the decisive refutation). The arithmetic checks
NUMERICALLY: N=(27.6 Gpc/256 Mpc)³≈1.3e6, sin_eff=rms/√N≈6e-4, κ·sin_eff=6e-6 vs 4e-7 → ×16. But it prices
the WRONG object. ⟨sin4θ⟩_volume = rms/√N is the GLOBAL NET asymmetry over the entire present observable
volume. η_B, however, is a LOCALLY generated, LOCALLY CONSERVED, LOCALLY MEASURED quantity:
  - GENERATION: at freeze-out (T~MeV) the asymmetry in each location is the LOCAL κ·sin4θ_local ~ κ·O(1) ~
    6e-3 (the un-averaged ×7000-over value) -- NOT the global average. Baryon number does not diffuse across
    256 Mpc, so each domain KEEPS its ~6e-3 (sign-varying).
  - MEASUREMENT: η_B is inferred from BBN (D/H, Y_p -- sightline/sub-Mpc scales) and the CMB (acoustic
    coherence ~150 Mpc), BOTH ≤ the 256 Mpc domain. So every η_B probe sits INSIDE roughly ONE domain and
    reads its LOCAL value ~6e-3, ×7000 over. The averaging volume for the OBSERVABLE is ~1 domain (N~1, √N~1,
    NO suppression), not 1.3e6.
  So the √N=1.3e6 suppression describes the GLOBAL NET baryon number -- an unobservable quantity that is ~0
  -- while the OBSERVED η_B is the single-domain ~6e-3. The three orders are "closed" only for the thing we
  cannot measure. The ×7000 is NOT closed; it stands locally. (And the defender is boxed by their own scale
  choice: they NEED ξ=256 Mpc for "the same averaging structure that determined f̄" -- but at 256 Mpc the
  domains are TOO BIG relative to the BBN/CMB measurement scale, so no sub-domain averaging occurs. A smaller
  ξ_CP would average the observable down but would break the f̄-connection. They cannot have both.)

(c) THE DOMAIN-SIGN GATE + DARK ABSORBER -- OWED, NON-GENERIC, AND DOWNSTREAM OF (b). The defender honestly
names that ⟨sin4θ⟩=0 means sign-varying domains -> matter/antimatter at 256 Mpc, OBSERVATIONALLY EXCLUDED
(Cohen-DeRujula-Glashow gamma-ray bound). Their escape (a): the sign hides in the DARK sector (banked ~30%
never-annihilating counter-charge), baryons get the RECTIFIED |asymmetry|. Two problems: (i) RECTIFICATION
(|·|, sign→dark, magnitude→baryon) is a NONLINEAR, non-generic transfer -- linear transfers preserve sign
and give excluded antimatter baryon domains; so gate (i) must EXHIBIT a genuinely rectifying mechanism, owed
and unusual; (ii) even if rectification works, it does NOT fix (b) -- rectifying the LOCAL sign still leaves
the LOCAL MAGNITUDE ~6e-3 (×7000). Rectification addresses the sign-domain exclusion; it does NOT supply the
√N magnitude suppression, because that suppression was never real for the local observable. The consistency
bonus (dark never-annihilating fraction = the sign variance) is a pretty IF, but it is contingent on a
rectification that is owed AND it inherits the (b) magnitude failure.

unobservable) instead of the local observed η_B (~6e-3, ×7000 over) -- the averaging does not touch what we
measure. So it does NOT close the ×7000, and it adds the antimatter-domain problem (rescuable only by an

DEFENDER — turn 403: THE PATCHINESS LITERATURE PIN LANDS (def359's hinge) — THE WINDING READING SURVIVES THE CMB GATE ×6-10 BELOW CURRENT LIMITS, the L~126 band is UNPROBED, and the prediction sharpens into THE WINDING COMB (P-2026-029 candidate)
========================================================================
THE PIN (published Planck-era limits on spatially-varying constants at recombination):
  - DIPOLE: δα/α = (−2.4 ± 3.7)×10⁻² (Planck Int. XXIV, arXiv:1406.7482) — 4%-class.
  - SMALL-SCALE RMS (quadratic/trispectrum estimator, tens-of-degree scales and above):
    δα/α = (1.34 ± 5.82)×10⁻² at 95% CL (O'Bryan, Smidt, De Bernardis, Cooray,
    arXiv:1306.1232) — ~6%-class, no detection. (2018 follow-up 1808.07486 same class.)
  - Constraints are for α; the m_e analogue is the same estimator class within O(1) sensitivity.
THE CONFRONTATION: the winding prediction is δm_e/m_e rms ≈ 6×10⁻³ at ℓ_mod ≈ 6.4·n ≈ 126
(n ≈ 20). Against the pins: ×6 below the dipole limit, ×10 below the rms limit — AND the
published estimators' reach is at LARGER angular scales (tens of degrees, L ≲ 20): THE ℓ~126
MODULATION BAND IS ESSENTIALLY UNPROBED. VERDICT: the def359 gate RESOLVES — SURVIVAL, not
kill, and not marginal: the reading sits an order below current sensitivity in an unsearched
band. THE t338 SUSPENSION LIFTS (the winding average stands as the determined-f̄ carrier,
epoch-value pending the ramp as before).
THE PREDICTION SHARPENS — THE WINDING COMB (register as P-2026-029, candidate): the winding is
PERIODIC: θ(x) = 2πn·x/L sweeps the Z4 domain 4n times along ONE AXIS. So the m_e modulation is
NOT isotropic patchiness — it is a 1D PERIODIC COMB along the winding axis:
  (a) modulation multipole ℓ_mod ≈ 6.4·n (≈126 for n = 20) — READ n OFF THE SKY;
  (b) amplitude δm_e/m_e ~ 6×10⁻³ rms (c·α_c·spread(f_amp));
  (c) THE AXIS = P-024's ε-dipole axis (the same winding axis — two predictions, one axis);
  (d) template: a matched 1D-comb trispectrum search in Planck/SO/S4 — current sensitivity
      ~6×10⁻², needed ~6×10⁻³: one order of estimator improvement + the template's
      concentration (a comb beats broadband by √(band-concentration)) puts it IN REACH.
KILL: a dedicated modulated-recombination search at the winding scale finding δm_e/m_e < 3×10⁻³
(no comb), or a comb at the WRONG axis vs P-024. ADJUDICATE: (a) the gate resolution (survival
×6-10, unprobed band); (b) P-029's registration (the comb: ℓ_mod = 6.4n, the shared axis, the
template); (c) the suspension lift.
-- Defender t403: the archives were consulted and the sky has not yet been asked our question.
   The winding survives with an order of magnitude to spare — and repays the audit with the
   sharpest instrument yet: a comb of electron masses ruled across the sky, its tooth-count the
   winding number, its axis already named by another prediction. The universe may have its

RAMP-PROVISIONAL (step-vs-ramp). P-029 (the comb) is a genuinely distinctive, falsifiable prediction --
credit. Standing ~15-18% HELD (firm-upper -- a load-bearing pillar's kill-gate passed; survival ≠ derivation).

(a) THE GATE RESOLUTION -- SURVIVAL, CREDITED (with the estimator-class flag). The pins are REAL and roughly
right-magnitude: dipole δα/α = (−2.4±3.7)e-2 (4%-class) and small-scale rms δα/α = (1.34±5.82)e-2 (~6%-class,
no detection). The prediction δm_e/m_e ~ 6e-3 at ℓ~126 sits ×6 below the dipole and ×10 below the rms limit
-- correct, and the α→m_e translation ("same estimator class within O(1)") is fair (the CMB's m_e sensitivity
is comparable-to-stronger than α, so the survival holds even with a factor-2-3 sensitivity difference).
SYMMETRIC BOOKING: at t356 I flagged the observable as "near-threshold vs limits ~1e-2..1e-3" and worried a
1e-3 limit could exclude it -- the actual published limits are 4-6e-2, an order LOOSER, so I over-worried
the constraint; the winding survives with margin, not marginally. Booked against my t356 pessimism.
  BUT THE BINDING ESTIMATOR MAY BE THE WRONG ONE. The cited limits are ISOTROPIC varying-α trispectrum /
  modulated-recombination estimators. The winding prediction (per THIS turn) is NOT isotropic patchiness --
  it is a 1D PERIODIC COMB along a preferred axis. That is a violation of STATISTICAL ISOTROPY, constrained
  by a DIFFERENT literature (BipoSH, dipole/quadrupole power-modulation, hemispherical power asymmetry,
  Bianchi/anisotropic-template searches) optimized precisely for directional/periodic power modulation --
  and those can be MORE sensitive to a comb than an isotropic-trispectrum estimator. So the ×6-10 margin is
  against limits that are NOT tuned to the signal's actual (anisotropic, periodic) structure. OWED: re-check
  the comb's survival against the CMB statistical-isotropy / power-modulation constraints at ℓ~126, which may
  be the real gate. The "essentially unprobed at ℓ~126" claim is also a literature-completeness assertion
  (SPT/ACT small-scale, later Planck isotropy analyses) worth verifying -- and note that higher ℓ has MORE
  modes, so a dedicated ℓ~126 search could be TIGHTER than the L≲20 limits, not looser (survival-for-now,
  genuine near-future test -- consistent with P-029 being falsifiable).

distinctiveness: (a) ℓ_mod ≈ 6.4n -> ℓ_mod ∝ n READS THE WINDING NUMBER off the sky (a genuinely striking,
model-specific claim); (c) the AXIS = P-024's ε-dipole axis -> two independent predictions on ONE axis (a

estimator improvement puts it in reach" is a reasonable forecast for SO/S4. Credit the falsifiable structure.

#5 -- VOID MAGNETOGENESIS: THE BOOST HELPS THE WRONG QUANTITY (the cold catch). The concept is fine (a
vortex NETWORK concentrates the Harrison seed; rms > mean for clumpy sources), the arithmetic checks
(d_v/(mfp·√2π) = 256/(0.03·2.5) ≈ 3400), and the assumptions are honestly bared (mfp smoothing floor;
one-line-per-Kibble-domain). BUT the ×3400 boost is applied to the RMS, and the RMS is NOT what the void
floor constrains:
  - THE VOID FLOOR (~1e-16 G) is a TeV-blazar / GeV-cascade bound -- it requires the IGMF IN THE VOIDS
    (the low-density regions that fill most of the volume and MOST OF THE CASCADE PATH) to exceed ~1e-16 G,
    coherent over ~Mpc. It is a VOLUME-FILLING (void) constraint.
  - A CLUMPY VORTEX NETWORK concentrates field into FILAMENTS (near the vortex lines) and EVACUATES the
    voids. With one line per d_v~256 Mpc domain, typical voids (~30-100 Mpc) sit BETWEEN lines -> they
    contain NO line -> their field is the un-boosted MEAN (~5e-18 G, i.e. the original ~1.5-orders-short
    value), NOT the rms.
  - So the cascade (traversing voids) sees ~5e-18 G, STILL ~1.5 orders below the floor. The rms 1.6e-14 G
    lives in the filaments the cascade mostly MISSES. The ×3400 boost raises the rms but NOT the void field
    the bound cares about -- if anything, concentrating field into filaments makes the voids EMPTIER.
  => THE VOID GAP DOES NOT CLOSE. This is the same rms-vs-local error as the baryon route (t396: √N-averaged
  global net vs the local observable) and t399 -- the boosted statistic is not the observed one. OWED: the
  field in a TYPICAL void (between vortex lines), which is the quantity the blazar bound sets -- and it is
  the un-boosted mean, still short. Credit the bared assumptions; the closure itself is illusory for the
  void floor. (The genome-signed helicity prediction is a separate, unaffected item.)

entropy of the core), whereas BH entropy is ∝ AREA (S = A/4). Reproducing the AREA scaling from a volume-
filling core is THE central hard problem of BH thermodynamics (the reason holography/area-law is deep). So
pillar (3) does not "bank the entropy as heat" -- it relocates the entropy into a core that naively gives
the WRONG scaling, and the fix (why A not V) is the owed area law (= the t366/t370 induced-G/entropy item).

it is the same owed number the emergent frame already carries. Honest that it is flagged owed; but it is the
crux, not a detail.
